condition.name: Bleeding
entries: If you have the Bleeding condition, you cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} except by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The condition ends when a creature finishes a {@variantrule Alternative Rest System|GrimHollowCG24|Quick}, {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}

condition.name: Corruption
entries: While you have the {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24} condition, you experience the following effects.

entries.name: Heroic Corruption
entries: You cannot have {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} or give it to another player character who lacks it.

entries.name: Corruption Levels
entries: This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24} level. If your {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24} level is 6, you either die or transform into a creature of the GM's choosing, under their control.

entries.name: Removing Corruption Levels
entries: The {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB}, {@spell Heal|XPHB}, or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell removes all of your {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24} levels and ends the condition.

condition.name: Dazed
entries: If you have the Dazed condition, you cannot maintain {@condition Concentration|XPHB}.

table.name: Lost Memories
table.caption: Lost Memories
outro: A {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell reverses this effect and restores the victim’s memory.
rows: The target becomes deeply suspicious of a character or NPC of the GM’s choice. They gain the following flaw: “I know they are seeking to betray me. I have to act first—it’s me or them!”
The target forgets all memories of a loved one of the GM’s choice. The next time the target sees them, it’s as if they are meeting them for the first time.
The target becomes stricken with doubt and gains the following flaw: “The world is out to get me, and I can’t win. I may as well give up now.”
The target forgets how to speak in one language known, decided by the GM.
The target forgets the location of something important to them, of the GM’s choice. It may be the location of a goal, their home settlement, or a hidden treasure.
The target loses an inconsequential memory such as what they ate a few nights ago or the name of an unimportant NPC.

table.name: Grievous Wounds
table.caption: Grievous Wounds

rows.name: Permanent Wound
entries: You sustain a grisly wound that may haunt you forever. Roll 1d10 on the {@table Permanent Wounds|GrimHollowCG24} table and apply the result.

rows.name: Festering Wound
entries: Your injury becomes infected, causing a fever. You have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws. A creature cannot ignore this instance of the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition through {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

rows.name: Internal Bleeding{@sup *}
entries: You suffer an internal hemorrhage and bruising. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by three times your character level.

rows.name: Wracked With Pain
entries: Your injury causes intense pain that limits your agility. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

rows.name: Concussion
entries: A brain contusion leaves you struggling to focus. You gain the {@condition Dazed|GrimHollowCG24} condition.

rows.name: Laceration
entries: lood wells unabaited from a deep injury. You gain the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowCG24} condition.

rows.name: Deep Wound{@sup *}
entries: You endure a blow that would kill many, but merely staggers you. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you start with one failed {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}.

rows.name: Battered{@sup *}
entries: Your muscles ache and you have had the wind knocked out of you. You gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

rows.name: Injured Wrist{@sup *}
entries: You have badly sprained your wrist or fractured a bone in your arm and are unable to use it effectively. You are unable to hold objects in one of your hands and you cannot take actions that require that arm.

rows.name: Damaged Eardrum
entries: A blow to your head impairs your hearing. You have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.

rows.name: System Shock
entries: You suffer a severe nervous shock. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.

rows.name: Injured Leg{@sup *}
entries: An injury to your leg or foot slows your movement. Reduce your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 10 Feet, to a minimum of 5 Feet. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} can't be increased, and you can't take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action.

rows.name: Torn Muscle{@sup *}
entries: This injury reduces your combat prowess. When you deal damage with a Weapon Attack or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, roll {@dice 1d4;1d6;1d8;1d10;1d12|1d4} and subtract the result from the damage dealt. Each additional time you receive this wound, increase the size of the die rolled (to 1d6, 1d8, 1d10, and 1d12). After reaching the maximum die size listed, replace further Torn Muscle results with Injured Leg.

rows.name: Stymied Recovery
entries: Your injury slows your overall recovery. The total number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} you can spend during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Alternative Rest System|GrimHollowCG24|Quick Rest} is equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).

rows.name: Severe Swelling
entries: Your swollen injuries give you an uncouth appearance. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks.

rows.name: Close Call{@sup *}
entries: You narrowly miss an otherwise dangerous blow. Instead of being reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you have 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

footnotes: Results with a {@sup *} next to them can be taken more than once

table.name: Permanent Wounds
table.caption: Permanent Wounds

rows.name: Fatal Wound
entries: Through an unrecoverable injury, you have been fatally wounded. You are now dead but can be brought back through normal methods.

rows.name: Lost Arm{@sup *}
entries: A serious injury disables or destroys one of your arms. You can't use that arm to wield a weapon or a shield, or to perform the Somatic component of a spell.

rows.name: Lost Leg{@sup *}
entries: A serious injury disables or destroys one of your legs. Reduce your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 15 Feet, to a minimum of 5 Feet. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} can't be increased, and you can't take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action. If you receive this result again your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by another 15 Feet (to a minimum of 5 Feet).

rows.name: Lost Eye{@sup *}
entries: You lose an eye. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) and Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight. If you receive this result again, you gain the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition.

rows.name: Dizzy Spells
entries: Undertaking vigorous activities leaves you addled. On odd numbered combat rounds, if you take an action, you cannot take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} until the start of your next turn. If you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you cannot take an action until the start of your next turn.

rows.name: Scarred Lungs
entries: Your injury makes it a struggle to breathe. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} maximum is permanently reduced by 1 for each of your character levels. Additionally, when you gain a level, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by 1.

rows.name: Hideous Scar
entries: You are horribly and visibly scarred.You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}) checks.

rows.name: Adrenaline Rush
entries: Your injuries make you vulnerable to further damage, but your survival instincts are heightened in moments of danger. If you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, any {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against you becomes a normal hit. If you are not {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, attack rolls made against you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

rows.name: Hesitant
entries: Your psyche has been scarred more than your body. The first time after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} that you have to make a saving throw to avoid the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you are {@condition Frightened|XPHB} even if you succeed on the saving throw.

rows.name: Old Pain
entries: This lingering injury causes you intense pain when it flares up. When you roll a natural 1 on any {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

footnotes: Results with a {@sup *} next to them can be taken more than once

table.name: Great Beast Mutations
table.caption: Great Beast Mutations
rows: Roll twice on the table, ignoring this result if rolled again.

rows.name: Spawning Pit
entries: Your skin begins to crawl moments before vermin chew through your flesh and erupt from your body. {@dice 1d3} {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} {@creature Swarm of Crawling Claws|XMM|Swarms of Crawling Claws}, {@creature Swarm of Insects|XMM|Insects}, or {@creature Swarm of Rats|XMM|Rats} appear in unoccupied spaces within 10 Feet of you. This also happens the first time you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} check.

rows.name: Bloodless
entries: You cannot spend {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} for any reason, including as the result of a spell or other {@variantrule Magical Effect|XPHB}.

rows.name: Corporeal Decay
entries: Your body begins to rapidly decay, giving you a corpselike appearance. When you gain this mutation, roll a number of {@dice 1d8;2d8;3d8;4d8;5d8;6d8;7d8;8d8;9d8;10d8;11d8;12d8;13d8;14d8;15d8;16d8;17d8;18d8;19d8;20d8;|d8s} equal to your character level and take Necrotic damage equal to the result. You have the Undead type in addition to other creature types.

rows.name: Lignify
entries: Your limbs stiffen and woody tree shoots pierce from beneath your skin. You have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage and your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} (all movement types) is reduced by 5 Feet per your level of {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24}.

rows.name: Scion of the Beast
entries: You carry some of the Great Beast’s corruptive nature. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn, you and another creature of your choice you can see within 60 Feet of you each make a {@adventure Mutation check|GrimHollowCG24|20|Great Beast Mutations}.

rows.name: Third Eye
entries: You grow a third eye. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or remove the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, but you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks.

rows.name: Extra Digits
entries: Your grow several extra fingers. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to avoid being disarmed, but you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) checks.

rows.name: Chameleon
entries: Your skin changes color in stressful situations. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks, but {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks.

rows.name: Oily Skin
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks made to escape a {@condition Grappled|XPHB|Grapple}, but you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Cold and Fire damage.

rows.name: Corrosive Blood
entries: When you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, creatures within 5 Feet of you take Acid damage equal to your character level.

rows.name: Sticky Feet
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} of 15 Feet, but your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 Feet.

rows.name: Luminescent
entries: You glow with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} to a range of 30 Feet. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

rows.name: Thinning Blood
entries: Your bodily fluids become thin and watery. You have the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowCG24} condition.

rows.name: Stone Shell
entries: Your skin swells and hardens into a tumorous rocky texture. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Piercing and Slashing damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Acid and Thunder damage.

rows.name: Arcane Conduit
entries: Your body becomes a conduit of magic. Add your current {@condition Corruption|GrimHollowCG24} level to your spell save DC and your spell attack rolls. When you cast a spell using a spell slot, make a {@adventure Mutation check|GrimHollowCG24|20|Great Beast Mutations}.

rows.name: Malformed Wings
entries: Spiny, tattered wings burst painfully from your back, giving you a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 20 Feet. You cannot move by any other means.

rows.name: Blood-Fueled Frenzy
entries: If you are Bloodied, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on melee attack rolls, and attack rolls against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. On each of your turns, you must first move as far as possible toward the nearest {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature you can see. If you are within 5 Feet of it after moving, you must take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to attack that creature with a melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

rows.name: Eldritch Combustion
entries: Your body fills with unStable eldritch power. If you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you die instantly, and each creature within 20 Feet of you must make a {@adventure Mutation check|GrimHollowCG24|20|Great Beast Mutations}.

rows.name: Permanent Transformation
entries: You gain the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}. If you already have this or another Transformation, you move to the next stage of that Transformation instead.

monster.name: Avatar of Slaughter
languages: All languages known before the curse

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The Avatar of Slaughter has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Imprison in Weapon
entries: When the Avatar of Slaughter is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by a melee Weapon Attack by a creature within 5 Feet, its spirit infests the weapon that struck the blow. The weapon gains the properties and Curse associated with a {@item Berserker Axe|XDMG}, though it retains its original weapon type. Additionally, while the curse remains the weapon is sentient and can telepathically communicate with any creature that wields it, encouraging violence and malice.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Avatar of Slaughter has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If the Avatar of Slaughter is destroyed, and not imprisoned in a weapon, it reforms and regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in {@dice 1d6} days unless a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell is cast on its remains.

trait.name: Silver Weakness
entries: Damage dealt by Silver weapons ignores the avatar's {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The Avatar of Slaughter's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 60 Feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 30 Feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Avatar of Slaughter makes four Fist attacks. It can replace one attack with a Gore attack.

action.name: Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d8 + 8}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 4d8 + 8}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the Avatar of Slaughter moved at least 20 Feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Piercing damage and has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of the target's next turn.

monster.name: Bloated Gastromorph
languages: All languages known before the curse

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The Bloated Gastromorph regains 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of its turns. If the Bloated Gastromorph takes Acid or Fire damage, this trait doesn’t function on the Bloated Gastromorph’s next turn. The Bloated Gastromorph dies only if it starts its turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn’t regenerate.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Bloated Gastromorph can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Bloated Gastromorph makes three Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a Tongue attack.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Slashing damage, and the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) and is moved up to 5 Feet toward the Bloated Gastromorph.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 4d10 + 5}) Piercing damage.

If the Bloated Gastromorph takes 40 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, it regurgitates all swallowed creatures at the end of that turn, each of which falls in a space within 10 Feet of the gastromorph and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. If the Bloated Gastromorph dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and can escape from the corpse using 15 Feet of movement, exiting with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Tongue {@recharge 5}
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 25 ft., one target. {@h}31 ({@damage 4d12 + 5}) Acid damage. A creature that is hit by this attack is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition and is moved up to 20 Feet toward the gastromorph. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

bonus.name: Swallow
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 17}, one Large or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the gastromorph (it can have up to two creatures swallowed at a time). {@actSaveFail} The target is swallowed by the gastromorph, and the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition ends. A swallowed creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the gastromorph, and taks 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Acid damage at the start of each of the gastromorph's turns.

monster.name: Body Snatcher
languages: all languages known before the curse
and all languages known by its host

trait.name: Electric Invigoration
entries: Whenever a Body Snatcher would take Lightning damage, it absorbs the energy and instead gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage, rounded down. The Body Snatcher also immediately recharges its Electric Torrent ability.

trait.name: Skin Sack
entries: The Body Snatcher is physically indistinguishable from its host and cannot be detected by nonmagical means. If the Body Snatcher is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and it has been inside its current host for less than 24 hours, the host regurgitates it then has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition and 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Body Snatcher makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Invade
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 15}; one Humanoid creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Body Snatcher. {@actSaveFail} The Body Snatcher departs its current host and moves into the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature. It then regains all {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and recharges Electric Torrent. If it had been inside its previous host for less than 24 hours, that creature has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition and 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. Otherwise, the previous host body becomes a lifeless skin sack.

action.name: Electric Torrent {@recharge 5}
entries: The Body Snatcher unleashes a large electric pulse. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, each other creature within 20 Feet of the Body Snatcher. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Lightning damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

monster.name: Dream Whisperer
languages: all languages known before the curse

trait.name: Aura of Confusion
entries: A creature that starts its turn or moves within 25 Feet of a Dream Whisperer must succeed on a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw or be affected as if they had failed a save against the {@spell Confusion|XPHB} spell.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Dream Whisperer has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Dream Whisperer can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Dream Whisperer makes three Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Slashing damage, and the target is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave int} {@dc 13}. {@actSaveFail} The Dream Whisperer gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the target for 1 minute, and the target rolls on the Lost Memories table.

entries.caption: Lost Memories
outro: A {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell reverses this effect and restores the victim’s memory.
rows: The target becomes deeply suspicious of a character or NPC of the GM’s choice. They gain the following flaw: "I know they are seeking to betray me. I have to act first—it’s me or them!"
The target forgets all memories of a loved one of the GM’s choice. The next time the target sees them, it’s as if they are meeting them for the first time.
The target becomes stricken with doubt and gains the following flaw: "“The world is out to get me, and I can’t win. I may as well give up now."
The target forgets how to speak in one language known, decided by the GM.
The target forgets the location of something important to them, of the GM’s choice. It may be the location of a goal, their home settlement, or a hidden treasure.
The target loses an inconsequential memory such as what they ate a few nights ago or the name of an unimportant NPC.

monster.name: Herald of Calamity
languages: all languages known before the curse

trait.name: Aura of Calamity
entries: An aura of misfortune surrounds the herald in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Creatures other than the herald within the area have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@dice d20} Tests. Whenever a creature in the area other than the herald takes damage from any source other than another creature (such as a trap, a hazard, or a fall), it takes the maximum possible damage.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The Herald of Calamity can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Invisibility
entries: The Herald of Calamity has the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition unless it is in an area illuminated by moonlight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Herald of Calamity makes two Life Drain attacks.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 4d8 + 3}) Necrotic damage, and the Herald of Calamity regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the amount of Necrotic damage dealt.

reaction.name: Mishap
entries: {@actTrigger} An attack roll against the Herald of Calamity hits it. {@actResponse} The herald gains a +6 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} against the triggering attack. If this causes the attack to miss, it is redirected to a creature of the Herald's choice within 5 Feet of it. Use the original attack roll to determine the success of the redirected attack.

trait.name: Displacement
entries: Attack rolls against the Mind Siphon have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, since it projects an illusion that makes it appear near its actual location. This trait is suppressed while the Mind Siphon has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Mind Siphon has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Mind Siphon can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Mind Siphon makes two Tentacle Tongue attacks at different targets.

action.name: Tentacle Tongue
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 5d8 + 4}) Psychic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 16}) from the Mind Siphon’s Tentacle Tongue, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the Grapple ends. The Mind Siphon can Grapple up to two creatures at the same time.

action.name: Consume Head {@recharge 6}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by Tentacle Tongue. {@actSaveFail} 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Piercing damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} If this damage reduces the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the Mind Siphon kills it and devours its head.

action.name: Synaptic Eruption {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 16}, each creature within 30 Feet of the Mind Siphon. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of the Mind Siphon’s next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

monster.name: Plague Carrion
languages: all languages known before the curse

trait.name: Cloud of Blight
entries: A miasma surrounds the Plague Carrion in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Each time another creature starts its turn or enters the area, it gains a -1 penalty to damage rolls. Multiple instances of this penalty stack, but a creature can't gain it more than once each round. The penalty is removed when the creature finishes a {@variantrule Alternative Resting System|GrimHollowCG24|Quick}, {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the Plague Carrion dies, it explodes in a cloud of sickening gas. {@actSave con} {@dc 17}, each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on the Plague Carrion. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Poison damage, and the creature’s {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to half the Poison damage taken (rounding down). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.
The {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum reduction lasts until the creature finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Plague Carrion makes one Bite attack and one Plague Touch attack, or two Plague Beam attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 4d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Plague Touch
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}49 ({@damage 10d8 + 4}) Poison damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} The target’s {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to half the Poison damage taken (rounding down).

action.name: Plague Beam
entries: {@atk r} {@hit 8} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) Poison damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of the Plague Carrion’s next turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Plague Carrion casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material component and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 16}, {@hit 8} to hit with spell attacks):

monster.name: Sightless Agony
languages: all languages known before the curse

trait.name: Shared Agony
entries: When a Sightless Agony takes damage, it emits a wave of energy, causing each creature within 5 Feet of it to take an amount of Psychic damage equal to half the triggering damage (rounding down).

trait.name: Stunted Flight
entries: When a Sightless Agony chooses to fly, it must land by the end of its turn or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Sightless Agony makes one Bite attack, two Claw attacks, and one Tail attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Psychic damage. If both Claw attacks hit the same creature, the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 17}). Checks made to escape the grapple have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Psychic damage, and the target is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave str} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

reaction.name: Wave of Agony (1/Day)
entries: {@actTrigger} The Sightless Agony becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB}. {@actResponse} {@actSave con} {@dc 17}, each creature within a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. {@actSaveFail} 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Psychic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

monster.name: Verminous Abomination
languages: all languages known before the curse

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The Verminous Abomination can burrow through solid rock at half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and leaves a 10-foot-wide, 10-foot-high tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Verminous Abomination makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Piercing damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Birth Ratlings {@recharge 5}
entries: The Verminous Abomination spawns {@dice 1d4+2} {@creature Swarm of Rats|XMM} within 5 Feet of it. The swarms may immediately move and use actions. On later turns, they move and take actions on the same {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} as the Verminous Abomination.

trait.name: Alluring Cry
entries: The cry of the Weeping Willow attracts creatures in a 120-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}from the willow. At the start of each of its turns, a creature within the area must succeed on a {@dc 18} Charisma save or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. On a success, the target is immune to this Weeping Willow's Alluring Cry for 24 hours.

While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the target also has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. On each of its turns, a {@condition Charmed|XPHB} creature must use its movement to approach the Weeping Willow by the most direct route, stopping when it is within 5 Feet of the Weeping Willow. It doesn't avoid {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, but if this movement would obviously cause damage (such as by walking through fire), the creature repeats the save before entering that space.

trait.name: Deep Rooted
entries: The Weeping Willow is immune to effects that would move it.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Weeping Willow has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Weeping Willow makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk m} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 4d10 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Lignify
entries: {@actSave con}, one creature within 5 Feet: {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, ending the effect on itself on a success. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Petrified|XPHB} condition instead of the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, becoming a tree permanently. {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} or similar magic returns an affected creature to their original form.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Weeping Willow casts one of the following spells without the need for components, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

trait.name: Silver Tongue
entries: The Trade Factor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Deception|XPHB}, {@skill Insight|XPHB}, and {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} checks when discussing trade deals or contracts.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Trade Factor makes two attacks using its Rapier.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage.

bonus.name: Gold Talks
entries: The Trade Factor throws 10 GP to creature within 30 Feet. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or gain the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the end of the factor’s next turn.

reaction.name: Paper Shield (1/Day)
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature that the Trade Factor can see makes an attack roll against the factor. {@actResponse} The Trade Factor produces a legal document sealed with a magical ward and imposes {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attacker’s roll.

trait.name: Academic Specialty
entries: The Preceptor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all ability checks using their chosen specialty and gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to a type of damage.

items: Biothurge, Master of Warbreed: Medicine, Poison
Blastwright, Master of Machines: Arcana, Fire
Decosopher, Master of Alchemy: Nature, Acid

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Preceptor makes three Scalpel attacks. It can replace one Scalpel attack with an Acid Flask, Incendiary Bomb, or Neurotoxic Cloud.

action.name: Scalpel
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d4 + 5}) Slashing damage, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Acid Flask (Decosopher)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Acid damage and 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Acid damage at the end of the target’s next turn.

action.name: Incendiary Bomb (Blastwright)
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Fire damage and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Neurotoxic Cloud (Biothurge)
entries: Targets in a 20-ft. Cone take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. If the target succeeds on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, it takes half damage and is not {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

bonus.name: Impromptu Golem (Biothurge, 1/Day)
entries: The biothurge turns a twisted mass spare bodies parts into a {@b {@creature Zombie|XMM}}. The zombie lasts until the end of the biothurge’s next turn or until it is destroyed. The biothurge can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to extend the duration for 1 round.

bonus.name: Latent Decoction (Decosopher, 1/Day)
entries: The decosopher activates a potion stored in its blood stream. The decosopher is affected by its choice of: {@i Haste, Healing (Superior), Speed, or Water Breathing}.

bonus.name: Magitek Interference (Blastwright, 1/Day)
entries: The blastwright enjoins a magical item or golem to cease functioning until the beginning of the blastwright’s next turn. If the item is held or is a creature, a successful {@dc 17} Intelligence saving throw resists the interference.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Preceptor casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}, +9 to hit with spell attacks):

items.name: Academic Specialty: Master of Warbreed
entries: The Preceptor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all ability checks using {@skill Medicine|XPHB}  and gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison.

items.name: Academic Specialty: Master of Machines
entries: The Preceptor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all ability checks using {@skill Arcana|XPHB}  and gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire.

items.name: Academic Specialty: Master of Alchemy
entries: The Preceptor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all ability checks using {@skill Nature|XPHB}  and gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid.

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The Veteran can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Group Discipline
entries: The Veteran has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws if at least one of its allies is within 5 Feet and both the Veteran and the ally don’t have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Individual Skill
entries: The first time on a turn that the Veteran deals damage with an attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} damage if the Veteran has no allies within 5 Feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Veteran makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The Veteran can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Group Discipline
entries: The Veteran has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws if at least one of its allies is within 5 Feet and both the Veteran and the ally don’t have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Individual Skill
entries: The first time on a turn that the Veteran deals damage with an attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} damage if the Veteran has no allies within 5 Feet of it.

trait.name: Power Shot
entries: The first time the archer deals damage with a Ranged Weapon on its turn, it deal an additional 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Piercing damage to the target, and the target’s {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 Feet until the end of the archer’s next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Veteran makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The Veteran can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Group Discipline
entries: The Veteran has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws if at least one of its allies is within 5 Feet and both the Veteran and the ally don’t have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Individual Skill
entries: The first time on a turn that the Veteran deals damage with an attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} damage if the Veteran has no allies within 5 Feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Veteran makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Battlemage casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material component and using Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}).

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The Veteran can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Group Discipline
entries: The Veteran has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws if at least one of its allies is within 5 Feet and both the Veteran and the ally don’t have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Individual Skill
entries: The first time on a turn that the Veteran deals damage with an attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} damage if the Veteran has no allies within 5 Feet of it.

trait.name: Poisonous Blade
entries: The first time the infiltrator deals damage with a Melee Weapon on its turn, it deals an additional 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Poison damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of the infiltrator’s next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Veteran makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The Veteran can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Group Discipline
entries: The Veteran has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws if at least one of its allies is within 5 Feet and both the Veteran and the ally don’t have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Individual Skill
entries: The first time on a turn that the Veteran deals damage with an attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} damage if the Veteran has no allies within 5 Feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Veteran makes two Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Acid Bomb (2/day)
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point within 60 Feet. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Acid damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only. Success or Fail: The area becomes Heavily Obscured for 1 minute or until dispersed by a Strong wind.

trait.name: Arms Expert
entries: The captain can don or doff its {@item Shield|XPHB} whenever it changes weapons.

trait.name: Field Commander
entries: Allies within 60 Feet who can see or hear the captain have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The captain makes three attacks using Longsword or Longbow in any combination.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Tactical Advance
entries: Each Free Swords ally within 30 Feet of the captain can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

reaction.name: Tactical Response
entries: {@actTrigger} The captain or a Free Swords ally that the captain can see is hit by an attack roll. {@actResponse} The captain commands one Free Swords ally within 90 Feet who can see or hear the captain to use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@condition Speed|XPHB} and make one Weapon Attack against the attacking creature.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The enforcer makes two attacks using their Dagger and Fighting Chain in any combination.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Fighting Chain
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 12}). The enforcer can only have one creature grappled by its fighting chain.

bonus.name: Strangle
entries: The enforcer deals 4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage to a target {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by its {@item Fighting Chain|GrimHollowPG24}, and the target is unable to speak until the start of the enforcer’s next turn or until it is no longer {@condition Grappled|XPHB}.

trait.name: Customized Poison
entries: The assassin prepares and applies specialized poisons to its weapons. The first time each turn that the assassin deals weapon damage to a creature, choose one of the following:

items.name: Abduction
items.entry: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The condition ends if the target takes damage or a creature within 5 Feet of it takes an action to wake it. {@actSaveSuccess} The target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Annihilation
items.entry: The target takes 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Poison damage.

items.name: Debilitation
items.entry: The target takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Poison damage, and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of the assassin’s next turn.

items.name: Suffocation
items.entry: The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, the target can’t speak or breathe.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If the assassin is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the assassin instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can’t use this trait if it has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The assassin makes three attacks using its Daggers or one attack using its Heavy Crossbow.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 4d4 + 5}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Heavy Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 4d10 + 5}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Cunning Action
entries: The assassin takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

reaction.name: Smoke Bomb (2/Day)
entries: {@actTrigger} The assassin takes damage from another creature. {@actResponse} The assassin creates smoke in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. The area becomes {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} for 1 minute or until dispersed by a strong wind. As part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, the assassin can move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and take the Hide action.

monster.name: Thaumaturge Initiated
languages: one regional language plus three other languages

trait.name: Disguise Other
entries: When the initiated casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB}, it can choose to have it affect up to 3 additional creatures (including {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostiles}s) that it can see within 60 Feet. It can choose the same or different disguises for each affected creature. When cast in this manner, the effect lasts for 1 minute or until the initiated chooses to dismiss it from that creature.

trait.name: Personalized Arcane Burst
entries: Each initiated is specialized in one pair of damage types from the following list:

items: Acid and Poison
Bludgeoning and Necrotic
Cold and Fire

entries: When the initiated uses the Arcane Burst action, it can choose to change the attack’s damage type from Force to one of the two selected damage types.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The initiated makes three Arcane Burst attacks.

action.name: Arcane Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Force damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The initiated casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

bonus.name: Malignant Transposition (3/Day)
entries: The initiated creates clouds in a 5-ft radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} in its space and in up to three additional spaces it can see within 60 Feet. The Spheres are {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} except to the initiated. The Spheres last until the start of the initiate’s next turn, or they can be dispersed by a strong wind.

When the clouds appear, the initiated can target one creature in one cloud with the following effect. {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} The creature swaps places with the initiated. The initiated can also teleport themselves to an unoccupied space within one of the other clouds.

reaction.name: Arcane Reflection (3/Day)
entries: The initiated casts {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell’s trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting. If the triggering attack misses and it is a spell or {@variantrule Magical Effect|XPHB}, the initiated can immediately end the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell to have the triggering attack target another creature within range of the spell’s originator.

monster.name: Thaumaturge Archmage
languages: one regional language plus five other languages

trait.name: Collaborative Rift Control
entries: When multiple Thaumaturge Archmages capable of creating and controlling Arcane Rifts are present, they can interact with each other’s rifts, and for every additional Thaumaturge Archmage, the maximum possible level of each rift increases by 2, up to a maximum of 15.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the archmage fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The archmage has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Personalized Arcane Burst
entries: Each archmage is specialized in one pair of damage types from the following list:

items: Acid and Poison
Bludgeoning and Necrotic
Cold and Fire

entries: When the archmage uses the Arcane Burst action, it can choose to change the attack’s damage type from Force to one of the two selected damage types.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The archmage makes four Arcane Burst attacks or makes two Arcane Burst attacks and uses the Spellcasting action.

action.name: Arcane Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 150 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 4d10 + 5}) Force damage and the creature is pushed back 10 Feet.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The archmage casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

bonus.name: Misty Step
entries: The archmage casts {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

reaction.name: Arcane Contingency
entries: {@actTrigger} The archmage is reduced to 40 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or less. {@actResponse} The archmage can cast {@spell Dimension Door|XPHB}. This does not consume a use of the spell.

reaction.name: Arcane Countermeasure
entries: {@actTrigger} The archmage is targeted by a spell, or the archmage is in the area of effect of a spell being cast. {@actResponse} The archmage casts {@spell Shield|XPHB} or {@spell Counterspell|XPHB}, or casts {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} targeting a spell in response to seeing that spell take effect. The archmage uses the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The archmage uses the Spellcasting action.

legendary.name: Create Arcane Rift (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The archmage creates a level 5 Arcane Rift in an unoccupied space the archmage can see within 120 Feet. The Arcane Rift is a Large magical object, which hovers, has 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} per Arcane Rift level, and has an {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} of 17. In addition to standard object properties, the Arcane Rift is Resistant to damage from spells and spell effects and is  {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to all conditions.
Creatures within 30 Feet of the Arcane Rift treat all movement away from the Arcane Rift as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

The Arcane Rift can be dispelled with a {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} spell, treating the Arcane Rift’s level as its spell level. It can also be destroyed if targeted by a {@spell Disintegrate|XPHB} spell.

items.name: Infuse Arcane Rift
entries: The archmage increases the level of one Arcane Rift it can see within 120 Feet by 2, up to a maximum level of 9.

items.name: Ignite Arcane Rift
entries: The archmage causes one Arcane Rift it can see within 120 Feet to expand to Huge or Gargantuan size before exploding, destroying the Arcane Rift. All creatures within 30 Feet are subjected to the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}. {@actSaveFail} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage per Arcane Rift level. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

items.name: Arcane Rift Summoning
entries: The archmage targets one Arcane Rift it can see within 120 Feet, funneling its magic to cast {@spell Summon Dragon|XPHB} using the Arcane Rift level as the Spell Slot level, destroying the Arcane Rift in the process. Upon summoning, the archmage can change the summoned creature’s creature type to any of the following: Aberration, Beast, Elemental, Fiend, or Monstrosity. This casting requires no somatic or material components.

monster.name: Watchful Priest
languages: one regional language plus one other language

trait.name: Enraging Sacrifice
entries: When the priest dies, each ally within 20 Feet who can see the priest gains 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute.

trait.name: Zealous Aura
entries: The priest radiates an aura of zeal in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from it. Each ally in the area has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} checks, and their successful attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage. This aura becomes inactive if the priest has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The priest makes two Castigating Word attacks.

action.name: Castigating Word
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) Radiant damage, and one ally of the priest within 20 Feet of the target can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The priest casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

bonus.name: Righteous Morale (1/Day)
entries: The priest chooses up to three other creatures it can see within 20 Feet of it. Each target regains 4 ({@damage 1d8}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and has  {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions until the start of the priest's next turn.

monster.name: High Priest
languages: one regional language plus two other languages

trait.name: Enraging Sacrifice
entries: When the priest dies, each ally within 40 Feet who can see the priest gains 20 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 hour.

trait.name: Zealous Aura
entries: The priest radiates an aura of zeal in a 40-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from it. Each ally in the area has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} checks, and their successful attacks deal an extra {@damage 3d6} Radiant damage. This aura becomes inactive if the priest has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The high priest makes three Sharp Rebuke attacks.

action.name: Sharp Rebuke
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Radiant damage, and one ally of the priest within 20 Feet of the target can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to regain 9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and make a melee attack.

action.name: Call Soldiers of the Faith
entries: The high priest calls forth celestials that take the form of {@dice 1d4 + 1||Warrior Veterans} {@creature Warrior Veteran|XMM|Warrior Veterans} or {@dice 1d3||Lions} {@creature Lion|XMM|Lions} that enter the {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} at the end of the high priest’s turn. The celestials follow the priest’s commands and remain for 1 minute or until slain.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The priest casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

bonus.name: Inspiring Monologue (3/Day)
entries: The priest chooses any number of allies within 60 Feet who can hear the priest. Until the end of the priest's next turn, each target has  {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions and does not provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

reaction.name: To Me!
entries: {@actTrigger} The priest is attacked or targeted by a spell. {@actResponse} The high priest chooses a Small or Medium ally within 5 Feet of itself. The priest and that ally swap places, and the ally gains 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and becomes the target of the attack or spell instead.

monsterFluff.name: Avatar of Slaughter
entries: In any war, the most vicious combatants revel in carnage, destroying homes and lives without a second thought. Within the confines of such conflicts, a {@spell Curse of Uncontrollable Wrath|GrimHollowPG24} can be the most powerful instrument of vengeance.

Brave knights and bloodthirsty tyrants alike are reduced to a state of primal rage upon becoming an Avatar of Slaughter. Under the right circumstances, even a pacifist can fall prey to this curse if the perpetrator responsible is truly vile.

An Avatar of Slaughter appears as a daemonic minotaur with smoke fuming from its nostrils, existing only to kill any one unfortunate enough to cross its path. They often seek out friends and family first, as they unconsciously attempt to demolish their former lives.

Engaging an Avatar of Slaughter in combat is a task best left to the extremely brave or equally reckless. Born in the fires of conflict, they cannot be completely killed by it, though their affinity for violence means a defeated Avatar of Slaughter’s essence seeks refuge within a weapon.

monsterFluff.name: Bloated Gastromorph
entries: Often whispered of in folk tales and legends, most frequently as a warning to children regarding the gruesome consequences awaiting those who recklessly indulge, the Bloated Gastromorph is an incarnation of gluttony and selfishness. This horrific creature has been transformed and twisted by the final stages of a {@spell Curse of Ravenous Hunger|GrimHollowPG24}.

Those afflicted become an enormous, slug-like monster possessing multiple fist-like claws, a wicked tentacular tongue, and a giant, voracious maw of gnashing teeth. Often dwelling in dank ruins of once-great castles, a Bloated Gastromorph cares about nothing but consuming every morsel of flesh it encounters, living or not. Bereft of memories of their prior lives, these creatures dwell fully in this new existence of unfettered consumption.

monsterFluff.name: Body Snatcher
entries: Body Snatchers are humanoids who have been transformed by a {@spell Curse of Damned Aging|GrimHollowPG24}. Notoriously difficult to locate, Body Snatchers frequently hide in plain sight, since they essentially resemble their host creature. It’s common that when on the trail of a Body Snatcher, monster hunters and investigators find themselves trailing a step behind the creature. The only evidence that typically remains is the withered and discarded skin sack of their latest victim.

Body Snatchers possess an amorphous form, which they use to inhabit the bodies of their victims, living inside them as a parasite that consumes their being from the inside. The Body Snatcher takes complete control of its host body, enhancing it with supernatural capabilities. The only sign that an individual has been invaded by a Body Snatcher is their erratic behavior and rapid aging—a host body can appear to age a lifetime in a matter of a few weeks.

While far from helpless, Body Snatchers prefer to avoid detection, engaging enemies only in dire circumstances or when changing hosts. They typically make every effort to complete the latter in seclusion to maintain their gruesome charade.

monsterFluff.name: Dream Whisperer
entries: Those learned individuals with aspirations of greatness, but who have fallen to fate’s twisted ironies and been afflicted by a {@spell Curse of Lost Sentiment|GrimHollowPG24}, may emerge from such trials as a Dream Whisperer. Now a deformed, hunched creature covered in contorted arms, the Dream Whisperer uses its many hands to grasp and shred the hopes, dreams, thoughts, and memories of its victims.

Though animalistic in behavior, Dream Whisperers often reside within larger settlements, cities, and the like, where they can spread their influence and remain undetected for long periods of time. Preferring to attack at night, Dream Whisperers often sneak into unsuspecting targets’ homes through windows and otherwise inaccessible openings, since their many arms allow them to scale otherwise impossible surfaces. Once inside, their arms serve a second function— to physically tear treasured memories from their victims and replace them with feelings of paranoia and doubt.

Fighting a Dream Whisperer is a dangerous task. Brave heroes have been driven mad by the Dream Whisperer’s aberrant attacks. Comrades-in-arms have been compelled to turn on each other, and the weakest minds snap, leaving a drooling mess in the creature’s wake. With each stolen memory, the Dream Whisperer seeks to slake its unquenchable thirst for its lost cognition.

entries.caption: Lost Memories
outro: A {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell reverses this effect and restores the victim’s memory.
rows: The target becomes deeply suspicious of a character or NPC of the GM’s choice. They gain the following flaw: “I know they are seeking to betray me. I have to act first—it’s me or them!”
The target forgets all memories of a loved one of the GM’s choice. The next time the target sees them, it’s as if they are meeting them for the first time.
The target becomes stricken with doubt and gains the following flaw: “The world is out to get me, and I can’t win. I may as well give up now.”
The target forgets how to speak in one language known, decided by the GM.
The target forgets the location of something important to them, of the GM’s choice. It may be the location of a goal, their home settlement, or a hidden treasure.
The target loses an inconsequential memory such as what they ate a few nights ago or the name of an unimportant NPC.

monsterFluff.name: Herald of Calamity
entries: The tragic individuals who become Heralds of Calamity were afflicted by the Fates themselves, or so the story goes. As recipients of a {@spell Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune|GrimHollowPG24}, Heralds of Calamity bring tragedy upon all who linger in their presence.

Legends say these foul beings hunt unseen, lurking near vital locations such as a village well, bridge, or other communal site where they can inflict many casualties before survivors think to abandon the place.

When seen, Heralds of Calamity appear as incorporeal skeletal figures, though they are invisible except under the light of the moon. Unlike other curse monsters, Heralds of Calamity are not physically formidable, nor do they wield tremendous arcane power.

Many monster hunters, however, have fallen believing that a Herald of Calamity would be an easy challenge. Expert archers have misfired, fatally wounding companions. Seasoned adventurers have slipped on trivial obstacles and received debilitating injuries.
Regardless of the circumstance, all who have engaged a Herald of Calamity have the same advice: Rely on the maxim that if something can possibly go wrong, it will.

monsterFluff.name: Mind Siphon
entries: When an individual places too much value on their own opinions and ability, and simultaneously mistrusts all others, they risk drawing the ire of those capable of afflicting them with a {@spell Curse of Fastidious Pride|GrimHollowPG24}. The curse begins with an ever-growing sense of hubris regarding their own abilities and knowledge. As their narcissism grows, their feelings of being valued by others diminish. Eventually the afflicted individual cannot rest, believing that tireless work and toil is the only way for them to live up to their own self-perceived greatness. The transformation into a hideous, malformed, multi-limbed abomination is coupled with an insatiable need to feed on the intelligence of others. As their victims multiply, the Mind Siphon uses their dismembered corpses to construct a macabre hive-like dwelling.

The Mind Siphon has a twisted insectoid appearance, possessing the torso of the original individual where the thorax would reside. In place of their original head is a gaping maw of fangs and tentacular tongues. From the base of the torso, the remainder of this creature resembles a chitinous abdomen with many appendages ending in hands upon which it walks. Lastly, a mouthlike protrusion at its front is topped by a dozen or more blinking eyes.

monsterFluff.name: Plague Carrion
entries: A Plague Carrion appears as a vile, decaying creature spewing pestilence across the landscape, destroying vegetation and poisoning living creatures unlucky enough to cross its path. A Plague Carrion is a sad creature that loathes its own existence and eschews combat, though such a creature defends itself when forced or cornered.

Despite being the least aggressive of all the curse monsters, individual Plague Carrions are believed to represent the greatest threat to the people of Etharis. Some even say Weeping Pox is a product of a Plague Carrion.

A {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24} is most frequently used by hags and dark sorcerers to spite beautiful rivals out of jealousy. Those who fall victim suffer a miserable fate. If not cured quickly, the victim becomes a Plague Carrion, a deformed creature covered in gaping cysts that expel clouds of profane corruption. Venomous insects belch forth from these calloused apertures.

A Plague Carrion is compelled by its curse to constantly wander, tormented by its own corporeal decay if it lingers in the same place for too long. This cruel punishment ensures its foul blight spreads far and wide throughout the land.

monsterFluff.name: Sightless Agony
entries: Transforming into a Sightless Agony is the result of being afflicted with a {@spell Curse of Crushing Sensation|GrimHollowPG24}. Victims find themselves fleeing the company of others in search of peaceful solitude, as even the gentle sounds of nature become jarring and obtrusive to these poor souls. Such unfortunates have often left those behind who care for them, or perhaps even those who have the need for some rare skill the individual possesses.

A Sightless Agony seeks a dark, quiet place to hide itself. They often tear out their own eyes and claw away their skin to escape the agony of sensation, and the company of others becomes entirely unbearable. In such a state, they know only a boundless, white-hot rage.

When discovered, a Sightless Agony appears as a mutated gargoyle with skin flayed from its malformed figure. They attack with razor-sharp claws and a mouthful of fangs, and though a Sightless Agony possesses wings, they are loath to fly far, since the feeling of the wind is nearly unbearable. Even standing in one place, the Sightless Agony quivers and twitches as sensations from their immediate environment cause them to flinch at the unseen.

monsterFluff.name: Verminous Abomination
entries: Few demonstrate avarice like those who fall victim to a {@spell Curse of Insatiable Greed|GrimHollowPG24}. Those afflicted transform into a bloated, rat-like creature known as a Verminous Abomination. The Verminous Abomination is consumed with a desire for possessions—from beautiful riches to the discarded trash of city dwellers: the form or value of the trinket does not matter. All objects are prized by a Verminous Abomination, and for another to possess them is considered an offense.

Verminous Abominations dwell beneath large population centers, creating labyrinthine lairs to guard the hoards they amass. Adventurers who seek to clear out a complex— be it within sewers, mines, caverns, or wherever such a creature might dwell—rarely return to the surface, as fighting a Verminous Abomination in its lair is a challenge that few can overcome.

This giant, malformed beast spews swarms of wretched ratlings from its bulging stomach, while it navigates its filth-ridden lair. It emerges from the surrounding filth to ambush interlopers at opportune moments, and then fleeing when overwhelmed.

monsterFluff.name: Weeping Willow
entries: The wise have been known to provide this advice: if you hear crying in a woodland or forest that has become strangely quiet, run as quickly as you can— though it may already be too late. The cry of a Weeping Willow is difficult to ignore and has often led the unwary to an untimely demise.

A Weeping Willow is the unfortunate victim of a {@spell Curse of Conceited Obsession|GrimHollowPG24}. Once a vain or narcissistic individual, they become a twisted half-tree, half-humanoid creature warped by self-love and malicious intent.

A Weeping Willow is obsessed with its own appearance, calling for brave heroes to aid it in its distress. Upon reaching the Weeping Willow, however, heroes find only a vicious predator, a grove of unusual shimmering trees, and a very likely fate worse than death.

Combating a Weeping Willow unprepared often ends in disaster. The creature’s enthralling cries and illusory beauty are difficult to resist, drawing its prey closer. The touch of a Weeping Willow can transform any creature's into a tree, bearing mirrored leaves for the vain creature to gaze into. Survivors of these encounters say that if you can resist the creature’s malignant lure, it’s best to leave them alone with their gardens of reflecting trees and self-admiration.

entries.name: {@book Augustine Trading Company|GrimHollowCG24|13|Augustine Trading Company}

entries: Success in all ventures and circumstances requires the unhindered flow of goods and services.

A bastion of free trade, champion of education, and at the forefront of innovation, the venerable Augustine Trading Company is everywhere in Etharis. Behind its shining image is a ruthless corporation with a draconic thirst for coin. From its headquarters in Morencia, the Company’s tendrils reach every corner of the known world, and even some corners long forgotten.

Founded by the disgraced clergyman Manoch Augustus, the Company originated as a local weapon distributor and evolved to become a global powerbroker. Though nominally headquartered in Morencia, the Company is found anywhere shipping lanes, caravans, or black-market networks do business.

Administered by a council of five called the Montarri lead by the financier Kolyhs “The Blazing Brain” Papinian, the Company controls various shell companies and governments via proxy ownership, debt-leverage, or economic entrapment. It funds wars, builds cities, collapses markets, and breeds monsters for battlefield use. It even maintains elite universities to develop the next generation of talent. The Company always takes the long view.

To the public, the Augustine Trading Company is a necessary engine of civilization, but in truth, it is a gilded machine of exploitation creating an empire bound by ledgers and blood. Adventurers who serve its interests are well-paid but expendable, and those who cross it face economic ruin… or worse. To many, the Company isn’t just business—it’s destiny.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Augustine Trading Company has employees from middle managers and Trade Factors, to specialists like Preceptors and containment agents who specialize in working with magical beasts. The most talented agents are usually headquartered in Morencia and dispatched as needed.

Local branch directors cultivate their own specialists, deploying them only for the most important missions where their talents are critical to success. Instead, mercenaries and adventurers make disposable employees when situations are dangerous or require deniability. Pay is quite generous, as are the perks that often accompany such missions, so being selected by the Augustine Trading Company is quite a triumph for an adventuring party. The tasks are usually quite clear and uncomplicated, as long as the party has few morals and even fewer scruples.

entries.name: {@book Company of Free Swords|GrimHollowCG24|13|Company of Free Swords}

entries: Blood and Honor, Strength in Steel

The Company of Free Swords is the pre-eminent mercenary organization on the continent, with branches in most major cities. The company takes all manner of contracts—from ridding a village of a monster, to protecting a town from bandit raids, to sending hundreds to fight a full-fledged war for a well-paying lord.

The company has a code of honor and an elite reputation they make sure to spread. Despite the name, its services do not come for free. If you want the best loyalty money can buy, a company known for slaying daemon lords and sending aether kindred into the void, then you want the Company of Free Swords, and you better be ready to pay.

Despite their love for coin, the Free Swords are not unscrupulous. The company's honor, its soldiers' well-being, and the outright refusal to commit atrocities are core principles. While individual branches may at times be swayed by coin to stray from their code, the Free Swords are known to “discipline” their own, so none take that chance lightly.
Skill and honor are the main qualifications for joining the Free Swords, and they have attracted warriors, priests, druids, rogues, spies, and monster hunters into their ranks.

entries.name: Agents
entries: Historically, new recruits into the Swords—regardless of their training or military service—spend at least a year in regimented training. The action they see is plenty dangerous, but only under strict veteran oversight. The training is as much to ensure their combat prowess as it is to instill the Company mindset and tenets.

Since Yrsa’s unorthodox yet well-documented ascension, there have been more exceptions made for those with proven records and skills. This has been met with decidedly mixed feelings among the veterans.

entries.name: Ranks and Pay
entries: The Free Swords embrace their “simple soldier” mindset and avoid a complex hierarchy. A recruit who’s completed their first-year training and initial engagements becomes a Free Sword — the typical foot soldier of the company. All Free Swords of the same rank are paid the same share.

After at least five years and having proven themselves, one becomes either a Veteran or a Specialist, gaining a bigger share than a regular Free Sword. Specialists are Veterans with unique skills. These include sappers who use engineering, alchemy, or magic to affect the battlefield, battlemages who are field-level combatants that bring powerful spell support, and infiltrators who are skilled in stealth, poison, and neutralization of individual targets. Other specialists — including several types of ranged combatants — also exist. Specialists must be proven in their area and receive a higher coin share than a Veteran. Finally, those who lead are promoted to Captains. Captains get the highest share of the coin.
Although there is no combat rank higher than Captain, there is a leadership hierarchy among the different chapters, which determines rank among the Captains.

In the field, a Captain can lead as few as one other Sword and as many as several hundred. For large battles, temporary military-style designations are given to different Captains to create the necessary hierarchies. This allows the company to field units of dramatically different sizes.

entries.name: Equipment and Visual Identity
entries: Free Swords are always proficient with the sword and {@item Shield|XPHB}, and many choose to carry these tried-and-true implements into battle. The company itself does not mandate equipment and encourages its members to take pride in the procurement and maintenance of the best weapons of war for themselves.

In times of mass battle, the Free Swords don their distinctive red and gold “Double Lion” tabards or those provided by their employer. Otherwise, the mercenaries are allowed to wear what they want. All are issued a sturdy double lion brooch, which they wear with pride, except in cases of espionage or other subterfuge.

While the company rarely provides more than the very basics of equipment, they do have access to some of the best merchants and artisans across Etharis, including many who set up permanently on-site at company locations. After all, where better to get a good price for weapons of war than amongst well-paid mercenaries for whom such things could mean the difference between life and death?

entries.name: Experienced Leaders
entries: Veterans of the Company of Free Swords are formidable foes on the battlefield. They are well equipped and combine the tactics of a well-oiled military machine with individual flare.
