While the company rarely provides more than the very basics of equipment, they do have access to some of the best merchants and artisans across Etharis, including many who set up permanently on-site at company locations. After all, where better to get a good price for weapons of war than amongst well-paid mercenaries for whom such things could mean the difference between life and death?

entries.name: Experienced Leaders
entries: Veterans of the Company of Free Swords are formidable foes on the battlefield. They are well equipped and combine the tactics of a well-oiled military machine with individual flare.

entries.name: {@book Company of Free Swords|GrimHollowCG24|13|Company of Free Swords}

entries: Blood and Honor, Strength in Steel

The Company of Free Swords is the pre-eminent mercenary organization on the continent, with branches in most major cities. The company takes all manner of contracts—from ridding a village of a monster, to protecting a town from bandit raids, to sending hundreds to fight a full-fledged war for a well-paying lord.

The company has a code of honor and an elite reputation they make sure to spread. Despite the name, its services do not come for free. If you want the best loyalty money can buy, a company known for slaying daemon lords and sending aether kindred into the void, then you want the Company of Free Swords, and you better be ready to pay.

Despite their love for coin, the Free Swords are not unscrupulous. The company's honor, its soldiers' well-being, and the outright refusal to commit atrocities are core principles. While individual branches may at times be swayed by coin to stray from their code, the Free Swords are known to “discipline” their own, so none take that chance lightly.
Skill and honor are the main qualifications for joining the Free Swords, and they have attracted warriors, priests, druids, rogues, spies, and monster hunters into their ranks.

entries.name: Agents
entries: Historically, new recruits into the Swords—regardless of their training or military service—spend at least a year in regimented training. The action they see is plenty dangerous, but only under strict veteran oversight. The training is as much to ensure their combat prowess as it is to instill the Company mindset and tenets.

Since Yrsa’s unorthodox yet well-documented ascension, there have been more exceptions made for those with proven records and skills. This has been met with decidedly mixed feelings among the veterans.

entries.name: Ranks and Pay
entries: The Free Swords embrace their “simple soldier” mindset and avoid a complex hierarchy. A recruit who’s completed their first-year training and initial engagements becomes a Free Sword — the typical foot soldier of the company. All Free Swords of the same rank are paid the same share.

After at least five years and having proven themselves, one becomes either a Veteran or a Specialist, gaining a bigger share than a regular Free Sword. Specialists are Veterans with unique skills. These include sappers who use engineering, alchemy, or magic to affect the battlefield, battlemages who are field-level combatants that bring powerful spell support, and infiltrators who are skilled in stealth, poison, and neutralization of individual targets. Other specialists — including several types of ranged combatants — also exist. Specialists must be proven in their area and receive a higher coin share than a Veteran. Finally, those who lead are promoted to Captains. Captains get the highest share of the coin.
Although there is no combat rank higher than Captain, there is a leadership hierarchy among the different chapters, which determines rank among the Captains.

In the field, a Captain can lead as few as one other Sword and as many as several hundred. For large battles, temporary military-style designations are given to different Captains to create the necessary hierarchies. This allows the company to field units of dramatically different sizes.

entries.name: Equipment and Visual Identity
entries: Free Swords are always proficient with the sword and {@item Shield|XPHB}, and many choose to carry these tried-and-true implements into battle. The company itself does not mandate equipment and encourages its members to take pride in the procurement and maintenance of the best weapons of war for themselves.

In times of mass battle, the Free Swords don their distinctive red and gold “Double Lion” tabards or those provided by their employer. Otherwise, the mercenaries are allowed to wear what they want. entries: Veterans of the Company of Free Swords are formidable foes on the battlefield. They are well equipped and combine the tactics of a well-oiled military machine with individual flare.

entries.name: {@book Ebon Syndicate|GrimHollowCG24|13|Ebon Syndicate}

entries: The night is our kingdom.

If behind every unexplained success lies an undiscovered crime, then the Ebon Syndicate’s successes are as innumerable as their crimes. The syndicate’s tendrils stretch out from the seedy shadows into every aspect of urban life in Etharis, and often beyond.

They are the beggar on the corner inconspicuously eavesdropping on nobles’ conversations. They are the thief cutting a purse string. They are the traceless spy planting evidence before the militia raid. They are the hooded assassin coming for you in the dark alley, and the smiling waiter serving you poisoned wine should you escape that alley. They have the thing you want, and the thing you need, and their eyes on the thing you hold most dear.

The syndicate primarily operates in Etharis’s biggest and richest cities, where targets and opportunities are most plentiful. Lesser organizations and freelance operatives soon find themselves facing a deadly offer: stop, join, pay, or find yourselves bleeding in a gutter. The latter is not an idle threat, but a quickly fulfilled promise—those who step on the Syndicate’s toes or try to take their territory are mercilessly dispatched.

Etharis’s other major factions may look down upon the syndicate’s activities, but they know enough to respect its capabilities. Even groups like the Augustine Company and the Watchers that publicly claim to oppose the syndicate have been known to take {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} of its unparallelled spy network.
The syndicate pursues every kind of under-the-table opportunity. From petty crime to organized drug smuggling, extortion, and assassination — if it needs to be done outside the law, they get it done.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The unknowing agents of the Syndicate are many. A wash maid with access to everyone from soldiers to nobles is a perfect scout for a den of thieves. A duke’s steward with a gambling problem may divulge the an important delegate's arrival, enabling a road ambush that can’t be traced. A gang of street thugs tipped off to a rich dandy’s passage won’t know that their attack forced the victim’s influential father promt a military reinforcement, leaving a distant outpost ripe for the taking. Such are the webs the Syndicate spins.

entries.name: The Shadow Academy
entries: For the chosen few identified to join as full members of the Syndicate, there is the Shadow Academy. The academy is not a school or training ground; there are no stern masked instructors preaching rules, and there is no graduation to a fancy moth mask. The Shadow Academy is itself a tenet, a concept of how agents are trained.

The core philosophy of the academy is of hands-on training: “Every lesson is an action; every action is a test.” Passage through the Shadow Academy is typically overseen by the member who vouched for the recruit. Trust is paramount. If you’re not willing to vouch for those you bring in, then they do not deserve anyone else’s time.

Recruits are expected to bring some level of craft to the Syndicate, but more importantly, the right mindset. Once a recruit is brought in, they are mentored by different specialists, learning tasks on the job, before being passed on to the next. The recruit’s performance is discussed by the leadership when they change mentors.

Promising recruits who fail at certain tasks are given chances to excel in other areas. Recruits that fail repeatedly, or who embarrass the Syndicate, tend to disappear. Many are killed, but some—if the knowledge they have poses little danger—may be released and watched or relocated. While there are no “instructors” per se, the Syndicate encourages skilled members to pass their knowledge on. In exchange, they get a skilled new member for their own crew.

entries.name: Specialists
entries: The Syndicate is not a military organization and has no official ranks other than Grayman. Members are referred to by name or alias, and often by their specialties, often using local slang. For example, spies might be called “ears,” “listeners,” or “flies,” while assassins could be “blades,” “blackmoths,” or “fins.” Slang varies widely across the continent.

entries.name: Local Enforcers
entries: Street-level agents deliver overt official messages. Thugs and brutes with some manners and basic business sense can beat both into a target when the need arises. In addition to common weapons, Syndicate enforcers favor fighting chains or “chain-knuckles” that give them the choice to strangle or bind their opponents. These have the benefit of being less conspicuous than a blade.

entries.name: {@book Ebon Syndicate|GrimHollowCG24|13|Ebon Syndicate}

entries.name: Elite Assassins
entries: The Syndicate's most skilled assassins concoct specialized compounds for each mission. They can make their target spasm in pain, suffocate in silence, or simply fall unconscious, allowing the agent to wisk them away or stage the murder as desired. Their smoke bombs help them escape or turn an unexpected fight in their favour.

entries.name: {@book Thaumaturge|GrimHollowCG24|13|Thaumaturge}

entries: We who shape magic, shape the world.

“Dear reader, it is only fair that you should be warned at this point, that if you’ve come to the pages of this fine tome for concrete information on the organization known as Grand Thaumatic Order of Archmages, you may find the words contained herein lacking. This is not for the lack of effort by the humble documenters of these things, but due to the simple truth that despite great effort, we have not been able to collect even a single piece of hard evidence of the organization’s existence…”

So reads the introduction to The Powers of Etharis— one of the best researched (if somewhat out of date) historical tomes known. If the Grand Thaumatic Order of Archmages, or Thaumaturge in short, does exist, then they have gone to great lengths to conceal their presence.

The existence of a secret society of archmages seems only logical. Between divination and arcane history, such individuals are likely to not only be aware of each other, but also to share many common interests and compete over high-magic knowledge and artifacts. The fact that there is no war amongst the world’s highest mages implies a certain level of cooperation or oversight.

Whether the widespread theories of the Thaumaturge having unleashed the Weeping Pox or banished Ulmyr’s Gate to thwart a competing arcane organization have any grain of truth is unknown. Even if not directly involved, the members of the order should at least know about them.
What is certain is that every great magical event, whether calamitous or benevolent, has been either blamed on or attributed to the order, and in many cases both.

entries.name: Agents
entries: Like the Ebon Syndicate, the Thaumaturge uses many pawns throughout the echelons of society to keep themselves hidden. True members of the order are immensely powerful Mages or Archmages of every stripe.

There is no strict hierarchy or list of titles in the Thaumaturge ranks. Even among its senior members, titles are chaotic, with individuals using whatever they fancy — Archmage, Thaumaturge, Grand Arcanist, Lord Sorcerer, and Witch Queen have all been used. Most commonly, however, individuals of such stature use their names rather than a title.

The most elite mages each have dramatic individual abilities and specialties. They can go beyond using the most powerful known spells, and some can control time and space themselves, and call upon cosmic powers.

entries.name: Initiated Mages
entries: Having been initiated into the Thaumaturge is in itself a badge of honour and recognition of magical power. Such initiates are not to be trifled with. Skilled in deception, illusions, and arcane trickery, initiates can create chaos in social and combat situations. These crafty mages can create patches of obscure areas, switch places with their enemies, and become invisible before unleashing a custom-crafted cantrip or powerful spell.

entries.name: {@book Thaumaturge|GrimHollowCG24|13|Thaumaturge}

entries: We who shape magic, shape the world.

entries.name: Thaumaturge Archmage
entries: Full-fledged Thaumaturge mages are arcane powerhouses. They can easily manipulate magic thrown at them, and teleport around the battlefield while throwing spells at their opponents. They can summon devastating rifts to unleash destructive energy or summon monstrous allies. Multiple mages can combine energies for even grander feats.

entries.name: {@book Watchers of the Faithful|GrimHollowCG24|13|Watchers of the Faithful}

entries: Though the gods no longer grace the heavens, the Arch Seraphs still guide the way.

Born in the fires of the Gods’ End, the Watchers of the Faithful rose to prominence first in Castinella as part of its theocratic government. Priests, paladins, chaplains, and other clergy rallied to the remaining Arch Seraphs to hold the fabric of society together. Throwing their faith behind the Arch Seraphs, the major churches reformed into a large organization dedicated to preserving the old faiths in a new form.

This young faction is utterly devoted to Sagrado Valieda’s Eternal Dogma: revere the Arch Seraphs, follow their laws as handed down by the Divine Speaker, and destroy all that is unholy and evil in their sight through any means necessary. Where the Arcanist Inquisition is militant in rooting out corruption, the Watchers instead pursue Valieda’s mandate to fight for the Arch Seraphs with fiery words. Swords are well and good in defense of the faith, but the real battle is in the hearts and minds of the faithful.

Through preaching and persuasion, the Watchers seek to make the change in the world that they believe the Arch Seraphs desire. Edicts declaring heresy inflame the Inquisition to hunt for monsters or tainted spellcasters. A public proclamation that a government is straying from the true path causes citizens to rise up and force change. Decrees are issued creating new ecclesiastical rules that the faithful embrace over secular laws issued by less religious rulers. Slowly but surely, the Watchers guide the world to a better place.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Watchers of the Faithful are not soldiers. While they may have some have martial training, their members are priests, spies, and evangelists. Watchers fight with words and not blades. They spur faithful populations to turn against those they deem heretical. They are not single heroes, but rather the voice behind the zealous mob. The most common missionaries are Watchful Priests who combine divine spells with an almost magical command of oratory to enflame crowds.

entries.name: {@book Watchers of the Faithful|GrimHollowCG24|13|Watchers of the Faithful}

item.name: Adder Stone
entries: These glassy stones are often found near the place where a marra was slain. Each Adder Stone is the size of a small fruit and has a natural hole in it. While you carry an Adder Stone, the first time you would gain the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition, the stone shatters and that condition is removed.

item.name: Aether Blood
entries: The most extravagant rumors claim the creation of this glowing purple liquid requires the blood of a unicorn -- a creature generally regarded as mythical. Regardless of the truth, the potent magic in a vial of Aether Blood leads many spellcasters to carry it for use in dire circumstances.

Consuming this potion enhances your spellcasting prowess for 1 minute. While the effect lasts, add 1 to the saving throw DCs for the spells you cast. In addition, whenever your spell could be cast using a higher-level spell slot, increase the spell's effective level by 1.

Prolonged use of Aether Blood is grueling. You can consume it safely a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1). Any further use results in painful addiction. While addicted, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength and Constitution ability checks and saving throws, and attack rolls against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

Consuming another vial of Aether Blood negates this effect until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, but you must also make a DC 10 Charisma save. On a failure, you become lethargic, and your {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} is reduced by 20 Feet for 1 minute.
The addiction lasts until you receive a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell, or until you have not consumed Aether Blood for 30 days.

item.name: Amulet of Curse Suppression
entries: This glittering amulet of ivory and silver can be used to temporarily fend off the effects of curses. While attuned to the amulet, the effects of any curses on you are nullified, as though they had been removed by the {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell.
However, this relief is only temporary. When the amulet is removed or you end your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to it, any curses it was suppressing return with full force.

item.name: Angelskin Armor
entries: This leather armor is made from the flayed flesh of angels.

While attuned to the armor, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Any Celestial that sees this armor immediately becomes {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostiles} toward the wearer, and any Fiend wants to possess it at any cost.

item.name: Arcane Oil
entries: This substance is created by distilling the jelly that encases the brain of a xakalonus. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to coat one weapon or up to 20 pieces of ammunition. If the coated objects are not magical, for the next minute, your attacks with the coated weapon or ammunition gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. If the coated objects are magical, they instead gain the ability to ignore the hraptnon's {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to magical weapons.

item.name: Arc Rifle
entries: In the unforgiving lands of Etharis, having reliable and efficient weapons is as essential as carrying water and food.
In that regard, skilled inventors are highly sought, and among them gnomes are the most renowned. However, innovation can sometimes go too far, and the Arc Rifle is the perfect example of how.

This part-science, part-arcane contraption is a rifle-like weapon housing glass tubes of sparking electricity and powered by a small perpetual motion device. Invented by a team of famous gnome engineers, it literally shoots lightning, hitting the target faster than other missile weapons and reducing flesh to ashes in mere seconds. Due in part to this tremendous efficiency, this weapon was quickly declared illegal in most regions of Etharis. It is also believed to destroy the target's soul when it kills them.

The rifle has 4 charges. You can expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to launch a torrent of lightning in a 60-foot long, 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} Lightning damage, or half as much on a successful save.

entries.name: Regaining Charges
entries: The rifle regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll
{@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the rifle becomes a useless hunk of metal and glass.

item.name: Ardor
entries: The blades of this axe smolder with an inner light, and the dark wooden handle is accented with silver. After the celestial realm turned back yet another assault from the forces of evil, a fiendish forge master received a vision, allowing them to craft a weapon like no other. Ardor now lies in a hidden vault, waiting to be wielded by a scion of darkness who strikes on behalf of the forces of the Arch Daemons.

The fiends don't realize that an equally powerful weapon rests in the hands of the celestials, who wage eternal war against them. In the interest of maintaining order, the fates who inspired the forgemaster to craft Ardor also sent a similar vision to a smith of heaven.

entries.name: Axe of Hell
entries: This magic battleaxe grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. When you hit with it, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d10} Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Champion of Evil
entries: While holding the battleaxe, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions.

entries.name: Ardor's Pursuit. Ardor's Pursuit:
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to toss the axe into the air, causing it to hover magically in your space. While the weapon hovers, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause it to fly up to 50 Feet to another spot within 50 Feet of you. As part of the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can either grasp the weapon if it is within reach, or cause it to attack one creature within 5 Feet of the weapon. The weapon uses your attack roll and adds your ability modifier to the damage rolls. The weapon ceases to hover if you grasp it, if you are more than 50 Feet away from it, or if you die.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While the axe is on your person, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18): {@spell Bane|XPHB}, {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB}, or {@spell Incendiary Cloud|XPHB}. Once you use the axe to cast a spell, that spell can't be cast again from it until the next dawn.

entries.name: Destroying the Axe
entries: The only thing capable of damaging the battleaxe is its celestial twin, {@item Zeal|GrimHollowCG24}. A creature wielding {@item Zeal|GrimHollowCG24} can choose to attack Ardor, which has an {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} of 21 and 300 HP. No other creature or object can damage Ardor.

item.name: Armor of Zombie Flesh
entries: This armor is formed from the tanned flesh of a zombie.

While wearing this armor, your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} becomes 14 + your Dexterity modifier. While attuned to this armor, when you take Necrotic damage, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to halve that Necrotic damage (round down).

item.name: Banshee Box
entries: This ivory music box is inlaid with silver decorations depicting tiny scenes of gruesome violence. If a creature holds the box for at least one minute, they can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to mentally command the box to make a sound or phrase of their choosing, lasting up to one minute. Once the lid is open, that sound or phrase continues to play on a loop until the box is closed. If the person who created the repeating sound travels more than 300 Feet away from the box, the lid closes and the music box ceases to play.

The box can create any soundscape, like haunting piano music, the sounds of a lively party, a spoken phrase, or a continuous series of screams. When the {@action Magic|XPHB} action is used to create the sounds, the user can also choose whether the sound is audible in an {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} with a 5-foot radius, a 30-foot radius, or a 120-foot radius.

item.name: Blessed Reliquary
entries: This small wooden or ivory box holds a piece of a saint. While it is on your person, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws caused by Undead creatures.
You can use each of your Channel Divinity class features one additional time.

When you use Turn Undead, you may choose for the targets to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on their saving throws. This can be used once. You regain its use after a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Bliss
entries: The magical drug comes in the form of a small, round pellet of white powder. If ingested by a Humanoid, that creature is filled with feelings of calm and happiness.
Upon ingestion, you have the following effects for {@dice 1d4} hours:

items: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.
You gain {@dice 3d10} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 15 feet.
You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}.
If you cause another creature to make a saving throw, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that save.

entries: After the effects wear off, you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, or you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

item.name: Blood Capsule
entries: This cylindrical flask is etched with arcane runes.
Humanoid blood stored in the blood capsule remains fresh for up to one year. The flask stores enough blood to sustain a vampire for one day.

item.name: Bow of Four
entries: This white wooden bow is set with four gemstones: a ruby, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

When you hold this bow, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} you can summon a magical arrow composed of one of the four elements. You must still make an attack roll to hit a creature with the magical arrow, and it does only the damage listed.

When you take the Attack action, you can attack with this summoned arrow. If you have a feature that lets you fire more than one arrow on a turn, you can fire other arrows as part of the Attack action.
Each time you summon a magical arrow, you must choose one of the four options below. If you do not fire the arrow within 1 minute of summoning it, it dissolves.

items.name: Arrow of Air
entries: On a hit, the arrow of air deals {@damage 4d8} Lightning damage.

items.name: Arrow of Earth
entries: On a hit, the arrow of earth deals {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Arrow of Fire
entries: On a hit, the arrow of fire deals {@damage 4d8} Fire damage.

items.name: Arrow of Water
entries: On a hit, the arrow of water deals {@damage 4d8} Cold damage.

item.name: Brazen Armor
entries: This armor, fashioned from the body of an Empyrean brazen bull, gleams brightly in direct sunlight. While wearing this armor, you have a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. In addition, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire and Radiant damage.

item.name: Cap of Bravery
entries: Woven from the remains of an old snarite, this cap provides the wearer with a sense of magical peace and calm that emanates from them. While wearing this cap, you radiate an aura in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. You and allies in this aura have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end effects that include the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition

item.name: Carvaloth Scale Armor
entries: This red-tinged armor made from carvaloth scales automatically shifts its color to camouflage you. When you wear this armor, Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made to perceive you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

item.name: Caustic Rapier
entries: This strange weapon is fashioned from the mandible of a hivewolf. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. When you roll a 19 or 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, that attack does an additional {@damage 2d6} Acid damage. In addition, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Acid damage while wielding the weapon.

item.name: Cinder Coat
entries: This floor-length leather duster is the color of dying embers. You have a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while wearing this armor.

The coat has 3 charges. When you hit a creature with a melee attack, and that creature is not at its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 1d10} Fire damage to the target. The coat regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Listener
entries: This is a cloak made of ears that were once part of a swarm of ears. It pulses gently and oozes yellow fluid from its many holes. While wearing this cloak, you can take an action to create one of two effects:

entries.name: Ooze
entries: You tug the cloak to create a puddle of waxy goo that covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within 30 feet of you. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}). This wax remains for 1 minute. Once this effect is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Hide
entries: You completely conceal your head with the cloak. You have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet, and you can hear all speech within 120 feet regardless of obstructions. These effects last for 1 minute.
Once this effect is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Mausgeist
entries: This fine garment is made of mausgeist fur with a silk lining. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Heightened Senses
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on hearing.

entries.name: Magic Protection
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Charm
entries: You can take an action to cast the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 13}). Once used, this property of the cloak can’t be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Stygian Bat
entries: This cloak is made from stygian bat hides and bristles with coarse black hair. While wearing the cloak, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on hearing.

In addition, you can take an action to gain a  {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet for one minute. While this effect is active, you also have blindsight with a range of 120 feet, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws while in the air. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of Therianthropy
entries: This spotted grey cloak is made of styrkvisnar hide. While wearing it, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) checks.

Additionally, you can take an action to transform into a wolf, eagle, or seal for up to 1 hour. The transformation otherwise functions as the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell, but you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to revert to your normal form. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coat of Lies
entries: This coat is made from the tongues of a harvester of lies’ victims. While you wear it, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks.

The cloak has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge while wearing the cloak to enhance the next spell you cast before the start of your next turn, allowing that spell to be cast without any Verbal or Somatic components.

item.name: Coin of War and Fear
entries: This dark metal coin is a token of heretical dogma that claims Empyreus and Beleth are one and the same. One side bears the Empyreus's icon, while the other shows Beleth's leering face.

While holding this coin, you can take an action to flip it. Roll a {@dice d6}. The icon of Empyreus shows on an even number, granting you the Boon of Empyreus. On an odd number, Beleth shows and confers the Boon of Beleth.

entries.name: Boon of Empyreus
entries: For one minute, you gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}, and you add {@dice 1d4} to the first attack roll you make each of your turns. If you would receive this boon again before the next dawn, you instead take {@damage 3d6} Radiant damage and the boon is not applied.

entries.name: Boon of Beleth
entries: For one minute, an aura radiates from you in a 10-foot radius. When an enemy ends its turn in the area, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. While the aura lasts, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage whenever you hit a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature with a weapon attack. If you would receive this boon again before the next dawn, you instead take {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage and the boon is not applied.

item.name: Coldrazor
entries: This infamous axe is wielded by the memori lich known as Runa Banasár. It appears to be made of ice, carved with runes and studded with various tiny, glittering gems.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 4d6} Cold damage. If a single attack with Coldrazor deals more than 20 points of Cold damage, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.
Immediately after hitting or missing with a ranged attack made with this weapon, it flies back to your hand.

item.name: Collar of the Undead
entries: Some people have no qualms about exploiting any opportunity for profit, including the sorrow caused by the loss of a loved one. Such people would appreciate the Collar of the Undead, whether as con artists looking for bereaved individuals to fleece, or as dark sorcerers in need of souls.

As an action, you can place this collar around the neck of a Humanoid creature of Large size or smaller that has died within the last 10 days. The creature returns to life with 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. The creature uses its normal game statistics, except that its creature type becomes Undead and it doesn't need to eat, sleep, or breathe. If its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are reduced to 0, the creature is utterly destroyed, turning into dust.

Each dawn, the creature’s {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum decreases by {@dice 1d4} as its soul slowly slips away. When the creature’s {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum reaches 0, or its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are reduced to 0, it falls apart into a heap of dust and is destroyed.

On each of your turns, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mentally command the creature if it is within 60 Feet of you, and it follows your orders to the best of your ability. If you issue no commands, the creature takes the Dodge action and moves only to avoid harm. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.
If the collar is removed, the creature is immediately reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Corrupted Tusk-Dagger
entries: This dagger is fashioned from a dragonborn vampire's fangs. When a wielder scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with the dagger, the weapon damage is {@damage 4d4} instead of {@damage 2d4}. In addition, on a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Crimson Mist
entries: This tiny bottle contains a cocktail of pheromones from creatures of prey. As an action, you spray the pheromones on one target within 5 feet, and its effect lasts for 1 minute.
Beasts or other creatures that must attack the nearest creature will target the affected individual instead, as long as that individual is within 60 feet of the attacker.

item.name: Crown of the Keppmir King
entries: This platinum crown is adorned with black gems and shells. While you wear the crown, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and can breathe underwater. Also, your Constitution is 20 while you wear the crown, unless it is 20 or higher without it.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This crown is cursed, a fact revealed only when an {@spell Identify|XPHB} spell is cast on the crown or you attune to it. Attuning to the crown curses you until you are targeted by the {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic; it is impossible to remove the crown. While cursed, you turn into a keppmir after {@dice 2d10} days.

item.name: Dreams Leaves
entries: Made from a mysterious, magically grown plant, this popular new drug induces vivid hallucinations in which the user's dreams appear to be fulfilled. Some users even see visions of branching possibilities that their life could take.

Upon consuming Dreams Leaves, you can choose to fall into a blissful, trance-like state for 1 hour, during which time you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. Taking damage ends the trance early.

If you remain in the trance for the full hour, you awaken with a faint insight into your future. Once before you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and use the new result.

Using this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} creates an addiction to Dreams Leaves. While addicted, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws. Consuming another dose of Dreams Leaves negates this effect until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
The addiction lasts until you receive a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell, or until you have not consumed Dreams Leaves for 30 days.

item.name: Ectoplasmic Whip
entries: This weapon is made of silver spirit energy that phases in and out of reality, making it extremely difficult for living creatures to perceive. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Attack rolls made with the whip against non-Undead creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

item.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of an elemental troll. For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} damage of a type corresponding to the troll whose spittle was used to craft the potion. No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.

item.name: Elixir of Life Protection
entries: After you drink this thick, chalky mixture, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum cannot be reduced for 10 minutes.

item.name: Enchanted Feather
entries: This magical feather can sometimes be found growing from a rabenhex. While holding the feather, you can take an action to cast {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB}. Afterward, the feather becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Fin Symbiote
entries: This tendril of flesh, harvested from an Aberration, melds with the body of its host. If you are a Humanoid, you can attune to this item by stitching it into your skin during the Attunement process. At the end of this time, the fin burrows into your body and bonds with your muscular system. Non-Humanoids cannot attune to the Fin Symbiote.
While attuned to the symbiote, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Symbiotic Health
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Immovable
entries: When you would be moved against your will, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to subtract 10 Feet from that movement.

entries.name: Aberrant Power
entries: If you have achieved at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}, add +2 to the DC of save DCs for your Aberrant Horror Boons.

entries.name: Symbiote Link
entries: You cannot voluntarily end your Attunement to this magic item. If it is physically removed from your body, it regrows in {@dice 1d4} hours unless you receive a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell before it grows back (which also ends your Attunement).

item.name: Faerie Crown
entries: This crown is woven of aromatic flowers. When you first don the crown after attuning to it, {@dice 1d6 + 3} butterflies begin fluttering around it.
When you are hit by a melee attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to release one of the butterflies. The attack misses you and destroys the butterfly instead.
Once all butterflies are destroyed, the crown withers and crumbles to dust.

item.name: Flammable Tincture
entries: This swirling mixture of red and white liquid is formed from the remains of a coldfire ooze. As an action, the vial of Flammable Tincture can be hurled up to 30 feet at a target, which must succeed on a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw or be covered with the liquid. For 1 minute, the creature loses its {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} or {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage and has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage instead.

item.name: Full Moon Extract
entries: This potent powder is supposedly created by grinding up the dried organs of a werewolf. Despite the risks associated with its use, it is a favored drug of many wrestlers, boxers, and soldiers because of the raw strength it provides.

Consuming this potion enhances your physical prowess for 1 hour. While the effect lasts, your Strength score becomes 19 (unless it was already 19 or higher), and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ability checks and saving throws.

Prolonged use of Full Moon Extract is risky. You can consume it safely a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1). Any further use results in painful addiction. While addicted, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws, and attack rolls against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

Consuming another dose of Full Moon Extract negates this effect until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, but you must also make a {@dc 10} Charisma save. On a failure, you become overcome by violent urges for 1 minute. During that time, you cannot take {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions} or {@variantrule reaction|XPHB|Reactions}. At the start of each of your turns, you regard the creature nearest to you that you can see or hear as your enemy. During that turn, you must move as close to the creature as possible and take the Attack action, targeting the creature. If your movement doesn't bring you within reach, you must use your Dash action to continue moving toward it.
The addiction lasts until you receive a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell, or until you have not consumed Full Moon Extract for 30 days.

item.name: Funeral Mask
entries: This delicate mask is made of thinly beaten copper that molds to the skin when worn. While wearing this mask, you can use an action to alter your appearance, including your height, weight, facial features, the sound of your voice, hair length, coloration, and distinguishing characteristics for 8 hours. You can make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your statistics change. You also can’t appear as a creature of a different size, and your basic shape stays the same; if you’re bipedal, you can’t use this item to become quadrupedal, for instance.
Creatures within 5 feet of you can use an action to make a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check to discern your true appearance.

If you have reached at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Lich|GrimHollowPG24|Lich Transformation}, you also gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while your appearance is disguised by the mask.
Once you use the mask, you cannot do so again until the following dawn.

item.name: Glass-Studded Armor
entries: While wearing this armor, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage. Whenever a creature hits or misses you with a melee attack, it takes {@damage 3} Piercing damage. An attacker can choose to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its attack roll to avoid this damage.

item.name: Gold Boar Ring
entries: The ring, made from the bristles of the gold mane boar, sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. This light shines even in areas where magic does not function, or where magical light would normally be suppressed.

item.name: Grenade of Remorse
entries: This substance, harvested from the tears of a remorseful storm, proves toxic to creatures of unnatural origins. You can take an action to hurl the grenade at a point up to 30 feet away. When it lands, the grenade explodes in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. Each Aberration, Fiend, and Undead creature within the area makes a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 8d6} Psychic damage on a failed save or half that much on a successful one.

item.name: Gristaline
entries: This gruesome mixture is made from ground mouruge bones, along with rotten meat and mud. When consumed, your Strength score becomes 19 for 1 hour (unless it is already 19 or higher). You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ability checks and saving throws for the duration.

item.name: Heaven's Herald
entries: This golden trumpet is never dulled by rust or dirt. As an action, you can blow the trumpet to create a rousing battle call that can be heard up to {@b 600 Feet} away.

When you use the trumpet, choose up to 12 willing creatures within {@b 600 Feet} to become emboldened. An emboldened creature has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws. In addition, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by {@dice 2d10} and it regains the same number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. These benefits last for 8 hours.
If you are a Fiend or Undead creature, you take {@damage 8d6} Radiant damage when you blow the trumpet.
Once you use Heaven's Herald, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Hogs' Wash
entries: This foul-tasting tea is brewed from the powdered tusks of doom boars. When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell.
Hogs' Wash can also be used as a component in removing a Shadowsteel curse (see "{@book Shadowsteel Curses|GrimHollowPG24|7|Shadowsteel Curses}").

item.name: Humble Frock
entries: While wearing this frock and not wearing any armor, {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} rolls against you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
If you have reached at least Stage 2 of the {@charoption Seraph|GrimHollowPG24|Seraph Transformation}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid revealing your true nature.

item.name: Hunter's Zeal
entries: This glowing green liquid sends a creature’s immune system into a hyperactive state. When you drink this potion, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison damage and to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. While the effect lasts, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid becoming cursed. When the potion's effect ends, you gain 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}.

item.name: Inquisitor's Draught
entries: Designed to aid in the capture and interrogation of mages, this silver syrup is also an effective method of freeing oneself from a cursed item. After drinking this potion, you are incapable of attuning to magic items for 1 hour. During this duration, you can take an action to sever all your Attunements to magic items (even cursed items). At the end of this duration, if you are attuned to any magic items, you die.

item.name: Iridescent Armor
entries: This shimmering breastplate is forged from a giant dragonfly’s carapace and adorned with a cape of gossamer fabric. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while you wear this armor.
While wearing this armor, you can take an action to transform the cape into dragonfly wings for 1 hour. While this effect lasts, you have a  {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet.

If you have reached at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Fey|GrimHollowPG24|Fey Transformation}, the armor's effect lasts for 2 hours, during which time you have a  {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet.
Once this effect is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Iron Fangs
entries: These iron tooth caps are inscribed with magical runes. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls with your Bite attacks, and your Bite attacks deal your choice of either force or Piercing damage.

item.name: Knifewing Cape
entries: This cloak is made from the skin of twenty knifewings. While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. If you fall, you descend 60 Feet per round and take no damage from falling.

item.name: Light Drinker
entries: This sword’s hilt and blade are forged from a single piece of black metal that absorbs the light around it. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals Cold damage instead of Slashing damage. While attuned to the sword, you gain darkvision to 60 feet and can see in magical darkness.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can cause the sword to emit magical darkness in a 20-foot radius, and illumination is limited to {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

While the darkness is active, you can use an action to expand or reduce its radius of darkness by 5 feet each, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 10 feet each. You can use this feature again at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Lindwyrm Venom
entries: This terrible venom is extracted and distilled from the deadly lindwyrm. It can be delivered via ingestion or a wound. A creature exposed to the venom must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 8d8} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength and Dexterity skill checks and saving throws.

item.name: Living Net
entries: This net is made of wire and thread from a suture golem. It can be used in the same manner as a normal Net, but once it restrains a target it coils and twists as if alive. Escaping the Living Net requires a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check.
The Living Net has {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} 15 and 15 HP. If destroyed, the net repairs itself after 1 minute.

item.name: Lodestone Armor
entries: This plate armor is lined with magnetic lodestone harvested from a different plane of existence. While wearing the armor, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to enhance its magnetic polarity for 1 minute. Loose stones, soil, and minerals are immediately attracted to the surface of the armor, granting you a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and reducing your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 5 feet.

Using this ability while it already active increases the {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} bonus by 1 and reduces your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by an additional 5 feet (to a maximum bonus of +4 and a maximum reduction of 20 feet). Once you reach this maximum effect, you can't activate the armor again until the duration has ended.

item.name: Lycanthropy Antidote
entries: Made with fzeg blood, this silvery potion is dotted with swirling flecks of red. Drinking this liquid removes the curse of lycanthropy.

item.name: Magebane Bomb
entries: This bubbling liquid is distilled from the remains of a magebane ooze. You can throw the container at an enemy’s weapon or armor, or at a spellcaster.

Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Acid damage. If the weapon was targeted, that weapon takes a -1 penalty to attacks and damage as well. If armor was targeted, that armor takes a -1 penalty to the {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. Both effects end after the wielder or wearer completes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
A spellcaster hit with a Magebane Bomb has a -1 penalty to hit with spell attacks, and the DCs of saving throws against spells they cast decrease by 1 for 1 minute.

item.name: Magical Prosthetics
entries: The brutal warfare and vicious monsters prevalent across Etharis leave many with Grievous Wounds, including missing limbs and other potentially debilitating injuries. The engineers and crafters of the world have made great strides in helping those people deal with those injuries through Prosthetics, which are covered in Chapter 5 of the Grim Hollow Player's Guide.

Magical versions of Prosthetics can be crafted with properties that mimic existing Wondrous Items or Rings. Typically, these Prosthetics replicate the body part that would wear that item. For example, a Magical Prosthetic leg could replicate the effect of {@item Boots of Elvenkind|XDMG}, while a Magical Prosthetic hand might act as a {@item Ring of Protection|XDMG}.

A Magical Prosthetic has the same rarity as the magic item it mimics. It counts as "wearing" an item of that kind, so if you have a Magical Prosthetic leg that mimics {@item Boots of Elvenkind|XDMG}, you can't wear another pair of magic boots.

item.name: Malleus Macabre
entries: This weapon is a blunt but jagged-edged bludgeon that is the object form of a kokela. You gain a +4 bonus to damage rolls made with this weapon, and attack rolls made with this weapon score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20. If you have abilities that increase your critical range with melee attack rolls, this weapon scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 17-20.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you spend one year at least a mile away from the weapon. While you remain cursed, you’re unwilling to part with the club, always keeping it within reach. You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with weapons other than this one, unless no foe is within 60 feet of you that you can see or hear.
Whenever you roll a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with this weapon, after dealing damage there is a 25% chance the Malleus Macabre casts {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB} at level 9 centered on you.

item.name: Mask of the Maestro
entries: Carved from yew, this mask is shaped in the likeness of a beast’s visage and has 3 charges. While wearing the mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} on yourself, with a maximum duration of 1 minute.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed. Wearing it curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. While you remain cursed, you cannot remove the mask.

item.name: Morose Monocle
entries: This opaque, grey eyepiece completely blocks normal vision for the eye wearing it. The only things you can see through it are grief eaters, and creatures experiencing significant pain, sorrow, or guilt. These creatures appear as white outlines, shining with an intensity that matches their emotional turmoil. The range of this unusual vision is 60 feet.
While wearing the monocle, you also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks made to gauge a creature’s emotions.

item.name: Mortal Coil
entries: This ring is composed of hundreds of rose gold wires woven to coil around the finger. It allows its wearer's spirit to resist the pull of the afterlife.

If you die while wearing this ring, your soul enters the ring and your body remains alive and {@variantrule Stable|XPHB} at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. When you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, your soul reenters your body and the ring turns into a useless lump of lead.

item.name: Muscle Graft
entries: Created through a gruesome process requiring the muscle tissue of a bugbear vampire, this graft melds to the flesh of a living creature who attunes to it. Your Strength increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. When you become attuned, you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution save or your Constitution decreases by 2, to a minimum of 1.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This graft is cursed. Attuning to it curses you for 1 year or until you are targeted by {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} or a similar spell. Removing the graft fails to end the curse on you, and you cannot end the {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} while the curse lasts. You develop a deep desire for rare meat and raw blood. At the end of each week, you must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or become a {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM} with no progenitor.

item.name: Net of Maiden's Hair
entries: Sewn from the plant creature known as maiden’s hair, this sweet-smelling piece of headgear bestows lingering magical properties. While wearing the net on your head, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks. Additionally, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an ability check to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Nightmare Staff
entries: This twisted staff is created from the remains of a marra. It has 3 charges and regains its expended charges daily at dusk.

When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you damages you while you hold this staff, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend {@b 1} of the staff’s charges to force the creature to make a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the creature sees a phantasm that causes it to have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. If the creature is already {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, it also takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage.

item.name: Paragon's Plate
entries: This ancient set of armor, which includes boots, greaves, a cuirass, gauntlets, and a helm, fully encases you in a shell of imposing steel, such that no part of your body is visible. While wearing this armor, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. If you have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you must still make {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}, but you can continue to move and act.
If you die while wearing this armor, you can continue to move and act within it for up to 1 minute, though you lose the ability to speak and to cast or concentrate on spells.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This armor is cursed, a fact revealed only when {@spell Identify|XPHB} is cast on the armor or you attune to it. While attuned to the armor, it is impossible to remove. Additionally, you automatically fail all {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the armor’s magic puts an immense strain on your body.

item.name: Pharazorthok Sprout
entries: This hand-sized fleshy sapling has writhing tentacles for branches and bulbous yellow eyes studding its trunk. You can take an action to plant it in the ground in your space, at which point it begins whispering telepathically to you. While the sprout is planted and within 30 feet of you, you have truesight with a range of 30 feet.

Anyone in the sprout's space can take an action to uproot it. If left in the ground for 1 minute, the sprout experiences a growth surge, uproots itself, and becomes a {@creature Roper|XMM} that is {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you and your allies.

item.name: Pigskin Armor
entries: This strong, flexible armor made from the hide of ogre swine is surprisingly supple. It is considered light armor rather than medium armor. When a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} is scored on you, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 17-20, you treat it as a hit instead.
However, while wearing the armor, you snore uncontrollably while sleeping, and your food intake doubles.

item.name: Potion of Glacial Resistance
entries: This brew is made from the ground horns of keppmirs. When consumed, this potion allows you to breathe underwater for 1 hour, during which time you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. It tastes salty and gives off a slight warmth.

item.name: Potion of Harm
entries: This concoction is the result of a failed attempt to make a {@item Potion of Heal|GrimHollowCG24}. It is indistinguishable from that potion without using divination magic of level 3 or higher.

When you imbibe this potion, roll {@dice 1d4}. That becomes your new {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total. If the Potion of Harm is given to a creature with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, they instantly suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure and begin dying if they are {@variantrule Stable|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Heal
entries: This ebon liquid is made from the remains of a downcast vampire. When you imbibe this potion, you regain all your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Life Sharing
entries: This shimmering liquid changes colors constantly within its vial. Made from the acid glands of the dreaded wolfentroika, this potion allows you to share your life force with others. After drinking this potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to choose a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see or hear. That creature regains {@dice 4d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and you take an equal amount of Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. The effect ends after 1 minute, at which point you gain 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Malice
entries: This brew is made from the spore sacs of three grippers. When consumed, this potion causes the imbiber to give off spores that anger enemies within 10 feet. Those enemies must succeed on a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw, or they must target the imbiber with their attacks for 1 minute. All weapon attacks are made with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Relentlessness
entries: This viscous potion is made from quoxxarie blood. After drinking this potion, when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} (no action required) and set your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to 1. This effect lasts for 1 minute, at the end of which you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} for each {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} spent during the potion's duration.

item.name: Profane Charm
entries: This charm is a charred feather harvested from a fallen angel, wrapped in silver wire. While you wear this charm, your creature type appears to be Humanoid for the purpose of {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB} and similar effects.

item.name: Quakescale Armor
entries: Fashioned from the scales of a quake drake, this armor rumbles softly with the sound of thunder during storms. While wearing it, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to thunder damage.

item.name: Queen's Token
entries: This humble acorn is one of the mightiest gifts a Faerie queen can bestow upon her subjects. While holding the acorn, you can cast the {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell. After using the acorn in this way, it immediately teleports back to the queen who bestowed it.

If the {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell is used in a way that the queen would perceive as a threat to herself or her realm, the spell does not work. Instead, the creature who made the wish immediately transforms into an oak tree. Only a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell cast with the Queen's Token can return a creature transformed in this way to its original state.

item.name: Ready Gunk
entries: This sticky paste is fashioned from the powdered bone armor of a bone trader. After you treat a weapon, {@item Shield|XPHB}, or other item that can be wielded in one or two hands with the paste, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to summon and equip it, as long as the item is within 30 feet of you. After using this effect three times, it loses its potency.

item.name: Resuscitation Bomb
entries: This foul-smelling concoction is made from the remains of stinking sludge. As an action, you can throw the vial up to 10 feet, where it breaks and releases a noxious vapor that fills a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. Each creature in the area with the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition ends those conditions. Each dying creature in the area has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its next {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}. Each creature that was not dying and did not have any of those conditions must instead succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Ring of Hidden Desires
entries: This rose gold band is set with an obsidian stone carved in the shape of a heart. While wearing this ring, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to attempt to learn a creature’s darkest desire. Select a creature that you can see. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you immediately learn the target’s greatest desire. On a successful save, you do not learn anything, and the creature is immune to the effects of the ring. If the creature you choose has an Intelligence of 3 or lower, the creature is unaffected. The ring’s power can be used once and recharges at midnight.

item.name: Ring of Nightmares
entries: This ring's crafting requires powdered brain from a restless lumberer. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend 1 charge to change the damage type of the next spell you cast before the end of your turn to Psychic damage.
In addition, while attuned to the ring, you’re  {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to spells or magical abilities that interfere with your sleep or cause nightmares while you sleep.

item.name: Ring of Radiation
entries: This ring is made of braided copper wire. While wearing this ring, your elemental energies are supercharged, allowing them to radiate from your body. When you cast a spell or use an ability that does Cold, Fire, or Lightning damage, the range of the spell or ability is doubled.

item.name: Ring of Veracity
entries: This golden band is stamped with images of smiling humanoid faces. While attuned to this ring, the wearer has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Search actions to make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check.
Additionally, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB} (save {@dc 15}) with the ring. The ring can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Rod of Ruin
entries: This rod is formed of stacked crystal spheres filled with smoke. The rod has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk.

When a creature you can see within 60 Feet of you damages you while you are holding this rod, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend 1 charge to force the creature to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature gains 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}. If you have the {@charoption Lich|GrimHollowPG24|Lich Transformation}, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its saving throw, and you gain {@dice 2d10} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when a creature fails the save.

item.name: Rod of the Pack Master
entries: This rod appears to be a piece of driftwood carved with images of wolves and bears. While holding the rod, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} on any number of willing Humanoids you can see within 30 Feet of you. The only forms you can choose for each target are {@creature Black Bear|XMM} and {@creature Wolf|XMM}, and the targets retain their Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. The effect lasts for 24 hours and requires no {@condition Concentration|XPHB}. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to end the effect early for any number of targets.
Once you use the rod to cast {@spell Polymorph|XPHB}, it can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
If you have reached at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Lycanthrope|GrimHollowPG24|Lycanthrope Transformation}, you can hold this rod in your teeth and use it while in hybrid or kindred form.

item.name: Sangromancer's Vial
entries: This vial of red glass and gold filigree is useful to practitioners of blood magic. As part of the Attunement process, you fill the vial with your own blood. Choose up to 10 of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and roll them, reducing your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum by an amount equal to the result. This creates a pool of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} that you can expend in place of your own when you cast a {@filter Sangromancy spell|spells|subschool=sangromancy}.

Each time you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the pool is replenished, restoring a number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} equal to half the number you rolled during {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}.
If you end your Attunement to the vial, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum returns to normal after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Second Skin
entries: This harness is woven from the hides of slain lycanthropes. When you attune to this item, the harness melds into your flesh, making it tough. Your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} is 14 + your Dexterity modifier. When you take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage from a source that is not silver, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to halve the damage (rounding down).
While you wear the harness, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks to interact with lycanthropes.

item.name: Sizzling Serum
entries: Created from the ichor in a potbelly’s stomach-pot, this volatile concoction is effective at damaging inorganic substances. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can hurl the serum at a Construct or an object that you can see within 30 Feet of you. The target makes a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 10d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Soulbond Stone
entries: This smooth and weighty stone has a dusk-colored gem embedded in its center. When pressed to the chest of a creature who has been dead for less than one minute, the stone captures a fragment of the dead creature's soul for later use.

The Soulbond Stone has 3 charges. While holding the stone, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Speak With Dead|XPHB} with the stone, as if the trapped soul's corpse was the target of the spell. When the last charge is expended, the stone shatters and the soul fragment returns to its rightful place.

item.name: Staff of Briars
entries: This wooden staff is made from the harvested claws of a braerog. It has 10 charges. While holding the staff, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge and cause thorn-covered rose vines to sprout up, covering a 20-foot square centered on a point within 5 Feet of you. This area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Once created, the briars are non-magical. They grow (or wither) as appropriate for the environment, just as a normal plant would.
The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the staff turns into rose petals and is lost forever.

item.name: Staff of the Sorcerer King
entries: This ebony staff bears a bronze spear tip and additional blades carved to depict a winged scarab. The staff can be wielded as a magic spear that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. You are considered proficient with this weapon while you are attuned to it, even if you aren’t proficient with spears. While holding the staff, you gain these benefits:

items.name: Spell Power
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

items.name: Cull Subjects
entries: Humanoids have a -2 penalty to saving throws against your spells.

items.name: Spell Shield
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells that target only you.

entries.name: Cursed
entries: This staff is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. Until the curse is broken with {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} or similar magic, you are unwilling to part with the staff, keeping it within reach at all times. In addition, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls made with weapons other than this one.

While cursed, you must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw each day at midnight. On a failed save, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by 10 unless you are Undead. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum can only be restored by a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell. If your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced to 0 while cursed, you die and immediately rise as a {@creature Wight|XMM}.

item.name: Stalk Symbiote
entries: This ocular organ, harvested from a living Aberration, melds with the skull of its host. If you are a Humanoid, you can attune to this item by stitching it into your scalp during the Attunement process. At the end of this time, the stalk burrows into your skull and bonds with your brain. Non-Humanoids cannot attune to the Stalk Symbiote.
While attuned to this item, you gain the following benefits:

items.name: Symbiotic Senses
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks, and you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls when surprised.

items.name: Aberrant Power
entries: If you have achieved at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}, you have blindsight with a range of 10 Feet.

items.name: Symbiote Link
entries: You cannot voluntarily end your Attunement to the Stalk Symbiote. If it is physically removed from your body, it regrows in {@dice 1d4} hours unless you receive a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell before it grows back (which also ends your Attunement).

item.name: Star Metal Ring
entries: This heavy steel ring is set with a chunk of shimmering star metal. While wearing this ring, your appendages are crusted with iridescent star metal. Your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}s are considered magical and deal Force damage.

item.name: Straitjacket
entries: This stained grey jacket has long arms reaching the floor and contains dozens of restraining straps. This magical jacket takes 10 minutes to don or doff. While wearing this item and not wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you add your Intelligence modifier to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. Also, you can cast spells without using Somatic or Material components that don't have a GP value listed. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}.

If you have reached at least Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}, you may roll twice whenever rolling on the {@table UnStable Form|GrimHollowPG24|UnStable Form table} and choose either result.

item.name: Suit of the Lamb
entries: This suit of comfortable woolen clothes, made from the wool of the rare and dangerous poffer, includes a shirt, trousers or a kilt, and thick socks. While wearing the suit, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and you don’t suffer effects from extreme cold weather.

item.name: Sun Shades
entries: These black leather goggles are set with cloudy glass that completely obscures the eyes of the wearer. You can take an action to don or doff these goggles. While wearing these goggles, you ignore your Sunlight Sensitivity, as well as any other ill effects you would suffer due to exposure to sunlight. The goggles also block your Darkvision, preventing you from seeing in the dark.

item.name: Talisman of Tormach
entries: The dreadful talisman is crafted from the skull at the center of a gore elemental. When you kill a creature with a melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} while you wear the talisman, you gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} for 1 minute.

While you have any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, your melee weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage on a hit.

item.name: Telltale Heart
entries: This cherry-sized bloodstone heart is the beating center of a poppet. While the stone is on your person, when you gain the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

Also, when you make an Intelligence ability check while holding the stone, you can listen to it, gaining unusual insight or access to esoteric lore. If you do so, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that Intelligence check. The Telltale Heart can’t be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Thought Talker Orbs
entries: This magical spying device, designed by gnome engineers on behalf of the Ebon Syndicate, is composed of two crystal orbs. The Emitter Orb is marble-sized, making it easy to slip into your target's purse or pocket. The Receiver Orb is fist-sized.

You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and touch the two orbs together to activate them for 7 days. Once activated, the Emitter Orb begins capturing thoughts within a 5-foot {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on it. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there makes a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw. On a success, that creature is immune to the effect for 24 hours. On a failed save, the holder of the Received Orb becomes immediately aware of that creature's surface thoughts for 1 minute, even if it leaves the area.

item.name: Thricecall Mirror
entries: This ornate hand mirror, made of carved iron and adorned with images of grasping hands, can be used to contact other planes. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can speak the true name of an extraplanar being, such as a Fey, Fiend, or Celestial, into the mirror three times. This summons the named creature in an unoccupied space within 5 Feet of the mirror.

A creature summoned this way retains its free will, and there is no guarantee it views you as an ally. It cannot leave this plane for 1 minute, after which time it automatically returns to its home plane unless it has the means to travel between planes on its own, in which case it can choose to stay.

item.name: Toadstool Toddy
