From beneath the earth, like blood swelling to the surface of a wound, tides of undead monstrosities charged from the fissures to devour the living.

Nov Ostoya was the epicenter of this catastrophe. Beneath the city, a vast necropolis was revealed by the earthquakes. It teemed with undead like maggots through flesh. It pulsed with dark energy like a heartbeat beginning to awaken.

Acting decisively, the aristocracy of Nov Ostoya sent troops to investigate the necropolis. Panic led the leaders to order the rifts collapsed using magic, sealing their own troops beneath the earth. The only thing that this act sealed would be the fate of Ostoya.

entries.name: The Tragedy of Morgen Hund
entries: Morgen Hund was a true champion of Ostoya. Born to traveling merchants, Hund was raised on the road. He traveled Ostoya with his parents, visiting every province and learning about its people. As he grew older, he learned to fight defending his family's caravan from thieves and monsters.

When war broke out with the Bürach Empire, Morgen enlisted as a loyal patriot. As the war dragged on, he proved his mettle and skill, slaying Büracher knights and mages while fellow soldiers died around him. Thrust into a position of leadership, Hund's valor and hatred for the invaders made him popular among the Ostoyan troops.

After {@area Fallowheart|07c|x} was captured by the Bürach Empire, Hund was called back to Nov Ostoya as a military envoy to answer to the Grand Duke about the state of the war. Estspine had been lost and Büracher soldiers were advancing into {@area Terinima|068|x} and north to {@area Raevo|066|x}. The Ostoyan Empire was slipping away from its people.

During Hund's stay in the capital, the earth shook and the sun disappeared. And Morgen Hund was chosen to lead soldiers down into the necropolis that had been revealed from the darkness of the fissures. His orders were to slay the undead. He was to find the source of the scourge and end it. With a battalion at his back, Hund descended beneath Ostoya. Neither he, nor his soldiers, ever returned alive.
After days of waiting and deathless ghouls continuing to emerge from the fissures, the Grand Duke made the decision to collapse the rifts. Arcane explosions buried the necropolis once more.

entries.name: The Sanguine Curse
entries: Morgen Hund found something in the darkness beneath Ostoya. Something ancient. Something that had slept for untold centuries before, awoken by the Darkfall. It changed Hund, transforming him into an undying and fearless monster. A monster that hungered.

Thought dead for weeks, miraculously Hund returned to Nov Ostoya, starving and furious. Betrayed by the Grand Duke, Hund began his slaughter in the capital by decapitating the leadership of the Empire. He then seized command of the military and turned his ire to the invading Bürach armies, driving them back across the Grey Spine over the course of several bloody years.

But Morgen Hund was not alone in clawing himself out of the darkness. A horde of vampires rose with him, blood starved after centuries of being buried beneath Etharis. Freed at last, these undead horrors aided Hund in his crusade for vengeance, and in return he gave them that which he had valued most.
The sovereignty of Ostoya.

entries.name: The Crimson Court
entries: The Ostoyan Empire is under the tyrannical rule of a cabal of vampires, extremely powerful in ability and personality, known as the Crimson Court.

Initially they were led by Hund, who reinvented himself as Klaus Vorgen the Betrayed. In the years since, nobody is certain what happened to Vorgen. Some say he was killed by another member of the Court; others claim he's buried himself in the {@area City Below|073|x}. A few believe he's still alive, somewhere in Ostoya, grieving over what he has wrought and hoping for a chance at redemption.

The Crimson Court is composed of seven ruling nobles who, through a tenuous alliance, rule over every aspect of Ostoyan life. While they are allies, no true loyalty exists among them. Each noble vies to sit at the head of the table, employing schemes that can take decades to bear fruit. Yet they're all aware their bloody grip on Ostoya only remains strong through shared strength.
Currently, the Court is ruled by Grand Duke Drago Koshevek. The remaining six members each rule over a province of the Ostoyan Empire, assigned to them by the Grand Duke.

entries.name: Vampiric Bloodlines
entries: The sanguine curse is ancient. Through the uncounted centuries its manifestations have divided into vampiric bloodlines, which vary greatly in appearance, abilities, and attitudes. The bloodline a vampire belongs to may be determined by their mortal heritage, as described in the {@i {@book Grim Hollow Monster Grimoire|GHMG|2|Vampire Ancestries}}, or it may reflect the nature of their sire. The following are a few of the bloodlines established in Etharis:

entries.name: Soman
entries: Soman vampires claim to be divine beings destined to rule over, and be served by, the mortal humanoids of Etharis. They believe the sanguine curse began from the blood of a from a death god, making them its immortal seraphs. Whether they truly are the descendants of such a being is a question for philosophers and monster hunters.

Soman vampires tend to dress lavishly and drink blood from wine chalices. They bend mortals to their whims through manipulation while playacting at benevolent sovereigns. Their countenance is a balance of grace and power that is often lacking in other bloodlines, enabling them to mask themselves as humanoids more easily than most of their undead kindred.

entries.name: Upyr
entries: Upyr believe they are the first and most ancient bloodline, having long abandoned their humanity along with any pretense of appearing mortal. Their crooked frames are the very image of a living corpse. Their limbs are stretched and emaciated. Their eyes are pale and bloodless. Upyr rest in coffins filled with the dirt of their homeland, feeling a deep connection to the shadowy earth of Ostoya. In truth, few have ventured outside the {@area City Below|073|x}.

For upyr there are no machinations. No intrigues. No masquerade of nobility. There is only the thirst. They shepherd mortals like a flock but do not care if they're worshipped or feared. Death is the only god left, and upyr are its seraphs.

entries.name: Fzeg
entries: An unholy biological alliance, the curse of lycanthropy and the gift of vampirism combine to become what is known as a fzeg. The ferocious strength of werewolves pairs horrifically with the ceaseless bloodlust of vampires. Easily the most physically deadly of the bloodlines due to their cleaving claws and bloodsucking bite, fzeg have no trouble hunting; their difficulties lie in stopping.

entries.name: Strigoi
entries: Strigoi are pallid, gangly, and utterly wretched creatures. Their eyes bulge from narrow features above a grin of pointed fangs. They're unrivalled as nighttime predators, stalking their prey from above by crawling upside down in cavern ceilings or beneath bridges. Strigoi feed on blood, flesh, and bone, making mutilated corpses of their victims.

Despite their hideous appearance, strigoi maintain their mortal intelligence. They will bargain with and manipulate mortals with the same cunning as other bloodlines, yet strigoi have far less patience for pretending to be courtly. Many of their progeny hunt freely through the cities of Ostoya to slake their thirst.

entries.name: Ecoukrylo
entries: More batlike than humanoid, ecoukrylo resemble chiropteran monsters more than nobles. Their sensitive ears and leaf shaped noses enable them to hunt prey in pitch blackness and over great distances, swooping down on leather wings in the midnight of Ostoya. Many who witness this horror swiftly forget that an ecoukrylo was once a mortal.

Vampires of this bloodline tend to adopt strange behaviors such as hording trinkets, shrilling as they feed, or spending a disturbing amount of time grooming themselves. Although quirky, ecoukrylo are devious shapeshifters. Many disguise their terrifying true form within a noble masquerade.

entries.name: Provinces
entries: Ostoya is divided into six provinces. Each of the six lesser members of the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x}, as overseen by the Grand Duke, is given autonomy over one of the regions. The rulership of a province is seen as both an honor and a burden, and each member of the Court treats their leadership differently. One thing is true regardless: failure to keep a firm grip on a province in a sign of weakness, and any show of weakness is an invitation to be removed from the Crimson Court—and possibly from Etharis entirely.

entries.name: Estspine
entries: Home to the first Ostoyans, Estspine was the province that gave birth to an empire. Its soil is stained with the blood of sacrifice and vengeance. From here the migrants spread to escape from Büracher oppression, and here they have rallied each time to defend their independence.

Estspine is under immense political and military pressure. The province is trapped between the spears of the Bürach Empire to its west, the blood-hungering aristocrats of the Crimson Court to its south, and the agitators of the Raevo Rebellion to its north. The simple folk of Estspine are in disagreement over who should rightfully lead them.

The province is a gateway to the Ostoyan Empire from the west, and therefore a powerful symbol to the Ostoyan people of resistance, independence, and pride. Above the river that flows from the Grey Spine looms the fortified city of {@area Fallowheart|07c|x}, which has been used as bastion to hold the Bürach Empire at bay for generations. By ruling over Fallowheart, the Crimson Court legitimizes their reign of Ostoya.

{@area Raevo|066|x} aims to liberate Fallowheart from the vampires' murderous grip. Armies from both sides of the conflict camp at the city walls like two wolves snarling over a carcass. Yet both Raevo and {@area Soma|06e|x} recognize that to weaken themselves in a struggle over Fallowheart would create opportunity for a Bürach invasion to seize the city. Therefore, a tense standoff endures.

The wider Estspine economy is fed by trade. The region exports lumber, produce, and minerals across Ostoya via the Empire's arterial rivers. Estspine also benefits from neighboring the Bürach Empire, from where notably daring merchants brave the Elian Pass to bring foreign wares. Stehlenwald black powder is of particular interest, in exchange for Soman wines and Raevan talismans.

entries.name: Notable Individuals

items.name: Viscount Langkirk Warlow
items.entry: By far the most obsessive and pitiable member of the Crimson Court, Viscount Warlow didn't earn his position. He was raised to it by the Grand Duke Koshevek. Whispers in Nov Ostoya say the Grand Duke deliberately chose a weak ruler for Fallowheart. One Koshevek could easily intimidate and manipulate. Other rumors claim the viscount is expected to fail at his task of overseeing Fallowheart, which the Grand Duke can use as a pretense to destroy Warlow for some past insult.

entries: Viscount Warlow is an ugly and insecure little man. The strigoi plays the part of noble badly, pretending to hold courtly dinners while horrifically devouring dismembered limbs in front of his subjects. Yet anyone who would dare comment on Warlow's bad decorum or the rumors about his weak position within the Court are likely to end up on his dinner plate.

items.name: Castellan Gavril Bosković
items.entry: A stoic knight from Castalore, Lord Bosković marched his army south from Raevo with a mandate to liberate Fallowheart from the Crimson Court. However, the civilian lives within the city have stayed his hand from ordering an attack.

entries: Instead, Lord Bosković has camped his army on the northern bank of the Arteriyan River while his agents quietly ferment rebellion within Fallowheart. If a civilian uprising were to occur, the Castellan is ready with soldiers to support them.

entries.name: Raevo
entries: Some say Raevo was destined for rebellion. The province was founded when a young diviner, Lissandra, dreamed of a shadow stretching from Nov Ostoya. She witnessed in her dreams the growing darkness slowly consuming the realm, until she followed her visions to the high cliffs of the north. Here, she saw a light. The place she stopped became the fortified city of Castalore.

The people of this province, Raevans, exhibit the same spirit of resistance and independence that began Ostoya. The commoners loath the undead with righteous passion. They consider any of their countryfolk who serve or aid the Crimson Court as traitors and cowards.

Since its secession from the Ostoyan Empire, Raevo has thrived under their governing magocracy. They have become the epicenter of unrestrained magical study, spell craft, and artifice. Magics thought too dangerous to explore in the Bürach Empire or Castinella are the very strength Raevo uses to resist the preternatural power of the Crimson Court.

Raevo has its fair share of difficulties, however. Aside from the frequent snowfall in winter and punishing summer rains, Raevo harvests are no better than the rest of Ostoya for the lack of sunlight. The latent necromancy of the realm makes the dead restless, leading to funerary rights that include decapitation and other desecrations of the dearly deceased. From the north, Valikan raids are seasonal and devastating, taking what little food and resources the smaller settlements have managed to scrounge together. Undead nightmares come hunting from the south, the Crimson Court exacting its blood tax one way or another.

Castalore is the largest settlement in Raevo and home to the governing magocracy. Designed as a fortress first and a settlement second, the impregnable city rests on a mighty cliff with massive stone walls and layered districts that give it the shape of a coiling python. Higher up the spiral are the more affluent neighborhoods, further from the mud and soldiers of the outer battlements. At the very top sits the Nightingale Tower, the magocracy's building of governance. The city has the distinction of never being occupied by either the Bürach Empire or the Crimson Court.

Despite its unified purpose, Castalore is not without its divisions or intrigues. Commoners will never fully trust the secrets of mages, and therefore the loyalty of the people is shared between the magocracy, the Order of Dawn, and even the druids of the {@area Solar Grove|072|x}. While outwardly a coalition, the ideals and spirituality of the Order of Dawn and Solar Grove can be a hindrance to the magocracy's pragmatic rulership.

entries.name: Notable Individuals

items.name: Viscountess Nebüma Zeilgor
items.entry: Brilliant and ever inquisitive, Viscountess Zeilgor replaced Virgilia Petinscu as the overseer of Raevo. Her predecessor was destroyed by Drago Koshevek after Zeilgor informed the Grand Duke of Petinscu's failures to bring Raevo to heel.

entries: The new viscountess is aware that her appointment, and her life, will be brief unless she can show progress in bringing Raevo back into the fold. As such, she and her strigoi progeny slink north of the {@area Roiling River|09f|x} to sow discord and weaken Raevo from the inside. A shadow sorceress and a spy, Zeilgor is unafraid to bloody her own taloned hands.

When in Nov Ostoya, the viscountess advises the Grand Duke on matters of religion, history, and all things arcane. It's rumored that her ultimate desire is access to the {@area Ravencourt Sanctuary|071|x}, making her quest to capture Raevo a personal ambition.

items.name: Mina Taliesin, the Lady of Masks
items.entry: The Raevo Republic is governed by the Council of Mages, who are self-elected from among their peers. Lady Mina Taliesin has been First Minister of the Council for decades, successfully defending her rule against regional strife, political coups, and assassination attempts. Her expansive spy network keeps her informed of all potential dangers to her government. Some suspect that she even has a highly placed contact within {@area Soma|06e|x} itself, allowing her to monitor and sabotage the efforts of the Crimson Court.

entries: Yet for all her years in public office, Lady Taliesin remains a mystery to her own people. At all times she conceals her face behind a decorative mask. This has earned her the moniker as the Lady of Masks, of which she seemingly has many to wear on different occasions. Most believe she does so to hide the scars of an assassination attempt. Yet her enigmatic history, and close ties to the Ravencourt Sanctuary, have led to wilder conspiracy theories.

What isn't in doubt is that Lady Taliesin seeks to destroy the vampire aristocracy and its control over Ostoya. But she schemes cautiously. Lady Taliesin does not wish to trade the bloodthirst of the Crimson Court for the conquest by the Bürach Empire.

items.name: General Artemios Dulka
items.entry: Leader to the Order of Dawn, Artemios Dulka has been fighting against the vampires of Ostoya since he was a boy. Still as zealous in his cause as when he was a young knight, years of battle have brought age and injury, but also insight into his enemy. General Dulka fights less in battles but trains new recruits personally, drilling into them the same passion that burns within himself.

entries: General Dulka was born a Soman noble before {@area The Darkfall|080|x}. His father was killed in Klaus Vorgen's cleansing of the nobility, which forced Dulka to abdicate his birthright and flee north to escape the vampires' jaws. Though that dream dwindles with age, General Dulka's ambition has always been to liberate the land that had once been his.

entries.name: Terinima
entries: Terinima is the largest and central province of the Ostoyan Empire, fed by the Great Arteriyan River which nourished its boundless horizons into arable farmland. Terinima is extremely valuable to the Crimson Court. Thanks to a large population of farmers, ranchers, hunters, and fishermen, the province is the breadbasket for the rest of Ostoya. It provides all the feed necessary for the vampires to maintain their own livestock—humanoids.

Terinimans are people of the soil. They have few ambitions besides keeping their families safe and fed. The lack of direct sunlight makes tending to sun-seeking crops difficult, but the residents of the province work with what they have. They're honest, blunt, and sturdy, just looking to get by one day at a time. They tend to keep to their own small communities, rarely travel, happy to sell their goods to the same merchant for the same pittance every month.

Those simple needs make Terinimans simple to rule. Their lack of ambition makes them unlikely to resist, and they're more inclined to report dissenters to the Crimson Court if it means their own families are spared any cruelty. Distrustful of outsiders but loyal to each other, Terinimans band together to deal with community hardships such as banditry or prowling beasts.

Settlements in Terinima are small, but properties are large. The exception is Crowsbend, which is an ever-growing town of craftsmen, merchants and smugglers. The one place most locals are fond of traveling to, Crowsbend is a hub of interprovincial trade, gossip, and entertainment. It's a muddy town of ever-expanding streets. At its center is the riverside market, which is always clamoring with local farmers, whining livestock, tinkering artisans, miserly traders, and traveling bards who sing of distant sunlit realms.

entries.name: Notable Individuals

items.name: Countess Augustine Vasinesca
items.entry: Surprisingly for an ecoukrylo, Countess Vasinesca is among the most charming of the Court. That doesn't mean she isn't eccentric. Known by noble and commoner alike as the "Blood Sommelier," Vasinesca's keen smell makes her excellent at sniffing out vessels with the most peculiar or decadent tasting blood. Her Teriniman subjects fear to be brought before her. Once Vasinesca detects a new scent, she insists on adding them to her stills.

entries: As the overseer of Terinima, Countess Vasinesca advises the Grand Duke on issues of food production and supply in Ostoya. But her true value to the court is finding the most delightful blends of blood to please even the most demanding vampiric palette. This talent has made her popular among her fellow sovereigns, even if they find her compulsive and chittering behavior uncouth.
Oh yes. I like this one. Bring him with us.
entries.by: Countess Augustine Vasinesca, on a Walk Through Town

entries.name: Bezcodru
entries: The borders of Bezcodru start and end at the tree line of The Stillborn Forest. The province is known for primarily providing two things: lumber and ore. The woods are ample and the mines run deep. The Tithewater River flows through the province and into the depths of the Glasswater Lake, both of which are critical for transporting the resources of Bezcodru elsewhere in the Empire. The waters, the mountains, and the woods all make Bezcodru an enviable fiefdom. But they are all also home to fearsome beasts and deathless monsters.

Bezcodrans are coarse. Blunt. Always on guard. The sunless sky of Ostoya often remains out of sight underneath the suffocating boughs of the Stillborn Forest, and anyone, or anything, could be lurking among the rustling bush. Bezcodrans work, eat, sleep, and rarely leave the safety of their homes.

Lunsk is the most noteworthy settlement of Bezcodru, built into the southern bank of the Glasswater Lake where it meets the Lower Tithewater. Lunsk is a port town. But it has none of the mercantile bustle that usually crowds such settlements. At its edges are large bonfires patrolled quietly by guards, each with their backs to the flame and their eyes trained on the woods. The villagers dim their lights using red-tinted glass in their lanterns and windows, trying go as unnoticed by the denizens of the Stillborn Forest as possible.

Mercenaries and monster hunters can make a tidy living in the Stillborn Forest, escorting folk for whom travel is an unfortunately necessary part of life. However, the profession is a perilous one. Undead spirits drift between the trees, trying to drag others to join them in their graves. Flesh hungry ghouls follow the scent of warm blood. Deadliest of all are the hunting packs of fzeg, the Baron's own fledglings, who are permitted to hunt freely in his realm.

entries.name: Notable Individuals
entries: {@b Baron Mihael, The Wolf}. Loyalty and honor are foreign concepts to the members of the Crimson Court. But Baron Mihael—a fzeg feared by his enemies as the "The Wolf"—comes closest to capturing those virtues. Prone towards the habits of lycanthropy, the Baron rules through violence more than any other Court member. But he believes in the strength of the pack and rewards his loyal servants richly.

Baron Mihael is a fearsome and cunning warlord. The Grand Duke favors him greatly, much to the jealousy and discomfort of the other vampire nobles. The Wolf has ended the lives of many would-be usurpers to the Grand Duke's title. He's razed villages sheltering rebel sympathizers and slaughtered vampire hunters who've dared travel south of {@area Raevo|066|x}. All of Ostoya fears the Baron greatly.

For his loyalty, and to keep the great wolf's appetite and ambition sated, Mihael has been given the rich barony of Bezcodru. He advises the Grand Duke on issues of resource production and infrastructure development. He's built roads across the realm, ensuring the swift deployment of troops to respond to Raevan rebels or Büracher soldiers. While Mihael does not lair within a castle himself, he ensures his fellow nobles have the materials needed to maintain the defenses of their own.

When not required elsewhere, Baron Mihael disappears into the Stillborn Forest. His fzeglaich retinue travels unseen between the trees, falling upon caravans or villages whenever The Wolf decides to collect his share of the blood tax.

entries.name: Umbroi
entries: Mud, muck, and misery. While Umbroi is the official name of the province, most call it the Black Mire. It's a dark, dank hellhole that swallows anything that wanders within. The ground is wet and thick. The air hangs heavy, reeking of decay. The trees are stooped into twisted shapes, and the ceaseless droning of insects whittles at the sanity of outsiders who choose to visit.

Umbroi is thick with insidious plants that creep from beneath the bog to drag victims below, while toxic fungi breathe their spores into the air. The thick peat mummifies humanoid remains. Even before {@area the Darkfall|080|x}, the Black Mire was renowned for its tales of hungry corpses clawing their way from the mud. Glowing spirits try luring wayfarers deeper into the brackish waters. Folk claim to have watched hag covens sing terrible songs over bubbling cauldrons filled with poisonous ingredients.
Only fools enter the Black Mire. Few can imagine why folk make their homes here.

Umbroians are quiet, suspicious, and prefer mumbling to themselves rather than holding conversations. They survive by remaining hidden, using secret water channels to move quietly between tiny settlements. Outsiders find it difficult to navigate the bog and are destined to meet a dismal end without aid from the locals. But the folk of the Black Mire don't give out their secrets, easily. The marshland conceals them even from the power of the Crimson Court, and when escorting travelers, they may intentionally take longer routes so the quickest and safest roads remain concealed only to them. Coin holds little value in the marsh. Umbroians trade only in food, tools, or favors.

The folk of the Black Mire share a morbid sense of humor. They s****** at outsiders' fear of the bog. Sometimes they laugh heartily when a victim is pulled screaming into the water by some fell beast or bog monster—even when it's one of their own. Rumors circulate that a cult of {@deity Beleth|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, the Arch Daemon of fear, is growing among the Umbroians. If so, choosing an appropriate guide through the area may be perilous. Such folk revel in the suffering of others.

The largest settlement in Umbroi is Riven, on the southern edge of the Black Mire. The town's size and shape is always shifting, as its stilted structures constantly sink into the earth and new ones are built to replace them. These waves of reconstruction cause the town to slide across the map, ever so slightly. People who travel to and from Riven infrequently must usually navigate a completely different layout each visit.

entries.name: Notable Individuals

items.name: Marchioness Iulia Cutov
items.entry: Quiet and ruthless, fools would assume the Marchioness Iulia Cutov was the weakest member of the Crimson Court. The Marchioness is more than happy to play this role whilst she bides her time. She has the heart of a schemer, and if she has her way, she will bring the entire Crimson Court and all of Ostoya to their knees before her.

entries: The Marchioness spends very little time in the Black Mire. Rulership of the province was given to her as a deliberate insult. Aside from exporting peat there's very little value to be extracted. Iulia Cutov's true role is patrolling the coastline with a fleet she commands from Nov Ostoya and stopping Raevan spies using the Black Mire as a hidden avenue into the rest of Ostoya. What the veil of Umbroi has actually given her is the ability to speak to {@area Raevo|066|x} without the knowledge of the rest of the Crimson Court.

Cutov advises the Grand Duke on issues of naval power and seabound trade. She keeps Valikan reavers from reaching Ostoya's southern coasts and ensures Morencian merchants can find passage through Castinellan blockades. But what no one knows—not even Countess Vulpescu's spy rings—is that Marchioness Cutov holds secret talks with members of the Raevan rebellion and other nations, whispering promises of land and access to Ostoya's riches if they help her defeat the other members of the Crimson Court. She desires sole leadership of the Ostoyan Empire. Whether her plan comes to fruition is precarious in its infancy, but the pieces are in place.

entries.name: Soma
entries: Soma is the seat of power for the Ostoyan Empire, holding the majority of its wealth. The region enjoys open plains of farmland, scattered woods, and a strong coastal border. Soma's defining landmark is Nov Ostoya, the City of Gargoyles. Built above the Black Bay, wealth flows through Nov Ostoya from the rest of Etharis in exchange for Ostoya's resources. The city is like a gaping maw draining the blood from its own provinces. Its streets are divided into two districts. The dangerous and dismal Low Streets are infested with gang members and bliss addicts, while the Crimson Heights are the decadent neighborhoods of the vampire elite.

Nov Ostoya is still scarred by the fissures that opened during the Darkfall. Beneath lies the {@area City Below|073|x}, also known as the Necropolis. Although authorities try to keep the entrances to the underground ruins sealed, minor earthquakes and curious explorers unseal them frequently.

These breaks in the surface of Ostoya extend to other regions of Soma, as well. Ghouls and cursed creatures continue to crawl out to this day. Many of these chasms have been gaping ajar for years, but on occasion new ones' crumble open to reveal fresh horrors. Soma is patrolled by the armies of the Crimson Court like hounds protecting a flock. But folk are often as afeared of their leaders as they are of the monsters beneath the earth.

Somans range from the most affluent of aristocrats to the desperate and destitute. They tend to be cutthroat, both politically and literally. Gangs form within the lower circles of society while the powerful trade lives with the nobility to keep fangs out of their own throats. Trust is difficult to earn and easy to lose. In Soma, you watch your back and cover your neck. Friends, with or without fangs, can be just as dangerous as the monsters outside the walls.

entries.name: Notable Individuals

items.name: Grand Duke Drago Koshevek
items.entry: The most powerful and ancient member of the Crimson Court, the Grand Duke Drago Koshevek cuts an unflinching and intimidating frame. The soman vampire could be carved from marble. He is cold, humorless, and considered cruel even by his fellow court members.

entries: It's unknown why the vampires of Ostoya slept in the Necropolis for untold centuries, only to emerge during the {@area Darkfall|080|x}. What is certain is the Grand Duke has existed long before the first migrants crossed the Grey Spine. When {@area Morgen Hund|05a|x} returned to the surface of Ostoya, Drago Koshevek stood behind him. Koshevek remade Hund as the vampire Vorgen Klaus. He unleashed the vengeful fledgling upon Ostoya's mortal leaders. And soon after, Vorgen Klaus was never seen again.

All fear to be beneath the piercing glare of Drago Koshevek. His appetite for power and blood is ceaseless. He has ended the lives of loyal servants simply to quench his thirst. Yet his malice is not without calculation, rewarding those who reinforce his position whilst crushing dissent at its mere whisper.

Koshevek is aware of his own limitations. Though he has marched at the head of many war hosts, he's avoided leading an invasion of {@area Raevo|066|x}. It's not the Order of Dawn he fears. The vampire lord hates mortal magic and detests the acolytes of the {@area Ravencourt Sanctuary|071|x}, with which he perhaps shares a primordial history.
Every deed Dragon Koshevek has committed since waking has appeared deliberate. One would be forgiven for thinking the Grand Duke has been planning his ascension for millennia.

items.name: Countess Katalina Vulpescu
items.entry: Blood, wealth, influence, strength, magic: all of these coalesce in the countess Katalina Vulpescu. Alluring and flamboyant, the Countess is the exact opposite of the Grand Duke. She is infamous for hosting lavish and debauched parties at her estate near Voyd, where she plays the part of glamorous and humble patron. All of this, of course, masks her true power: secrets.

entries: While she puts on an affable and conciliatory façade, Vulpescu is just as heartless and savage as her peers. She merely avoids getting dirt upon herself. Her network of spies, informants, and brutes rules a good portion of Nov Ostoya and the surrounding Soma, extending like tendrils into the rest of Ostoya and beyond.

Many believe that she knows secrets about the Grand Duke himself, which she wields as a blade to Koshevek's ribs whilst the two outwardly appear affectionate. The rest of the Court know to be wary of Vulpescu. She has the Grand Duke's ear, advising him on issues of national intelligence and foreign relations.

entries.name: Other Locations of Interest
entries: The following are other locations of interest in Ostoya.

entries.name: The Ravencourt Sanctuary
entries: Miles from the Raevan shore the horizon is obscured by a blanket of fog called the Mistwall. Those who, by accident or design, sail into the fog end up hopelessly lost. The Mistwall conceals an island that is marked on very few maps but is well known to the sages of Castalore. It is home to one of the most secretive and protected places in Etharis; the Ravencourt Sanctuary.

In Ostoya's distant past, the seer Lissandra came to the shores of {@area Raevo|066|x} and discovered a derelict boat. Aboard she found a wax-sealed envelope and inside were written ten names, including her own. Assembling her crew of nine others, Lissandra took the boat and sailed into the Mistwall, believed to have perished until they returned seven years later. Except Lissandra was no longer with the group. And each of the others had changed.

Power is what the Ravencourt Sanctuary offers. Every seven years the boat returns with nine mages of various disciplines, and ten new pupils depart with their names written in the wax-sealed envelope delivered by their forebears. To be chosen is a great honor. Apprentices vie for favor with the magocracy in Castalore hoping for their name to be written within the letter. Some even begin preliminary studies in preparation.
Nevertheless, when the boat returns to harbor on the Raevo coastline and the names are read, all are inexorably in attendance among the crowd. Yet never are all ten names expecting to be called.

Regardless of expectation, all ten depart for the Mistwall, but only nine of them return every seven years. The cycle has been unbroken since Lissandra. What isn't known is what happens to that tenth student. Are they sacrificed to the mysterious Keeper, who ministers the Ravencourt Sanctuary? Do they remain on the island as servants or teachers? Are they fed to the seas to maintain the Mistwall? None but the Keeper know, and they hold their secrets close.

Of the sanctuary itself, the wizards who've returned describe a crumbling castle built above shoreline cliffs. A ceaseless storm envelops the castle. Flashes of lightning are a constant warning before the crack of thunder. Debris from the castle drifts through the air, headless of the wind and rain, including entire towers which orbit the central keep like an orrery.

Past graduates describe the interior of the Ravencourt Sanctuary in a way that sounds dreamlike. The doors don't always open into rooms that make physical sense. The end of a small hallway may open into a resplendent library. A repository of arcane knowledge unseen anywhere else in Etharis. The same door the next day leads into a scorch-marked laboratory, the monstrosities from a previous experiment still lurking in the dark corners.

Lodgings are damp, and a chill wind howls through the corridors. The only fire to be had is conjured with magic—a first lesson for new apprentices. The students must survive the Ravencourt Sanctuary before they can learn from it.

Despite the perilous education, the Sanctuary is the only place in Etharis where magic is taught entirely without restraint. Students read ancient tomes forbidden in Erlefurt or Tol Leyemil. They commune with spirits and conjure daemons for interrogation, permitted to make pacts if the terms are agreeable to the Keeper. Spells deemed offensive to the Arch Seraphs are practiced openly. The secrets of the cosmos are laid bare before the apprentices. Those who cannot endure such trials return to Raevo scarred by their experiences, rather than empowered by them.
All of this occurs beneath the watchful eyes of the Keeper. Although this mysterious entity can make themself appear human, the Keeper is certainly not. At least not anymore.

The Keeper acts as a guide for students within the Ravencourt Sanctuary more than a teacher. They whisper to their students from the quiet darkness, encouraging each pupil to follow their deepest ambitions. Knowledge is what the Keeper provides. How it's wielded is in the hands of each apprentice.

The Keeper is omnipresent within the grounds of the Sanctuary, often appearing in more than one place at a time. Sometimes the Keeper is content to take the shape of a raven with tattered feathers, patiently watching their pupils practice spells. Other times the Keeper will adopt the form of a loved one or rival from a student's past, or a shape emblematic of a student's greatest fear. Regardless, the eyes of the Keeper are always the same—rapturous and deep, their pupils a subtly swirling cosmic void.

Nobody is certain of the Keeper's intent. Conspiracists claim the Keeper is the head of the Thaumaturge, a secretive cabal of mages most people don't believe exists. Others ponder if the Keeper is the Ravencourt Sanctuary itself—a place where magic coalesces and calls out to those able to grasp it.

entries.name: The Solar Grove
entries: Deep within the forests of western Raevo, the Solar Grove has remained hidden from the prying eyes of the vampire nobility. Though the sun may be absent from the Ostoyan sky, the druids of the Solar Grove can still feel its warmth on their skin. The folk of the grove are few. But their hope has never dwindled, despite the enduring darkness.

At the center of the grove grows the Solarwood, a magnificent tree with silver bark and burnished gold leaves. Radiant light showers down beneath its boughs. The grass and flowers that grow among its roots burst with life and color. The druids seed and harvest plants within the tree's glow, grateful that such a beacon shines for them in Ostoya's eternal night.

The Solarwood was cultivated by Sylina Florian, an archdruid old enough to remember when the true sun shone through the trees of her forest. Well read in her youngest years, Sylina heeded the ancient writings of Lissandra and anticipated the coming darkness. She performed repeated rituals to draw upon the power of the sun and imbued it within a sapling. By the time of {@area The Darkfall|080|x}, the Solarwood had already towered above its neighboring trees.

The Solar Grove is hallowed ground. Undead cannot come within the light of the tree and vampires are burned by its radiance. But what has kept the grove safe is secrecy. If the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x} learned of the Solarwood's existence, they would no doubt stop at nothing to destroy it. This has made Sylina and her druidic circle reluctant to join {@area Raevo|066|x} in its rebellion.

Both Mina Taliesin and General Dulka are aware of the grove. The General envisions another purpose for the Solarwood than letting it sit idle deep in the forest. Its bark is exceptionally durable, able to deflect an axe blow and only malleable to druidic woodcarvers. What's more, the wood is warm to the touch while it emits its radiant glow. Surely it's deadly to a vampire. Would that it could be felled for timber and turned into a thousand stakes or spears to equip an army.

Sylina Florian is horrified by such a suggestion. She refuses to sacrifice the soul hope of her druidic circle. Nevertheless, she's provided a handful of smaller branches to be crafted for Dulka's purpose. Solarwood Weapons are therefore extremely rare, usually carried by veteran knights within the Order of Dawn.

entries.name: The City Below
entries: Untold centuries before the Ostoyan Empire, eastern Etharis was home to a civilization with a different name. What this realm was called has been lost to history. Yet its bones have now been unearthed by the Darkfall.

Folk brave or foolish enough to descend into the fissures of {@area Soma|06e|x} find themselves in a labyrinthine ruin that runs throughout the land like veins beneath the surface of the skin. Known as the City Below, this necropolis is concentrated beneath Nov Ostoya but it reaches as far as {@area Fallowheart|07c|x} or {@area Malkovia|09d|x}. The necropolis is filled with broken streets and ruined buildings. Temples, statues, and houses have all swallowed by the earth. The walls of the City Below are decorated with magnificent murals. A forgotten language is scribed into the stonework, describing ancient myths and stories.

What remains a mystery is how deep the necropolis descends into Etharis. It's an underworld infested with zombies, ghouls, skeletons, and wraiths, making it dangerous to explore the city's secrets. These revenants are believed to have once been the citizens of the old civilization.

The Crimson Court are content spreading the lie that they are the most ancient and dangerous creatures to awaken from the {@area City Below|073|x}. But archeological evidence suggests there is something even greater buried in the dark, which the vampires themselves dread and revere.

entries.name: Hymn of Viduami
entries: At the dawn of time there was darkness. A darkness so black it smothered every light it touched and snuffed every life it crept over. A god of death, most ancient even among its own kin. The Viduami.

This god was fascinated by light. It was enamored by every flickering candle, every burning campfire, and spark of light within living creatures. It longed to feel warmth, for it was cold. But everything it touched perished. Eventually, its amorousness turned towards the stars and the sun herself. Viduami sought Aurelia's heat, but the pantheon knew if the sun was extinguished then all their mortal creations would wither and die. So, Viduami was betrayed and imprisoned within the furthest place the gods' could find. Deep in the darkness beneath the earth.

Centuries have turned into millennia. Eventually, early mortals discovered Viduami's tomb. They awakened and worshipped the old god, hoping that to appease death was to escape it. The Viduami heeded their prayers but remained trapped. The gift of its divine blood was all it could give.

But a god of death cannot grant eternal life. Its worshippers died, yet their souls remained trapped. They were transformed. The first undead and the progenitors of all undying things. The first vampires.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The following quests hooks provide ideas for adventures that might take place throughout the Ostoyan Empire.

entries.name: Saga of the Hidden Sun
entries: A lone and wounded traveler has arrived in the {@area Solar Grove|072|x}, causing a crisis. The traveler bears the tattoos of a Valikan and calls himself Erik of Clan Morgöng, claiming he sailed south to raid the shores of the Crimson Court. His crew were slaughtered in the effort. Erik has been on the run ever since, barely outpacing the strigoi of Viscountess Zeilgor.

Sylina Florian is deeply worried Erik has led the viscountess's fledglings to discover the Solar Grove. She must expel him. Furthermore, Sylina must ensure the vampires pick up Erik's trail after he has left the grove to lead them away. The archdruid seeks the help of adventurers who may be able to safely escort the Valikan back to his homeland.

Erik promises if he reaches Clan Morgöng, he can raise an even larger warband who will return to Ostoya to avenge his former crew. They will join the {@area Raevo|066|x} rebellion. But first, the fastest way to reach Valika from the Solar Grove is through the Valley of Mists, a nightmarish maze of cliffs and caves most don't find their way out from.

Sylina offers a small handful Solarwood stakes to the party who undertake this adventure, provided they promise to slay the strigoi on Erik's trail. The group must then brave the Valley of Mists. Inside, they must navigate treacherous terrain, deep natural dungeons, and fearsome monstrosities.
The adventurer's will know they've made it when they first see the sun.
How did you manage to lose all of the sheep?

entries.name: An Evening to Remember
entries: The Countess Katalina Vulpescu is hosting an evening gala at her estate. She's arranging an assassination.

The prey is the Viscount Langkirk Warlow, who the countess wishes to see removed to make room in the Crimson Court for her consort, Lady Valentina Bergow. This assassination is not sanctioned by the Grand Duke.

Countess Vulpescu provides her hired assassins with invitations to the gala. When she meets with them during the event, the countess provides her coconspirators with a yellow crystal, claiming that if it's placed into Warlow's goblet, it will immediately dissolve and turn the blood to acid. She promises Warlow's death will be a spectacularly gory affair, making it an evening to remember.

The difficulty, as Countess Vulpescu admits, will be successfully spiking the viscount's drink without his notice. Accomplishing this will take some creativity. How the assassins slip the crystal into Warlow's glass and convince him to drink it is entirely up to them. Alternatively, the yellow crystal could be fed to a mortal who the viscount then feeds upon directly—a cunning strategy against a strigoi such as the viscount, who prefers to devour their victims entirely.

Countess Vulpescu has chosen not to administer the poison herself, securing plausible deniability should her assassins fail. She has no compunction feeding them to the Court if their scheming does not succeed. That does mean that Vulpescu must trust her coconspirators, who could alternatively choose to use the yellow crystal against her to curry favor with Viscount Warlow. Or they could target any other Crimson Court member who happens to be present at the gala.

entries.name: Like Lambs to Slaughter
entries: A {@area Teriniman|068|x} shepherd named Ciril Meri is hiring guards to accompany him and his flock of sheep to market in Crowsbend. Ciril is shepherding six hundred sheep. He's offering to pay five {@item Gold|XDMG|Gold} leura for each one that survives until Crowsbend.

The journey from Ciril's farm to the Teriniman capital will take several days of strolling through Ostoya's eternal night. Countless predators, brigands, and even worse horrors lurk within the dark along the way. Protecting the herd will require caution and vigilance.

This journey may pass close to a restless cemetery. The skittish animals only agree to move past once the spirits inside have been calmed. Ghouls might crawl from crevices or ruins linked to the {@area City Below|073|x}, stealing sheep whenever they can't get their talons on humanoid prey. Crawling graves could awaken if passing over a field that was once an ancient battlefield. Closer to Crowsbend, desperate bandits from the city may attempt to capture a number of Ciril's sheep, either to ransom back or take to market themselves.

If more than seventy percent of Ciril's sheep survive the journey, he'll be overjoyed and pay six leura per animal instead. If less than forty percent make it, the shepherd will be distraught and only pay four leura per sheep to compensate his losses.

entries.name: Minesweeping
entries: The Burrowfell Mines are the largest source of iron ore in the Ostoyan Empire, and vital to {@area Soma's|06e|x} war efforts to suppress the {@area Raevo|066|x} rebellion and deter Büracher invaders. Baron Mihael watches it with a stern gaze. If ore production does not meet his expectations, he raises his blood tax on the community that runs the mines.

The foreman of the Burrowfell Mines, Cecilia Krieg, is searching for exterminators. The mines are a labyrinth of caverns and tunnels, which have been dug over decades since before {@area The Darkfall|080|x}. In the deepest tunnels, walls have occasionally collapsed to reveal entranceways into the City Below. This newer discovery has been a double-edged sword for Krieg and the miners. On the one hand, they've uncovered new treasures that have pleased the Baron besides the ore, while on the other hand undead horrors have begun crawling up the mineshafts and attacking the workers.

entries.title: Order of the Dawn War Pick

entries: The Burrowfell Mines are a huge complex. Krieg is offering ongoing payment for mercenaries to patrol the mines and kill anything that should already be dead. Particularly brave wardens may even choose to explore the City Below itself. Krieg is happy for her wardens to keep any treasures they find in the ancient ruins, provided they're preemptively striking down the dangerous monsters they also cross paths with.

Baron Mihael may choose to visit Burrowfell Mines at any time. If he's pleased with the progress the miners are making, The Wolf takes no issue with Cecilia Krieg hiring mercenaries to keep the mine safe. He may even wish to meet these wardens for himself. If progress has slowed for whatever reason, Krieg's mercenaries will not necessarily be spared the blood tax.

entries.name: The Riven Rascals
entries: Something in the Black Mire is stealing the children of {@area Umbroi|06c|x}. Historically the Marchioness has not been concerned with folk disappearing in the bog. It's not a particularly rare occurrence. But now children have vanished in Voyd, and the villagers are demanding action from the Marchioness. She has relented to hire monster hunters in exchange for a higher blood tax.

A distraught child, Blair, witnessed her friends being taken. She's entrusted to the hunting party as a guide. Blair is a clever child and knows she's being given as bait for whatever creature has disappeared her friends. But she's also brave. Blair insists on accompanying the hunters, even if they don't need her aid.

Travelling deep into the Mire, Blair points to the glowing lights that her friends followed before vanishing. These floating wisps bob and weave playfully through the bog. The sounds of children laughing and chattering echo near them.

Following the lights eventually leads to a woman and a man standing beneath a crooked tree. Their clothes are tattered and waterlogged. Their skin is eaten by mold. They are spirits of the Black Mire, and the will-o'-wisps that orbit them are the children they've taken. Beneath the trees is a horrific mound of small bodies.

The two wraiths can be banished easily enough. If they are spoken to instead, they recount in sepulchral whispers how their own child went missing in the Black Mire. The couple died while searching for him and have been taking other children since in their confused spectral state. If the hunters can discover the fate of the wraiths' child, the couple will depart peacefully, though investigating further could uncover an even worse monster or a conspiracy tied to Riven.

entries.name: The Raven's Apprentices
entries: In a harbor village on the coast of {@area Raevo|066|x}, a group of strangers meet for the first time. The next day, a wizard at the docks reads each of their names from a wax-sealed letter, inviting them to board a boat bound for the {@area Ravencourt Sanctuary|071|x}.

As apprentices of the Sanctuary, they will face countless dangers intended to challenge them individually and as a group. Each will choose to specialize in different avenues of magic. They will practice their arcane arts both during downtime, and while exploring the countless passages and hidden chambers of the Ravencourt Sanctuary. Certain doors may even lead them into other planes of existence.

Each apprentice will also have their own relationship with the Keeper. Perhaps the Keeper will adopt the form of lost loved ones or confront a pupil with the horrors of their past. Each apprentice will explore their own character, whilst also learning about their companions.

The Ravencourt Sanctuary will of course make each apprentice more powerful. Wizards will learn copious new spells. Sorcerers will train to control their magic and accept their ancestry, while warlocks will be invited to explore the extent of power their contract grants them. Some apprentices may rehearse the art of magic through song, and others might learn to wield spells whilst clad as a knight. An apprentice may even reach out to the Arch Seraphs for divine magic, reading sacred scriptures in the Sanctuary's library. That's not to say the Keeper won't test their faith.

When seven years have passed, the apprentices will return to Raevo. One will carry a wax sealed envelope of their own. In the aftermath of their training at the Ravencourt Sanctuary, what sort of mages will they become? A beacon of light in the darkness of Ostoya? Or the very villains the Arcanist Inquisition warns Etharis against?

entries.name: The City of Fallowheart
entries: Fallowheart is a city caught in the jaws of conflict. It lies on the western border of Ostoya, the first bastion against invasion from the Bürach Empire. Throughout its long history, Fallowheart has repelled countless assaults and sieges; as the gateway into the Ostoyan Empire, any invasion is doomed to fail if it cannot first capture Fallowheart.

At the same time, Fallowheart is caught directly between the grip of the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x} in the south and the rebellious influence of {@area Raevo|066|x} in the north. The battle for Ostoya's soul is concentrated on Fallowheart.

entries.name: Geography
entries: {@area Kaer Vena|090|x} casts a dark shadow over Fallowheart. The ancient fortress is built on a rocky crag that rises from the Arteriyan River, causing the water to crash and break around it. Kaer Vena is virtually impregnable and the seat of power in {@area Estspine|064|x}, usually held by the province's ruler. In times past, such rulers were descendants of the emigrants who traveled through the Grey Spine to found the city of Fallowheart. Presently, Kaer Vena is the blood fort of {@area Viscount Langkirk Warlow|092|x} of the Crimson Court.

Fallowheart is built on the Arteriyan River's southern bank, its great walls enclosing the city's inner boroughs. The settlement is besieged by dark forest on all sides; twisted tree branches scrape at the city's walls like fingers trying to claw their way inside.

Fallowheart's southern road leads toward the Stillborn Forest and eventually to Nov Ostoya. That road also forks toward the Grey Spine and the mountain pass that connects Ostoya to the Bürach Empire. Currently, the pass is held by the western nation, who have a small fort garrisoned at the eastern base of the mountains. Büracher spies frequently skulk into Fallowheart, while Ostoyan scouts watch the mountain pass closely; neither nation moves without the other noticing. Anxiety grows in Grabenstein to the west about the news of armies mustering outside Fallowheart's gates.

The bridge that crosses the Arteriyan at Fallowheart leads north to {@area Malkovia|09d|x} and Castalore. Those roads are traveled by soldiers loyal to Raevo, where the Crimson Court's influence thins. Though fewer lives are claimed by monsters in that direction, skirmishes are fought between Raevo militias trying to gather strength at Fallowheart and {@area Soma|06e|x} loyalists determined to undermine such efforts.

By far the quickest and safest way to travel through Fallowheart is by boat. Merchant ships, freight barges, and passenger boats arrive and depart from the docks in {@area Brevic|086|x} daily. This form of transport heavily favors travel and trade with the southern provinces of Ostoya, where the nation's rivers spread like arteries.

Fishing in the Arteriyan is a regular source of food for the city. In addition to the docks of Brevic, a small fishing village called Malach crouches on the north bank of the river. {@area Malach|08f|x} has become a rest stop for travelers entering or leaving Fallowheart. It's also a way in and out of the city without being seen—if one is willing to pay a smuggler's toll.

The village of {@area Blud|08f|x} similarly clings to Fallowheart's southern gate. The settlement grew organically around what had once been a noble's estate before {@area The Darkfall|080|x}. Locals now use the old mansion as a tavern. New homes frequently appear along the road into town, built by commoners hoping it's safer to live in sight of Fallowheart's gates.

entries.name: History
entries: When the first emigrants to leave the Bürach Empire journeyed through the Grey Spine in search of a new home, they soon discovered {@area Kaer Vena|090|x}. While the settlers were initially hesitant to build in the shadow of this inexplicable castle, Kaer Vena seemed to await their arrival. Its halls were empty and silent; those who erected it had long since disappeared. In need of refuge, the pilgrims claimed it.

The architecture of Kaer Vena was quite unlike the strongholds of either dwarves or humans in the Bürach Empire. Its stonework was arching and eerily ornate, murals made from skeletons lined the corridors, and sculpted bats spread their wings above doorways. The macabre imagery inside Kaer Vena might have frightened away the pilgrims had it not been for another sigil carved into the castle's great doors—a radiant sun.

Those who built Kaer Vena seemed to revere the sun: its likeness was emblazoned above mantels and painted across the ceiling of the main chamber. To the migrants, it was a symbol of the goddess Aurelia, and its appearance convinced them more than anything that this eastern land had been promised to them.

entries.name: Lady of Vengeance
entries: The separation of Ostoya from the Bürach Empire was not sudden. The realm that would one day call itself Ostoya began as a scattering of settlements that spread eastward from the Grey Spine, nestled along the twisting rivers and through the untamed pastures of this new land. Some folk still called themselves Büracher for a generation; then a new identity began to form. When the Bürach Empire came by force to reassert its laws and taxes, it was at Kaer Vena the eastern folk rallied to defend their newfound freedoms.
The Ostoyan armies gathered beneath the banners of the {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24|Arch Seraph Solyma} and her chosen prophet, Saint Dayla.

Dayla was humbly raised in the farmland of {@area Estspine|064|x}, a generation removed from the migration across the Grey Spine. But she remembered the stories her parents told of their struggles in the Bürach Empire—wealth stolen by nobles, mages imprisoned and separated from their families, loyalty to a distant Emperor enforced with iron. That such oppressors would come to reimpose their laws on the eastern free lands filled Dayla with rage and vengeance.

Saint Dayla's words, in the name of the Lady of Silence, changed the soul of Ostoya: from meek farmers who fled their homeland into warriors of defiant fury—the first war of secession forged Ostoya into a nation.

entries.name: The Darkfall
entries: At the time of the Darkfall, Fallowheart was embroiled in a second war against the Bürach Empire. The power of the west dwindled in the years following Emperor Leopold I's assassination. While the Hearthkeepers clutched to keep their fracturing empire from falling to pieces, seizing land seemed a shrewd strategy for bolstering Büracher national pride.

No hero rose this time to rally defenders at Kaer Vena. The Ostoyans fought for their homes but fell beneath the Bürach war machine. For a time, Unter and Raulish banners hung from Kaer Vena. Then the sun melted from the sky.

When Klaus Vorgen returned from Nov Ostoya to Fallowheart, he brought death to the invaders. No longer concerned with preserving life, Vorgen ordered the Ostoyans into battles they couldn't win simply to exhaust the Bürach battle lines. When the Ostoyans lay dead in the field, Vorgen raised them repeatedly, until their corpses were too broken to be of service.

The Büracher retreated in terror. Strigoi and fzeg hunted them down. There was no mercy—for what mercy can death provide? When his vengeance was satisfied, Vorgen gave Fallowheart to his Crimson Court to decide who among themselves should rule it.

entries.name: Notable Locations
entries: The most notable locations in Fallowheart include those described below.

entries.name: Grinfield
entries: Grinfield is Fallowheart's central and largest borough. Its pointed architecture, with steep tiled roofs and gothic archways, is typical of an Ostoyan city. Not a single stone of masonry is without some embellishment: every surface is an opportunity to engrave a tangle of thorny roses, a skeletal bas relief, or the glaring countenance of an owl. This grim ornamentation typifies how the architecture of ancient structures like Kaer Vena influenced the building styles brought from the west by the Büracher emigrants.

Most public businesses in Fallowheart are found in Grinfield, including merchants, trade posts, and reputable healers. This makes it a desirable borough for urban living. Homes are usually small multistory town houses with gothic facades lining cobblestone streets.

The folk of Grinfield are quiet and withdrawn, choosing not to acknowledge the grim realities of living in Ostoya. They are workers, tradesfolk, business owners, or inheritors with a modicum of wealth. Though they may have comparatively little to lose, they value what they do have when weighing the cost of rebellion against the oppressive Crimson Court.

entries.name: The Marla
entries: Facing into a main city square with multiple intersecting streets, the Marla is a dominating five-story building at the heart of Grinfield. The Marla is Fallowheart's largest inn and taphouse. Its banquet hall is frequented by travelers and locals alike, making it an excellent venue to gather news about the city and abroad.
The Marla is recommended as the most comfortable lodgings in Fallowheart, though folk warn against renting any room on the top floor.

entries.name: Cemetery
entries: The cemetery is a dark void among the lights of Grinfield. The earth is choked with corpses buried one upon the other, making the ground uneven and the tombstones lopsided. Fetid limbs and faces poke from the soil, picked at by ravens who crowd the branches of dead trees.

Near the center of the graveyard towers a mausoleum; this gothic building is the only other structure left by those who built Kaer Vena, decorated with similar ghastly engravings of skeletons and gargoyles. Rumors about the mausoleum suggest it's an entrance to the {@area City Below|073|x}—yet nobody has ever seen its interior for fear of the Gravekeeper.

entries.name: Augustine Trading Post
entries: The Augustine Trading Company operates several warehouses in Brevic, but their public-facing business is in Grinfield. They offer a large selection of resources and equipment, and occasionally sell Magic Items to select clients. The Trading Post also provides services such as transport or message delivery.

The Augustine Trading Company is officially neutral in the Ostoyan civil war, though it's known to have contracts on both sides. The specifics of such dealings are private, but the Trading Company prizes its access to vampire blood for their research in Morencia.

entries.name: Brevic
entries: Brevic's skyline is unique for its large storehouses, the masts of merchant ships in the harbor, and the Belltower near Fallowheart's western gate. The city's northern corner runs up against the muddy banks of the Arteriyan River. Its narrow, poorly lit streets are wet with filth, fish offal, and the water used to wash it all away.

The folk of Brevic are gruff, poor, and resentful. They prefer the company of locals to strangers, though they feign politeness to anyone cleaner and better dressed than themselves—they know the gold flows downriver. Trust comes slowly in Brevic, as the district has become a battleground for Fallowheart's criminal organizations.

entries.name: Belltower
entries: The Belltower rises above the western streets of Fallowheart like a sentinel guarding the city. It was originally built as a watchtower to warn {@area Kaer Vena|090|x} of approaching armies. More recently, it acts as a barracks for rapid deployment of soldiers to defend the western wall or quell civil unrest.

The presence of the Belltower does little to make the citizens of Fallowheart feel safer, least of all because the soldiers housed inside are under the command of the Viscount. Additionally, it's rumored the Belltower has become a hideout for agents of the Volitant, underground enforcers loyal to the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x}.

entries.name: Fish Market
entries: The Fish Market is a shanty town of ramshackle stalls crowded into a muddy square. Between the vendors hawking fish pulled directly from Fallowheart's muddy river, other goods are sold that may be considered too cheap, damaged, or illicit to be sold elsewhere in the city.

The Fish Market is always cacophonously busy. There are many eyes watching what happens here, but also many places to get lost in the crowd. Despite its folksy charm, the Fish Market is rumored to be a hidden battleground between the smugglers of the Ebon Syndicate and agents of the Volitant.

entries.name: Cerest
entries: Cerest is the hill atop which Saint Dayla's Cathedral is built. The great dome and steeples of the cathedral crown the hill and watch over the surrounding city like a shining beacon of hope in Ostoya's eternal night.

A band of soldiers guard the cathedral steps. Their allegiance is to {@area Raevo|066|x}, and their mandate is to protect the sacred hill from the Crimson Court. Truthfully, they're able to do little more than chase away vandals and {@area Soma|06e|x} loyalists; their numbers are too few in the event the Viscount decides to bleed the cathedral entirely.

entries.name: Saint Dayla's Cathedral
entries: The high arched windows of Saint Dayla's Cathedral are always lit by countless flickering candles. The singing of the congregation spills out into the surrounding city; their voices are an invitation for honest hearts to take shelter in the cathedral's light and join in the prayers to the Arch Seraphs.

Saint Dayla's Cathedral was built to honor the prophet of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. Her bones are buried beside it. In Dayla's name, the cathedral is a symbol of resistance against the oppression and autocracy the Crimson Court represents. Yet the congregants are not soldiers who can fight for their freedom against the Viscount's monsters. Furthermore, hope has not yet spread to the whole city; some consider the congregation to be mere agitators and Bürach sympathizers.
Nevertheless, those who hold faith with the Arch Seraphs or are foes of the Crimson Court can find friends in Saint Dayla's Cathedral.

entries.name: Est Vale
entries: The eastern corridor of Fallowheart is built between the low hills near {@area Kaer Vena|090|x} and the rise along which the city walls run. The borough is most notable for its larger dwellings, which belong to the city's wealthiest, and its barracks, which are home to Fallowheart's standing garrison. Though technically loyal to the city itself, the garrison is under Viscount Warlow's command.

entries.name: Violet Rose
entries: The Violet Rose is outwardly an upmarket taphouse. It's a venue for the city's wealthy and connected to liaise with the Viscount's inner circle, and therefore petition the Court outside official channels.

The more sinister purpose of the Violet Rose is for the privileged to shirk their obligations to the blood tax. Wealthy families or organizations who owe blood to the Court can instead send a relative or commoner in their employ to the Violet Rose. Here, the Viscount and his progeny feed on these sacrificial lambs at their leisure. Records are kept ensuring all taxes are paid.

entries.name: Grisham's Tower
entries: The crumbling tower in Est Vale is a mystery. It's stood for more generations than anyone can remember, yet it certainly wasn't there when the first migrants journeyed through the Grey Spine. Fallowheart's mighty walls awkwardly jut around the tower as if to accommodate its presence. There's no public record of the tower's construction, though rumors claim it belonged to a powerful wizard from whom it got its name.

The languishing citadel sits at the top of a low rise. Its ornate, iron-bound doors are barred to any visitors, and there is no light in any of the windows. Approaching the tower causes one's hair to stand on end, which superstitious locals take as an indisputable sign the tower is haunted. Yet those educated in the arcane can deduce the presence of a powerful eldritch aura emanating from the tower.

entries.name: Bridge Forts
entries: The bridge that connects Fallowheart to the Arteriyan's northern bank is protected at each end by two fortresses and in its middle by twin watchtowers. The design is such that an invading army would need to fight for every foot gained along the bridge. There are also water gates that can be dropped between the bridge's supports to block river travel.

entries.name: Blud & Malach
entries: Blud runs along the main road from Fallowheart's southern gate, as if the city walls simply couldn't fit every building inside. Near the center of the borough is the Baron's House, a boisterous tavern operating out of what used to be a noble's mansion. The bar staff wear the gaudy outfits found in the mansion's dusty wardrobes as uniforms. Travelers stop by the Baron's House to hear news or rumors before entering Fallowheart or leaving the safety of its lights for the wilderness.

Malach grew organically as a small fishing village. It soon became its own rest stop for folk traveling northward, a place to find cheaper food and board than inside the city. Most recently, Malach has gained a reputation as a den for smugglers moving contraband through Fallowheart via the {@area Brevic|086|x} docks. Raevo soldiers protect Malach from investigation by the Viscount's authorities.

entries.name: Kaer Vena
entries: The great battlements of Kaer Vena watch over all of Fallowheart. The black castle is at once a symbol of Ostoya's resistance against the Bürach Empire and its oppression beneath the Crimson Court. Currently occupied by the {@area Viscount Langkirk Warlow|092|x}, the blood in the streets of Fallowheart flow from Kaer Vena.

The castle is heavily defensible. Only a single stone bridge connects Kaer Vena to the southern shore, and a fortified gatehouse protects the south end of the bridge from assault. The Arteriyan River flows treacherously around the crags beneath Kaer Vena; landing boats on the island is impossible, even if one were able to then scale the sheer cliffs beneath the castle walls. The impregnability of Kaer Vena is what made it a bastion against Bürach's invasion.

Inside, the castle is cryptlike. High stone walls enclose small chambers and narrow passageways; arched iron windows invite in only the darkness of Ostoya, and some rooms are without windows entirely. Endless rotting doors, secret corridors, and gothic ornaments make Kaer Vena a sepulchral labyrinth to explore.

Warlow's throne room is unimpressive. Flickering embers from the fireplace cast an orange glow on the cold stone. Heraldic shields, rusted Weapons, and faded tapestries collect centuries of dust on the walls. The names of Fallowheart's rulers who hung them have long been forgotten.

The Viscount sits on a simple chair in a corner of the room. A cluster of ash-faced courtiers attend him with their backs to the fire, their shadows shielding the Viscount from the light. Warlow listens to petitioners from the darkness; a hunger glints in his eyes.

entries.name: Important Individuals
entries: Notable people associated with the city of Fallowheart include those described below.

entries.name: Viscount Langkirk Warlow
entries: Viscount Warlow is a hideous man with a sickly complexion. His yellowed eyes bulge from a narrow face. His needle-like fangs are barely contained within his lipless mouth. His ears are tattered, and his long nose is withered. The Viscount resembles a horrifying corpse dressed as a flamboyant noble. His crooked spine bends so far forward his hands could reach the ground if he wasn't leaning heavily on a cane.

Of course, the frailty of the Viscount's appearance belies the truth: Warlow is a powerful strigoi. He rarely needs to exercise his own strength, yet those who've witnessed it cannot forget the sounds of bursting flesh or splintering bone. His pleasure is the prime concern of his court, lest the Viscount feel a hunger.

Warlow is cruel, manic, and sometimes droll. Yet his apparent whimsy masks a withering rage and insecurity that demands submission and worship. Warlow's obsession with power is fueled by anxiety about his subordinate position within the Crimson Court. He fears Fallowheart being snatched from him by an ambitious rival—or worse, a displeased Drago Koshevek. Therefore, he demonstrates little pity when ensuring {@area Estspine's|064|x}'s mines meet the demands of the Grand Duke.

Viscount Warlow believes the shred of civility and decorum he affords his subjects separates him from lesser monsters. He playacts listening to courtly petitioners and invites wealthy commoners to macabre dinners. Those who attend him must ignore the blood on the Viscount's teeth and fingers. He shrewdly sorts his mortal allies from his enemies, rewarding those who please him while plotting punishments for those who don't. Yet the Viscount knows he can't simply kill whoever he chooses.

Maintaining resource production to ensure the Viscount's wealth requires a functioning society, as does defending Ostoya from the Bürach armies to the west. Warlow can't afford to incite rebellion by creating martyrs, nor can he weaken his own Soman army by inviting conflict with {@area Raevo|066|x}. Therefore, the Viscount tolerates a measure of dissent in his city but limits the harm they can bring to him. He employs more surreptitious Weapons to eliminate threats to his power while maintaining deniability of his most deplorable actions.
Nevertheless, Langkirk Warlow's violent and obsessive nature leaves little doubt of the spiteful lengths he would go to ensure he is never deposed.
Look at my lovely children. I wish I could have them all for dinner.

entries.name: Sister Abigail Florentine
entries: The congregation at Saint Dayla's Cathedral was left leaderless when their priest was arrested for fomenting rebellion. Father Amriel had been an outspoken critic of Viscount Warlow, and growing faith in the Arch Seraphs undermined the Crimson Court's supremacy.

When despair seemed certain, Sister Abigail stepped in to lead the congregation instead. The young acolyte has not yet performed the rites to call herself priestess, yet her valor and conviction makes others look to her for guidance. Outwardly, Sister Abigail preaches kindness and the rule of law—she does not wish to repeat her predecessor's mistakes and give pretext for her arrest. Yet privately, Abigail is aware she's a spiritual leader in a time of war. Her cathedral is a beacon of hope in the dark; while it stands, {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}'s vengeance is possible against the monsters who oppress Fallowheart.

Sister Abigail's congregation are the eyes and ears of the Raevo army, who camp on the northern bank. And when the time comes for Raevo to claim Fallowheart, the commoners will pour from Saint Dayla's Cathedral, pitchforks in hand, to overthrow the nobility.

entries.name: Solyma's Champion
entries: There are many legends about Saint Dayla. A verse in her scripture describes how Lady {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} forged a host of celestial machines sent to defend Ostoya alongside Dayla's army. Not everyone interprets these written words literally—but the story must bear some truth, for one such construct remains in Fallowheart.

{@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}'s Champion is a {@b {@creature Pactbreaker|GHMG}} bound to Saint Dayla's Cathedral. It follows the commands of the priest leading the congregation, detecting those corrupted by malevolent influences. Currently, it serves as Sister Abigail's bodyguard. Anyone entering the cathedral with malintent must face the Champion.

entries.name: Stanislava
entries: Stanislava leads the Fallowheart sect of the Volitant. She is a dhampir who seeks the favor of the Crimson Court. By uprooting the Viscount's enemies hiding in the city's underbelly, she hopes to be legitimized into the nobility.

For now, Stanislava operates from the shadows. She corrupts the city watch with coin earned by the Volitant's illegal activities, which include theft, blackmail, information brokering, and assassination.

entries.name: Edward Vilgé
entries: Vilgé is a career thief and assassin who worked his way to the top of the Ebon Syndicate in Fallowheart. Born in {@area Malkovia|09d|x}, he has no special loyalty to this city or the Court; he holds no ideals at all aside from coin. However, he and the Ebon Syndicate have raised Viscount Warlow's ire by smuggling Weapons and information to Raevo.

Vilgé's newfound preference for supporting Raevo is simply a means of survival. His agents are being hunted by the Volitant; his businesses are being targeted by city guards. Vilgé needs allies and will strike any bargain that keeps him and his associates out of the Viscount's fangs.

The current headquarters of the Ebon Syndicate in Fallowheart is an unmarked boat docked in {@area Brevic|086|x} called the {@i Edict of Freedom}. Vilgé is prepared to escape the city at any moment—yet the larger Ebon Syndicate would look poorly upon abandoning its interests in Fallowheart entirely.

entries.name: The Gravekeeper
entries: The Gravekeeper is a lumbering revenant loosely wrapped in soiled rags. Its desiccated frame drags a shovel behind it, stalking Fallowheart with the scraping sound of metal on cobblestones.

Nobody knows who the Gravekeeper was or how it came to be. It haunts the city's streets, searching for cadavers to bury in its graveyard, going to great lengths to claim the recently deceased. The Gravekeeper breaks into homes, assaults hearses, and pulls dead vagrants from alleys. Loved ones are dragged unceremoniously through the streets and their remains crushed into shallow graves between countless others in the cemetery; they receive no burial rites or funeral.

Attitudes towards the Gravekeeper have changed through the centuries. As terrifying as it appears, it never harms anyone living who doesn't interfere with its work—but those that do often end up buried in the cemetery themselves. In times of plague, the Gravekeeper has even been considered useful, cleaning the city of diseased carcasses. Yet its desecration of loved ones, dragged and buried without ceremony or blessings, is a humiliation to those already grieving.

There have been several attempts to destroy the Gravekeeper. Most fail in the city streets, where the assailants are crushed and mangled by the Gravekeeper's shovel. On other occasions, funds have been raised to hire a monster hunter or mage, and the Gravekeeper is defeated in battle. Each time it returns, soon clambering from Fallowheart's mausoleum rejuvenated and ready to resume its grisly work.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The following quest hooks can be used to flesh out adventures set in the city of Fallowheart.
