Her uncompromising manner has put the Watchers on a violent path against those nations and factions she believes violate the Eternal Dogma.

entries.name: Other Leaders
entries: The other current High Priests are:

items.name: Audres D'Xeo (Aphaelon
entries: The High Priest of the Arch Seraph of Temperance supports a more tranquil approach than the faction currently employs, and she finds herself, and her sect, often at odds with the current Divine Speaker.

items.name: Carma Aruejo (Morael)
entries: The High Priest of the Arch Seraph of Sacrifice worries about the collateral effects of their faction's aggressive evangelism, while at the same time supporting the heroes who do it. When the Council votes, her vote is often unpredictable and is easily swayed.

items.name: Lawirsen Kheshev (Zabriel)
entries: The High Priest of the Arch Seraph of Truth is quiet when it comes to political maneuvering, but controls a network of spies and librarians that gather information to aid the faction's missionaries in their work. He is a tacit supporter of the current Divine Speaker.

items.name: Srokkush (Solyma)
entries: The dragonborn High Priest of the Arch Seraph of Justice believes the faction should take an even more militant role in rousing the common folk to burn heretics, criminals, and those who truck with eldritch abominations. He is a strong supporter of the current Divine Speaker.

items.name: Velten Giessers (Miklas)
entries: The High Priest of the Arch Seraph of Mercy sends missionaries to heal the sick and care for those in distress. For the common folk, priests of {@deity Miklas|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} are a welcome sight, and win many converts, making Velten perhaps the second most powerful High Priest after the Divine Speaker, and a natural rival. Most disagreements on the Council start between the two of them.

entries.name: The Arcanist Inquisition
entries: The Watchers of the Faithful and the Arcanist Inquisition have a special relationship. The Inquisition views the Watchers as clergy worthy of respect, but not capable of doing the direct, violent work the Arch Seraphs require. The Inquisition can rely on divine spellcasting support and a steady flow of reports from the Watchers.

In turn, the Watchers see the Inquisition as a blunt instrument that can be turned against enemies of the faith; even easier to manipulate than the common folk. By passing along reports of "heretics" from their missionaries in the field, the Watchers regularly induce the Inquisition to eliminate those who stand in the Watcher's way.
The two factions find their alliance is mutually beneficial, but rarely coordinate their actions; each trying to use the other when it suits their purposes.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Watchers of the Faithful are not soldiers. While they may have some have martial training, their members are priests, spies, and evangelists. Watchers fight with words and not blades. They spur faithful populations to turn against those they deem heretical. They are not single heroes, but rather the voice behind the zealous mob. The most common missionaries are Watchful Priests who combine divine spells with an almost magical command of oratory to enflame crowds.

entries.name: Important Locations
entries: The Watchers of the Faithful maintain their seat of power in the Grand Temple of Toletum. This massive cathedral forms a starburst shape with the main nave and central chapel devoted to {@deity Empyreus|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} and smaller chapels radiating off the center. The glories of the {@area Arch Seraphs|352|x} are depicted in statuary and murals, religious splendor rarely seen elsewhere in {@book Castinella|GrimHollowCG24|6}. Each chapel displays a sacred relic, though they are only replicas and the true relics are well-hidden below. Rumors say that behind the beautiful walls lies a secret passage. The rumors are wrong, there are several, and also many holes that Watchers use listen to the whispers of those within its walls.

High above the central chapel rises a six-sided tower topped by the Shining Chamber where the Divine Speaker and the Council of High Priests meet around a hexagonal table. The leader of each sect sits beneath a massive stained-glass window, depicting the Arch Seraph that they follow.

The grandeur above ground is but a small piece of the whole structure. The Watchers have constructed several levels beneath the Grand Temple in secret. These passages allow the Watchers' agents to come and go without being observed, and access the secret passages and whisper holes within the Grand Temple itself.

In the levels below, the Watchers maintain a vast scriptorium and a library to collate all the information that flows from their agents. On the bottom level, the holy relics of each sect also rest in separate, private shrines in hidden chambers. Each sect claims that their relic possesses great power, and its touch bestows power upon the most deserving believers. Only {@deity Zabriel|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} sect is telling the truth. The {@i Tome of Saint Lysimachus} grants knowledge to those who would use it in the fight against evil. Others granted the chance to lay hands upon a relic are always "found wanting" by the Arch Seraphs.

Elsewhere, the Watchers of the Faithful make their homes in churches, traveling shrines, and all places of faith. Stationary Watchers rarely make their allegiance known, reporting back to the Grand Temple while pretending to be simple clerics of their Arch Seraph. These churches usually have hidden spaces where friendly agents might hide or private conversations might occur.

To better perform their duties, wandering evangelists wear patches depicting the symbol of the Watchers and travel with wagons called {@i capillarante}, that unfold into shrines. These rolling chapels contain all manner of banners, rugs, candles, and relics designed to impress the common people. It is not uncommon for the wagons to also carry food, coin, and cudgels that might help to spark a riot, should one be needed.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: Most quests involving the Watchers of the Faithful are in some way wrapped up with religion, though not always to the glory of the {@area Arch Seraphs|352|x}. All too often the mortal leaders of the faction make political decisions to undermine their rivals or enrage a mob against a secular ruler. Completing or foiling these missions are best suited for characters skilled in oratory or stealth.

entries.name: Civil Unrest
entries: The populace of Adleheim has been roused against a local lord. Lady Mina Oberlinder is accused of heresy and the worship of {@deity Malikir|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. The townsfolk burn effigies and block all goods from entering the keep where she hides. The characters are asked to uncover the source of the accusation and deescalate the situation...preferably without slaughtering citizens. The origin is a Watchful Priest named Alect Brightson sent by the Watchers after Lady Oberlinder expelled the local cleric of {@deity Empyreus|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} for being a faction spy.

entries.name: Northern Captive
entries: Valikan Raiders following Unnal Kellsdottir have captured a Watchful Priest named Vasesclaav in a raid on a seaside village and are trying to ransom the priest back. After the raid, the village can no longer afford the ransom, and the characters are needed to rescue the priest. When the characters find the Valikan camp, it is larger than expected, and Vasesclaav refuses to leave. From his prison cage he has been trying to convert the Valikans away from their paganistic Primordial religion to worship of the Sword Saint. He has at least one convert, and he is betting his life that he will win over many more.

entries.name: Relic Recovery
entries: Trusted members of the faction are tasked with recovering a holy relic of {@deity Empyreus|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, a magic pauldron said to have been dropped by the Sword Saint himself that blesses the worthy to be almost invulnerable to daemonic attacks. The relic is held in a monastery of blasphemous vampire "priests" in Ostoya that have caged dozens of innocent mortals. The Divine Speaker is desperate to recover this relic so that her sect might have a true relic like the sect of {@deity Zabriel|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, and she is willing to overlook whatever the characters might have to do to recover the relic; even leaving innocents behind.

entries.name: Recruit the Downcast
entries: A downcast name Rezzaeil has been seen living hermitically in the foothills, and the Watchers would like the downcast to join their faction. They call on the characters to travel into the rough countryside in the {@book Bürach Empire|GrimHollowCG24|3} to find the downcast. Unfortunately for the characters, Rezzaeil once fought for the Arch Daemon {@deity Tormach|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, but an alliance has been made even more unlikely because the downcast has heard the call of the Great Beast. The creature has shattered his mind and twisted his body with strange mutations. Once the characters are in the hills, the thing he has become starts hunting them for sport.

entries.name: Village Hysteria
entries: A forest village called Clarnesse is in a religious uproar and witch hunt, searching for werewolves and fey warlocks living among them. A Watchful Priest named Jahnfelix Pröll has created an environment of mass hysteria where fear, false accusations, and personal grudges are destroying the village. While hunting for the heretical abominations, it quickly becomes clear that those sent to the flames by their neighbors were innocents. Indeed, Jahnfelix has been corrupted by the worship of {@deity Venin|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, the Great Deceiver, and the characters will have to turn the people of Clarnesse against the priest and his guards before they burn down an entire village in search of heretics.

entries.name: Priestly Escort
entries: A Watchful Priest named Elissan asks for a safe escort across a foreign land (such as {@book Valika|GrimHollowCG24|7} or Ostoya) so she can preach and convert the locals. The characters are asked to act as her bodyguards throughout the journey, both in settlements and through the wilderness. If successful, and they demonstrate their faith in the Arch Seraphs to Elissan, she will speak for them should they choose to join the Watchers of the Faithful.

entries.name: Relic's Origin
entries: A fire beneath the Grand Temple of Toletum was so inexplicably hot that it cracked the stones of the altar that houses the relic of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. High Priest Srokkush did not truly believe that his sect's relic held any power, but the relic was completely untouched by the fire. Is it blessed by the Lady of Vengeance? Not yet willing to trust the characters in the underhalls, he sends them on a mission to uncover the true origin of the relic: a red beaded scarf called the Torremuria, said to have been worn by a simple Morencian midwife who slew an abomination in defense of an infant. The trail leads to a path of lies planted by the fiend Zuurag, the Oathbreaker, as part of a plan to corrupt the Grand Temple from within. Once the plot is uncovered, the High Priest further requests the characters sneak into the Grand Temple and steal the shawl so it can be destroyed with any shame falling upon the sect for harboring a corrupted artifact.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: The Watchers of the Faithful do not give gold, land, or title to their followers. Instead, the faction's strength lies first in its divine magic. Worthy allies might be granted clerical spells of {@filter fifth-level or lower|spells|source=aag;ai;bmt;egw;ftd;ggr;idrotf;phb;sato;scc;tce;xge;xphb;rmr;scag;eepc;erlw;vgm;mot;frhof;efa;lox;toa;tftyp-dit;pota;oota;cos;skt;tftyp-tfof;wdh;wdmm;slw;cm;scc-tmm;dsotdq;kftgv;qftis;bgdia;crcotn;rot;tor;wbtw;us;tofw;lrdt;hotdq;dc;tftyp-toh;ditlcot;tftyp-tsc;tftyp-thsot;gos;sdw;hol;scc-ck;scc-hfmt;veor;jttrc;oow;dip;tftyp-atg;pabtso;tftyp-wpm;scc-arir;dosi;efr;fs;kkw;rmbre;lmop;vnotee;dd;drde-bd;drde-twoo;drde-tdon;fraif;drde-tfv;drde-bts;drde-sd;hotb;ffotr;drde-dotsc;drde-acfas;drde-fwtvc;drde-das;grimhollowpg24|level=0;1;2;3;4;5|class=cleric} for good reason, such as lifting a curse by casting {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB}. The Watchers can also provide advice and divine guidance by casting {@spell Augury|XPHB} or even {@spell Commune|XPHB} to help a party with difficult decisions. The Watchers will not, however, bring back the dead. For some reason, such power is beyond the capability of even the {@creature High Priest|GrimHollowCG24|High Priests}, though the Watchers will provide a very touching funeral service and a plot of land in a sanctified cemetery to prevent any deceased from rising as undead.

Valued agents might be gifted {@i {@item Potions of Healing|XDMG|Healing Potions}} or prayer books containing uncommon {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG|Spell Scrolls}. Characters who earn great favor might even gain reliquaries with the ability to cast divine spells like wands or staves.

In addition to magical aid, the faction's temples offer safe havens to its members; particularly useful against creatures that can't enter hallowed ground. The healers of the Watchers are talented beyond magical aid. For believers, prayers to the Arch Seraphs in their temples while resting give {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saves against magical contagion or curses, and removing two levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} per {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While at an allied temple or church, characters might also have access to information about the area, especially as it pertains to potential heretics or monsters that the Watchers are aware of.

The greatest gift that the Watchers can bestow is the touch of their holy relics. Each sect holds a holy relic that they claim possesses a small piece of their Arch Seraph's power. When lain upon the brow or shoulder of a favored believer, the sect's {@creature High Priest|GrimHollowCG24} claims that if the Arch Seraph wills it, the relic can grant a blessing that bestows knowledge of a feat or the ability to cast a specific spell. Only {@deity Zabriel|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}'s relic truly possesses this power, and the feat or spell is always something most useful against whatever evil the character is currently hunting. This blessing lasts for one month, unless the bearer commits a sin against the Arch Seraph, and then the character instantly lose access to the blessing.

entries.title: {@i Arcane magic is a scourge on all of Etharis. No mortal can be trusted to practice it without becoming corrupted}.

items: The Arcanist Inquisition is more than a religious order, it represents the core philosophy at the heart of the {@book Castinellan Provinces|GrimHollowCG24|6}. The Inquisition was founded by "the Unifier" Montego Valieda, after bringing peace to the warring provinces. Valieda claimed to have communed with {@deity Empyreus|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, the Arch Seraph of Valour, who told Valieda that arcane magic was a curse endangering its wielders and the greater world. Immediately after unification, he established a new order that would limit and control the use of magic across Castinella.

Upon establishing the Arcanist Inquisition, Montego took the name Sagrado, as a symbol of transformation and devotion to the seraph. He set upon fulfilling the seraph's mandate of cleansing the curse of arcane magic from the provinces. To this end, he initiated his most fervent followers as Inquisitors. These "messengers of the seraph" travelled far and wide. They not only preached the dangers of magic but also identified and began to arrest those suspected of being magically gifted.

The Inquisition was not initially violent, and the arrested were intended to live in humble conditions, avoid arcane practices, and dedicate themselves to the seraph's service—whether they wanted to or not. The Inquisitors sought to identify the gift at the youngest possible age, claiming it would be easier to contain. The rise of dark magic cults and monster attacks throughout Etharis only empowered the Inquisition's dogmatic teachings. It also helped that in all matters outside of the arcanely gifted, the Inquisitors sought peace, justice, and valour.
They sought to embody moral purity, and their clearcut mindset was easy to embrace for those unwilling to wrestle with more complex truths.

With the Inquisition set upon its path, Sagrado Valieda traveled to the Shattered Peaks to bring the message of the Inquisition to the dragonborn. Finding them amenable to the Arch Seraph's teachings, Valieda sealed their support, and the Inquisition's hold on power, by offering them an opportunity to return to Castinella and rebuild their ancestral city of Ember Cairn. Many dragonborn embraced the Inquisition's cause and returned. This has proven to be a prosperous arrangement, and today, the dragonborn of Ember Cairn are some of the most zealous proponents of the Inquisition's dogma.

Although it had been Valieda's intention that future leaders would take his title when he passed on, out of respect for his legacy, no other leader has taken the name Sagrada. The tradition to take a transformative name remained, but more recently, the title of Lord Inquisitor has become more prominent.
Ember Cairn became the heart of the Inquisition, and the massive Ember Cathedral—which the dragonborn constructed at Valieda's behest—its headquarters.

The cathedral is a wonder of architecture and engineering. Constructed in a massive hollowed-out rock formation, it combines an impressive visage with a vast underground complex that includes everything from Inquisitor quarters to prison cells. It is said that the underground complex is so massive and its paths so twisted that only the Inquisitors can find their way.

The ruling seat of the Inquisition is located at the cathedral, and currently belongs to Lord Inquisitor Crevax, an imposing seven-foot-tall dragonborn. Crevax is zealous, uncompromising, and entirely committed to the teachings of the Seraph.

Over the years, and more than ever under Crevax's reign, the treatment of the Inquisition's prisoners has shifted from monastic-like confinement to cruel imprisonment. There are those within the Inquisition who believe it is losing its way, but they often find themselves afraid to speak their minds, for fear of having the tables turned on them. After all, it takes merely a word or suspicion to get thrown into the Cathedral's maze.

entries.name: Goal: Eradicate the Arcane Blight
entries: The Inquisition views any magic other than divine as a curse to be exterminated. Those who carry the curse are to be pitied and separated to minimize their damage and reduce their opportunity to endanger others or pass it on.

Aside from locating and capturing those who practice or have access to non-divine magic, the Inquisition has long sought ways to strip such magic from those "cursed" with it. They have carried out many brutal experiments with this over the years, but have yet to discover a reliable method.

entries.name: Goal: Will of the Arch-Seraph
entries: The Inquisition sees its mission is to fulfill the will of the {@deity Empyreus|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24|Arch Seraph Empyreus}, and to live a life of moral purity and valor. While it is best known for their persecution of arcane magic, Inquisitors also fight monsters, provide healing, and cure curses. To those they find worthy and pure of faith, the Inquisition can be a protective shield against an increasingly dark world.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Inquisition works as much on propaganda as on actual deeds, and its agents are rarely hidden. The very mention of the Inquisition can strike fear into the populace, and they use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to their benefit. Servants of the Inquisition proudly wear the Inquisition's emblem, which they get inscribed onto as many items as possible. Unless they are part of an Inquisitor's retinue, lowest rank fighters, who are barely more than thugs, typically don't have uniforms.
Inquisitor-level agents, including priests, knights, warders, and judges, tend to have both symbols and a uniform. Uniforms are determined by the individual chapter of the faction.

{@i Grim Hollow: The Monster Grimoire} contains the statistics and tips on using several {@b {@filter Arcanist Inquisitor|bestiary|source=ghmg|search=inquisitor}} agents. Alternatively, consider leveraging {@creature Cultist|XMM}, {@creature Knight|XMM}, or {@creature Priest|XMM} statblocks.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: Even when it's not the direct villain, the Arcanist Inquisition should feel like an oppressive presence for the player characters. In situations when they work for the Inquisition or collaborate with them, there should always be an air of doubt regarding whether they have done the right thing.

entries.name: Hunting Mages
entries: Shortly after the characters arrive in a smaller Castinellan settlement, they learn of the arrival of a group of Inquisitors, who are hunting mages. The Inquisitors immediately take notice of the party, whether or not there are mages among them. The characters must either escape or defeat this branch of the Inquisition, but many locals are on the Inquisitors' side and ready to turn on them.

entries.name: Catching Thieves
entries: Agents of the church seek help tracking down a band of thieves who've made off with a priceless relic. The band was aided by a mage, who can be tracked to the city of Camont. However, the Inquisition has set their own trap, as they look to capture both the thieves and any mages in the group sent to recover the relic.

entries.name: Ally's Execution
entries: While on an unrelated quest, the party is given assistance or shelter by the village's journeyman mage. Later, they learn that the mage has been arrested by the Inquisition and is being taken to the nearest city for a public execution. Time is short and the characters wishing to save the mage must either ambush the Inquisitors en-route or attempt a daring rescue in the city.

entries.name: Herbalist's Arrest
entries: While visiting the shop of a "magic herbalist," the characters are interrupted by the Arcanist Inquisition, who have come to arrest the herbalist for taking part in a dark conspiracy to destroy the city. The herbalist proclaims his innocence and attempts to gain the party's favor. In truth, the herbalist is one of four mages in the city who are readying a ritual to summon an {@area Aether Kindred|36b|x}. The Inquisition has only heard rumors of the conspiracy and are unaware of the greater plan. The herbalist uses anti-Inquisition sentiment to fool the characters. The characters must either work with the Inquisition to uncover the sinister plot, or, if they side with the herbalist, find themselves unwitting enablers of dark magic and must reap the consequences.

entries.name: Child Healer
entries: Seeking healing or a curse remedy, the characters arrive at a small village which has recently become famous for a child who is said to have performed healing miracles in the name of the Arch Seraph. Upon arrival, they learn that the miracle child is missing, and her parents are desperate to find her. The arrival of an Inquisitor further complicates the matter. Newly-promoted and a true believer in the Seraph's word, Ghodelia Vasca, has been sent by her corrupt master Kodolus to judge the girl for blasphemy and ensure she is silenced.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: Parties that agree to work alongside the Inquisition may be rewarded with divine spellcasting and divine-themed Magic Items. The Inquisition's specialty is items and spells designed to counter magic, such as Armor or shields that resist spells, scrolls that counter or {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, or {@spell Silence|XPHB} casters.

Being on good terms with the Inquisition can provide characters access to interrogate captured mages, and libraries filled with forbidden texts about curses, magic, and monsters. Access to secret teachings and powers of the Arch Seraph is rumored to be available to the highest-level Inquisitors.

entries.title: {@i I have slept since the world was young. Yet now that I'm awake, all I feel is hunger}.

items: The Crimson Court is the ruling body of Ostoya, consisting of the seven most powerful vampire nobles in Ostoya. The Court governs through fear and command of vast wealth and resources, demanding complete obedience of their servants in return for protection from the undead and the {@book Bürach Empire|GrimHollowCG24|3}.

Formed after the Darkfall, the Crimson Court consisted of Ostoyan nobility transformed by the undead plague released from the City Below. The vampiric curse proved crucial in turning the tide in the Second Bürach-Ostoyan war, and as such, the citizenry remains submissive to their leaders. For the commonfolk, donation to the blood tax is a fate far preferred to death by banshee's wail, or even worse—the attention of the Court itself.

Currently, the Court is ruled by Grand Duke Drago Koshevek. The Grand Duke is capable of executing political schemes and battlefield tactics in equal measure, though fear is his greatest weapon of choice. As the most ancient and powerful among the Court, all bow to the Grand Duke's whims, lest they feel his wrath. His obsession with defeating the Bürach Empire has made him unpopular with the other members of the Court, though none dare make their opinions known.

The other six members each rule a province of {@book Ostoya|GrimHollowCG24|4}, given relative autonomy and control over their vassals and holdings as long as they serve Grand Duke's schemes. Behind closed doors, all members and lesser nobles engage in subterfuge, manipulation, and assassination to wrest power from one another and become leader of the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x}. Even the most politically savvy nobles discover they are both player and pawn.

Some Court members gather for lavish masquerades or fight together in blood-soaked battles against invaders. Others may actively avoid fellow members for fear of treachery. The Court is equally likely to negotiate and bargain with each other as they are to conspire against each other for their own aims.

Each Court member resides in their own estate or keep within their province. The location of the residence is publicly known to the commonfolk, but only the foolish arrive uninvited, lest they become the target of traps or unwitting participants in a noble's depraved interests. On rare instances, the Grand Duke summons all Court members to his fortress stronghold of Blackharbour in Nov Ostoya. Invitations to such meetings often herald a change of Court membership.

entries.name: Goal: Slake the Endless Thirst
entries: The citizenry of Ostoya justify their fear-soaked obedience to the Crimson Court by convincing themselves that the Court protects them from the undead that roam the nation and the Bürach Empire that covet their lands. While protection is a responsibility of the Court, the vampire nobles have far greater pursuits that operate on the scale of decades rather than years.

Ultimately, confrontations with the undead and Bürach serve the greater goal of growing a nation solely devoted to the existence of vampires. The Court's thirst for blood and hedonistic tendencies must be fueled by a subservient living populace. Those of low ambition are satisfied with the fear and power they command over their provincial subjects. Those of higher aspirations envision expanding the Darkfall's boundaries and being worshiped as gods, if not ascending to godhood themselves.
As such, vampire nobles are slow to bestow vampirism to even the most obedient mortal servants, as each new vampire represents a potential rival for power and blood.

The unspoken truth among the Court is that a careful balance of vampires to mortals must be maintained, otherwise the vampire nation of Ostoya can no longer support itself. A main priority of the Court is the destruction of the Raevo insurgency, as it represents the one point of light in Ostoya that risks their cattle rebelling against them.

entries.name: Agents
entries: Each member of the Crimson Court controls many lieutenants, including minor nobles spawned from the ruling vampire. {@creature Soman Vampire Berserker|GHMG|Soman Vampire Berserkers}, {@creature Soman Vampire Noble|GHMG|Nobles}, and {@creature Soman Vampire Sangromancer|GHMG|Sangromancers} are detailed {@i Grim Hollow: The Monster Grimoire}, or use any standard {@creature Vampire|XMM} stat blocks to represent these creatures.

Each vampire is responsible for some aspect of governance and is, in turn charged with leading servants further down the feudal pyramid. The Court employs many mortals, some who serve out of genuine loyalty, others out of fear, and still others in the hopes of being deemed worthy of becoming vampires themselves.

The Crimson Court happily uses lesser undead to inflict terror or as disposable soldiers when needed. Indeed, it has become a grotesque game (presumably started by Viscount Warlow) for each Court member to decorate their undead in garish Armor and grafted limbs to see who can strike the most fear in an enemy. Such undead are usually captured by mercenaries or unlucky serfs.

Ostoyan Vampires despise other lords of undead, such as liches, who they view as abhorrent imitations of themselves. Though they may employ other entities from within the City Below, such undead are never raised to the level of the Crimson Court. Nevertheless, these monsters possess ancient power and knowledge, highly sought after among the Court, even if the vampires do not always grasp the implications of accepting such aid.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The Court welcomes well-meaning adventurers into Ostoya, regarding them as easily manipulated bodies to unwittingly carry out schemes. Indeed, it's a favored pastime of some Court members to ‘compete' for the number of mercenaries in their employ to demonstrate their power and influence over their rivals. Especially successful agents may rise to become prominent players in Ostoyan politics, potentially influencing the outcome of plots.

entries.name: Delving Below
entries: Baron Mihael the Wolf sends the party deep into the City Below to retrieve an ancient artifact rumored to empower the undead. However, neither the Grand Duke, nor the other Court members, are to be aware of this mission. While underground, the party comes across rival mercenaries hired by Countess Katalina Velpescu to retrieve the same artifact. These mercenaries are willing to work with your party to increase the chances of finding the artifact, but the question of who will claim it remains unanswered.

entries.name: Undead Turmoil
entries: Viscount Langkirk Warlow sends the party to a village in Estpine that is under threat by an undead creature that's a mystery even to the vampire nobles. This new undead is a creation by Sacha, a secluded necromancer hoping to create a weapon to protect his village without paying the Blood Tax to the Court. The creature shakes free of Augustin's control and begins to wreak havoc on villagers and vampires alike.

entries.name: On Behalf of the Court
entries: The Grand Duke sends the party on a diplomatic mission to {@book Liesech|GrimHollowCG24|8} to establish communication with the city's leader, the vampire Analita von Raiza. The Grand Duke hopes to convince von Raiza to sabotage Bürach supply lines in return for membership on the Court. If the other Court members learn of this agreement, they may not welcome the idea of another rival to compete against.

entries.name: Masked Intent
entries: Marchioness Iulia Cutov hires the party to attend an extravagant masquerade held by Countess Vasinesca with the intention of assassinating her. However, your patron's involvement cannot be made known, or your party will incur the wrath of at least two Court members.

entries.name: Thirst for Power
entries: A failed assassination attempt on a member of the Crimson Court spurs the Court to hire the party to track down who is responsible and make them an example to any who would threaten the Court's hold over Ostoya. But what does the party do when investigations point towards another member of the Court?

entries.name: Desperate Measures
entries: To retain her seat at the Court, the disgraced Viscountess Zeilgor employs the party to establish diplomatic relations with Raevo. She has discovered that one of Raevan wizards is conspiring with one of her vampire lieutenants, Lord Gabren Arlov. Lord Arlov has been sharing court secrets and knowledge from the City Below to fuel Raevo's defenses in return for the province bending the knee to Lord Arlov once he becomes a member of the Crimson Court. The party needs to identify the traitor and convince Raevo of the deceit.

entries.name: Expanded Domain
entries: The Grand Duke instructs your party to escort an arcane weapon discovered in the City Below to the front lines of the war against the Bürach Empire. The party learns that the weapon consumes the souls of vampires and mortals alike within range, fueling the machine and expanding the domain of the Darkfall. If news of this weapon reaches the other members of the Court, the Grand Duke's position will be threatened.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: As rulers of Ostoya, the Crimson Court can bestow many rewards to those who serve them. Beyond rewards of wealth, useful agents can gain lands and holdings of their own within the nation, complete with their own serfs, taxation benefits, and responsibilities protecting its peoples from enemies both undead and living.
The Crimson Court possesses many ancient artifacts from the City Below, including items steeped in necromancy and sangromancy.

Those who have proved their loyalty to the Court may be invited to receive the {@charoption Vampire|GrimHollowPG24|Vampire Transformation}, becoming a vampire noble themselves. Ancient texts from the City Below may also contain the secrets of the {@charoption Specter|GrimHollowPG24}, {@charoption Lich|GrimHollowPG24}, or {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24} Transformations.

entries.title: {@i Hire the knight if you want him to die bravely. Hire me if you want the job done.}

items: Guilds of monster hunters first came to be when hunters with shared methods gathered for support and to share knowledge. It is from this tradition that hunters still use "guild" to refer to the organization they belong to and their hunting methodology. The Carver Guild was once an organization of only carvers. Today, with members spread far and wide, carvers are present in many guilds. For example, the Landhaus Hawks once had at least four carvers on their roster, but the guild was founded by Hendretta Voland, an occultist. What unifies all monster hunters is the practice of studying their quarry's nature and approaching the hunt with knowledge, even when it is applied through force.

The often undocumented history of monster hunting makes the origins of its varied disciplines hard to pinpoint. The oldest recorded tale of a carver defeating a monster by learning of its weaknesses comes from the Valikan Saga of Ostra Cragstalker. The trappers trace their beginnings to two separate accounts—one originates with the dwarves of Stehlenwald using an ingenious contraption to contain a massive troll, while the other tells of Charneault knights who were forced into trapping when their sworn spirit demanded that they forcefully relocate rather than kill their enemies.

Mage-hunting occultists are often associated with Castinella given the region's tradition of arcane persecution. However, due to their own arcane dabbling, the relationship with the Arcane Inquisition remains volatile and unpredictable.

Despite the many horrific folk tales born of disgust and persecution, the devourers have no widely believed origin. While their teachings refer to the wisdom of past devourers, there isn't one who is considered the first. Because of the thematic connection with vampirism, devourers are suspected to have come from Ostoya. However, they aren't any more prominent there than in other areas of Etharis, and substantial evidence suggests they predate the Darkfall.

Today, smaller method-specific guilds still exist, and the methodologies remain distinct enough that carvers or trappers likely have more in common with each other than with other monster hunters of the area. This is partly because most hunters train through apprenticeship, with an apprentice learning from an experienced monster hunter.
It should be noted that because their members are often shunned by others, devourer guilds—when they exist—usually consist entirely of devourers. These guilds are very private.

Larger guilds now rival the smaller mercenary companies. However, unlike mercenary companies, there are very few monster hunter guild halls. Some of the bigger guilds do have their hall or lodge in a city, but most use a castle or other distant and well-secured location. Many monster hunters and smaller guilds travel extensively and have no permanent home. All monster hunters are trained to work individually, accepting some individual commissions, even when part of a guild. They often join adventuring groups or mercenary outfits to complete specific hunts.

entries.name: Goal: Kill Monsters, Get Paid
entries: Of all the factions of Etharis, the Monster Hunter Guilds probably have the simplest goals. While not entirely unified in purpose—and certainly not in method—most want to ply their trade and to be paid for completed contracts. Some guilds are more mercenary and won't slay anything without coin, while others prioritize the altruistic protection of innocents.

entries.name: Goal: Knowledge Is Our Weapon
entries: All monster hunters seek to use knowledge to defeat their quarry. Many Monster Hunter Guilds take an academic pride in locating, preserving, and sharing the knowledge needed to defeat monsters. This information not only helps hunters defeat their foes but also to fight superstition and misinformation among commoners, something that hunters are encouraged to do at every opportunity.

entries.name: Agents
entries: Most monster hunters are independent and rarely follow a rigid organization. Their numbers are also small, allowing the more accomplished hunters to be known by name. Many guilds have leaders chosen by a council, with infighting for leadership roles being extremely rare.

One notable leader is Weaver Faloenea, a Dreamer who leads The Nightshield, arguably Altenheim's most prominent monster hunting guild. Faloenea is a powerful occultist whose divination powers are renowned. It is said that she can reach into a monster's dreams to learn their true nature. Many have beseeched Faloenea to use her powers to learn the true nature of the Great Beast, but she has refused each time.

entries.name: Monster Hunter Template
entries: You can create NPC Monster Hunter by taking features from the {@i Grim Hollow Player’s Guide} and combining them with an existing humanoid statblock.

Add the following to an existing statblock to give it a monster hunter flavor. Where a {@variantrule Difficulty Class|XPHB|DC} is required, use a {@variantrule Difficulty Class|XPHB|DC} of 10 plus the creature’s {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Applying this template increases the monster’s {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB|CR} by 1.

entries.name: Studied Response
entries: {@actTrigger} a creature the Monster Hunter can see within 60 feet makes a Melee or Ranged Attack. {@actResponse} Before the Attack Roll, the Monster Hunter makes one Attack with a Weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature. If the attack misses, the Monster Hunter regains the use of its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entries.name: Terrorize the Terrors (Carvers)
entries: When the Monster Hunters hits a creature with an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, it can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the Monster Hunter’s next turn.

entries.name: Gnawing Hunger (Devourers) (3/day)
entries: When the Monster Hunter deals damage to a creature with a Melee Attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage dealt.

entries.name: Arcane Interference (Occultists)
entries: The Monster Hunter has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} against spells. Additionally, when a creature the Monster Hunter can see within 60 feet casts a spell or makes a Spell Attack, the Monster Hunter can use Studied Response before the spell is cast.

entries.name: Scorpion Anchor (Trappers) (3/day)
entries: When the Monster Hunter hits a creature with a Ranged Weapon Attack, it can deploy the Scorpion Anchor. If it does, the creature gains the {@condition {@condition Restrained|XPHB}|XPHB} condition. A creature {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the anchor can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against the Monster Hunter’s save {@variantrule Difficulty Class|XPHB|DC}. If it succeeds, it is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The characters are likely to run into Monster Hunters when seeking information, competing for or collaborating on a hunt, or rarely as a stalking enemy.

entries.name: Apprentice Rescue
entries: Attillah Kregg, a retired monster well hunter living in a small village, asks the characters to find Raffus Thorn his experienced apprentice. Raffus did not return from his most recent hunt. Given the monster he believes Raffus was hunting, Atillah is hopefull his apprentice is still alive, but likely not for long. The old man offers to reward the characters with a magic weapon from his hunting days if they return with Raffus.

entries.name: Hunted
entries: The party finds themselves targets of a monster hunting guild, because one of them has a transformation or was working with a monstrous ally, such as a Warlock patron or the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x}. If it comes to violence, the monster hunters have done their homework and are able to neutralize some of the party's greatest strengths.

entries.name: Noble Commission
entries: Wealthy Bürachar Duchess Katrina Vishovska offers a massive bounty to whoever brings her the heads of a monster pack terrorizing one of the villages in her domain. Among those who seek to claim the bounty are a monster hunting guild. The party can either try to beat the hunters to the prize or work together and split the reward.

entries.name: Guild Misdirection
entries: The characters are hired to investigate a dramatic assassination. The investigation leads to the local monster hunter guild, who claim that they are also seeking the murderer and offer to assist. In truth, the guild has begun taking humanoid contracts and was behind the murder. The guild points them to investigate a remote location, where an ambush awaits.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: A hunting guild might reward monster hunter characters with advanced equipment and resources such as Trapper Tools or Devourer Decoctions. They may also know how to construct Magic Items useful against specific monsters, such as a magic sword or an item that tracks the creature. Guilds often have salvage and item crafting recipes, and may trade for salvage.
Monster hunters value knowledge, with some guilds maintaining a library. A monster hunter character might be able to add a new monster type to their grimoire after completing tasks for the guild.

Most guilds tend to be private, but when there is a true threat, or someone has earned their respect, they may provide a safe haven to rest or craft. A party may also found their own guild, or a branch of an established guild.
Hunting guilds are sometimes too busy or too small to fulfill every contract, and may pass these on to adventurers. Most guilds aren't wealthy enough to put up bounties themselves.
He's an odious, self-serving, mercenary, but he gets the job done.

entries.title: {@i No knowledge should be feared, because knowledge is power.}

items: The Morbus Doctore appeared shortly after the release of {@area the Weeping Pox|448|x} in {@book Liesech|GrimHollowCG24|8}, cataloguing its symptoms and trying to treat the infected with a combination of alchemy and medical science. Sadly, they were rarely successful, but rumors abound that sometimes, they might be the only chance the infirm have.

The Morbus Doctore are most often found treating the infected of Liesech, though they do travel to other major cities. They are easy to spot with their boots and gloves stained with dried blood, strange coats and satchels hung with vials, and frightful masks meant to protect them from disease.

The medical and scientific knowledge held by the Morbus Doctore is staggering. While caring for those afflicted with the Weeping Pox and other illnesses, its physicians use these opportunities to examine the living and the dead. Their blend of arcane magic and science has given rise to unusual, but sometimes successful, treatments of dubious morality.

The arrival of a Morbus Doctore is both a blessing and a curse. Their presence means disease is present, with misery not far behind, whether from illness or treatment. No breakthrough comes without suffering, and a physician must become inured to patients' anguish. Some who've seen the Doctore work claim that they revel in every death. They torture their "patients." They perform cruel experiments with no regard for their charges: grafting skin, extracting marrow, and injecting tinctures of exotic origin. Then, they observe with fascination, scribbling notes in the name of science. The most insidious rumor suggests they were even responsible for Weeping Pox's initial spread.

Members regularly share insights with each other, so no valuable knowledge is lost or hoarded, but they do not share their research outside of their cabal. Information flows along through the faction's pyramid organizational structure, where each level of agents accepts reports from those below and forwards them to those above, preventing members from identifying any but those directly above or below them. At the top of the faction sit the Trustees.

The five Trustees of the Morbus Doctore are shrouded in mystery. Unknown to the public, no Doctore has seen beneath their plague masks or knows their identities. Stories say that when the Weeping Pox came to Liesech, the Trustees were the first to treat the sick. The horrors they witnessed and failed to prevent created a manic obsession to understand the disease that they have since spread to others.

In those rare moments when the Trustees must speak, it is the Attending who does so. From the emotionless, rasping voice that echoes within his mask, it is believed the Attending is male and probably elderly. The Attending delivers pronouncements with the cold logic expected of the rest of the faction. {@i "This subject must die so others might live. No knowledge is evil. The experiments must continue."}

entries.name: Goal: Mend the Broken
entries: The Morbus Doctore are a sinister faction with questionable methods. Publicly, they claim they only seek to cure illness and injury, but they often appear more interested in experimentation and witnessing the effects of a disease. With so much death in their vicinity, accusations of evildoing are inevitable, and may be well founded. After all, discovery through experimentation is the basis of learning. In the search for knowledge, it is easy to lose sight of the humanoid cost of that knowledge. Uncovering a new cure cannot be accomplished without understanding the limits of a patient's strength and the full dangers of diseases like the Weeping Pox.

The Morbus Doctore want to understand the basis of health and life. Some join the faction after witnessing the ravages of the Weeping Pox with a genuine desire to help, while others seek knowledge at any cost. The faction's leadership leans towards the latter group. A future of perfect health where no one gets sick, where life can be made endless, where anything biological can be repaired, modified, and controlled...this is the true goal of the Morbus Doctore, even if not all of its members know it.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Morbus Doctore have an insular, hierarchical structure with its members knowing only one or two members above them and only a handful below them. Members use secret titles for their ranks that are not used with non-members.

At the base of the faction are Adjuncts and Orderlies. While most Morbus Doctore hide their identities behind plague masks, the faction relies on these less mysterious members to interact with the public. Internally called Adjuncts, local doctors and nurses interact with a skittish public, boosting trust in the faction. In return, local physicians get the benefit of the the Morbus Doctore's medical expertise. Hired muscle, apothecaries, chemists, or others who don't work directly with the public are called Orderlies. These members complete the many necessary tasks that the doctors cannot be bothered with, or risk doing.

The average faction member is called a Resident. They are apprenticed to a more experienced Fellow, who cultivates a regional network of Adjuncts and Orderlies that interact with the public, gather information, run errands, and serve as muscle when needed. A typical Resident is {@b {@creature Plague Doctor|GHMG}} whose statblock can be found in the {@i Monster Grimoire}. Alternatively, a {@creature Noble|XMM} augmented with {@item Potions of Healing|XDMG|Healing Potions} or a {@creature Priest|XMM} armed with arcane magic can be used.

Underneath the Trustees are the Fellows. Each Fellow generally has one or two apprentice Residents. Fellows pursue their own ends, nominally in the service of the faction, but with wide discretion as to their methods and goals. Their approaches frequently filter down through the organization they supervise, coloring the public's opinion of the Morbus Doctore in a neighborhood, city, or region. A typical Fellow is a {@b {@creature Master Plague Doctor|GHMG}} (also found in {@i The Monster Grimoire} ), or a {@creature Mage|XMM} who can cast some healing spells.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: Adventurers, whether Orderlies or not, are needed to take risks members are not equipped to handle. Characters might be tasked with defending doctors from angry mobs or acquiring rare ingredients for treatments from distant locales.

With so much grief surrounding them, accusations of evildoing are inevitable—and sometimes well-founded. Not every member is without a moral center, but individuals often overstep their moral and ethical bounds more frequently than they should. While it's unlikely the Trustees would ever question the research practices of its members, the characters might be called upon by those outside the faction to investigate the demise of an otherwise healthy patient or the spread of disease wherever a Morbus Doctore goes.

entries.name: Dangerous Patient
entries: The Morbus Doctore seek to rescue an escaped patient named Yannick, who should be in quarantine. Yannick is hiding in the city. If found, he claims the Doctore infected him and resists efforts to return him. He is clearly still contagious, and his current hiding spot endangers infecting nearby innocents.

entries.name: New Treatment
entries: The Morbus Doctore offers an experimental treatment to an afflicted party member, such as a cure to {@area the Weeping Pox|448|x}. The treatment works, but also inflicts strange side effects such as mutations, a craving for sentient flesh, a drug dependency, or even an Aberrant Horror transformation.

entries.name: Change of Heart
entries: Teodo Harlindt is a halfling resident who has begun to question his role as a member of the Morbus Doctore. He sincerely wants to help others and knows that not all treatments are pleasant, but he questions the methods of his fellow Doctore. He wants out of the organization, hoping to take his library of medical knowledge with him, but someone has discovered his intentions. He needs help to make it out alive.

entries.name: Medical Caravan
entries: The Morbus Doctore seeks help transporting one of their caravans across the country. Strange illnesses occur in every village visited by the caravan. Carolina DiBello, a nurse in the caravan, is a survivor of several diseases but was supposedly cured. The cure is not entirely effective, and while she is no longer sick, she has become a carrier, spreading sickness everywhere she goes. Worse, the Doctore know this, and are using the opportunity to run tests on both Carolina and the ones she infects.

entries.name: Village Outbreak
entries: An entire village that was supposedly inoculated against disease has been infected by the Weeping Pox. Despite their best efforts, the Morbus Doctore seem unable to stop the outbreak. The physicians seek help quarantining the village while they work, but the horrifying truth soon becomes clear. A clandestine branch of the Morbus Doctore is spreading the disease to test the efficacy of their new inoculation versus a placebo.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: The resources of the Morbus Doctore are centered around health, so it is no surprise that their members have access to care, such as it is. Members can usually be certain of aid for injuries, Poisons, and diseases, though there is always a chance that any aid includes some "necessary experimental treatment" that often has significant side effects, for better or worse. This care is always in the form of tinctures, medicines, and Potions rather than divine magic.

Rumors say the most accomplished of the Morbus Doctore can cure death itself using concoctions distilled from lycanthropes, oozes, plants, or undead. The revived however always come back with a {@book Transformation|GrimHollowPG24|6}, or have their Species changed to {@race Arisen|GrimHollowPG24} as part of the process.

Members of the Doctore are provided Potions and experimental decoctions that increase the effectiveness of allied adventurers. In addition, the faction has significant stores of knowledge on diseases, herbs, Poisons, and the creatures that use them. Hunters of such creatures might trade fresh samples of such creatures for knowledge about them.
Being extremely secretive and with no central base, the Morbus Doctore cannot provide safe havens beyond the occasional hospital ward.

entries.title: {@i The scourge of undeath will disintegrate before the cleansing light of the dawn.}

items: Ostoyans who refused the rule of the {@area Crimson Court|05c|x} fled to the northern province of Raevo. There, they settled in a fort outside of Castalore and formed the Order of Dawn, a militia committed to the destruction of the undead and the liberation of Ostoya.

The Order of Dawn adheres to Ostoyan traditions with roots long before the Darkfall, revering the Arch Seraph {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, Lady of Vengeance. As such, the Order's hatred of the undead is fueled by pious anger, giving no quarter to their enemies. Members are trained from childhood to hunt and destroy the undead. Captured undead are studied and dissected so that their weaknesses can be committed to memory and whispered as prayers before and after rest. Every waking hour is devoted to the pursuit and end of the Crimson Court and all undead that roam Ostoya.

The Order is led by General Artemios Dulka, a Soman noble who fled the vampire curse and is obsessed with destroying the Crimson Court. Dulka is driven by the dream of an Ostoya without the Darkfall, without undead, and without the Crimson Court. The mere sight of a vampire within his vicinity triggers this typically benevolent leader into a vengeance-filled fervor.

Similar to their leader, the Order possesses admirable intentions but achieves them through methodical, merciless, and zealous means. Graves are unearthed and corpses burned to prevent undead rising. The Order has a high tolerance for collateral damage as long as the target is destroyed in the process. As such, Ostoyans outside of Raevo have mixed opinions on the group, regarding their presence as trouble for not just undead, but also for those serving them, no matter how begrudgingly. There is little room for compromise among the Order.

While primarily based in Raevo, the Order has repurposed a few abandoned forts and wrecked structures on the fringes of Ostoya into strongholds. The Order particularly favors the rehabilitation of ruined churches, regarding the restoration as symbolic to their mission. From these strongholds, the Order systematically cleanses the nearby area of undead influence, ensuring that every village is clear before pushing their boundaries further out. The process requires unending vigilance.

Safe houses deep within Crimson Court controlled provinces provide refuge for their spies and assassins while also providing a platform to recruit new members. These safehouses shift frequently as a form of pragmatism mixed with paranoia. As enemies of the Crimson Court, the Order of Dawn is no stranger to deceit and betrayal.

Despite their size, the Order of Dawn retains an air of superiority and self-righteousness when dealing with others. Even Raevan mages, who welcomed the first members of the Order into their lands, are considered faithless for turning to the arcane arts for protection rather than the traditional teachings of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. However, once one joins the ranks of the Order, they can expect steadfast loyalty and comradery.

entries.name: Goal: Cleanse the Undead
entries: Undeath is an insult to the living. The Order of Dawn exists to eradicate undeath in all its expressions, foremost the Crimson Court. Members of the Order of Dawn know better than most that just a speck of the undead curse is all that is needed for its evil to spread tenfold. As such, the Order of Dawn spares no quarter for those afflicted with undeath; never resting until every ghoul, zombie, specter, or vampire is utterly destroyed.

Unlike monster hunter guilds who work for coin, the Order of Dawn is driven by higher ideals, believing themselves to be the searing eyes and burning sword of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} herself. The Order is no stranger to the dark. They are willing to take extreme measures if it means cleansing Ostoya of undead. However, those who embrace such measures are carefully observed for concern that those who relish destruction and lose sight of the light will one day succumb to the darkness. The Order understands and takes pride in the precarious position they hold. They demand unflinching loyalty and a fervent hatred for the undead. Every member remains ever vigilant and ready to battle the undead.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Order of Dawn maintains a militaristic structure where piety and obedience are ingrained into its members from an early age. After basic training, members are assigned to one of three divisions, each committed to a different approach to cleansing Ostoya of undead. Each division is represented by a symbol related to {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} which is tattooed onto the skin of its members. The mark contains blessed inks that prevent the member from ever being resurrected.

Few in number, members are either organized into small strike teams to carry out precise mission objectives or embedded into Raevan battalions to bolster larger armies that anticipate confrontation with undead.

Sentinels are {@creature Knight|XMM|Knights} trained for direct confrontation against undead forces. They have a vast knowledge of all weakness points of every type of undead and train in squadron formations and battlefield tactics to best counter their enemies. The symbol of the sentinels is a glowing {@item Greatsword|XPHB}.

Stalkers are {@creature Spy|XMM|Spies} trained to seek out undead, using the unending darkness against their enemies. For clandestine operations involving high-profile targets, elite stalkers bestowed with the blessing of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} are sent to deal with the target immediately. Elite stalkers use the {@creature Assassin|XMM} stat block, dealing radiant damage instead of poison damage on relevant attacks. Stalkers are marked with four rays of light in the shape of a dagger.

Cleansers are {@creature Priest|XMM|Priests} trained to deal with the incorporeal, equipped with banishing and protection spells. Within their fortifications, Cleansers provide spiritual guidance to the Order. While on missions, Cleansers are responsible for purifying corruption. Cleansers are marked with a golden pattern around their lips, symbolizing the vow of silence of their Seraph.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The Order is slow to accept help from those outside their organization. However, when their pragmatism outweighs their piety, they will hire the assistance of mercenaries or monster hunters. This occurs most often when dealing with particularly dangerous foes, when the Order is outnumbered, or when they wish to keep their involvement hidden. Even then, the Order is slow to divulge any information considered non-essential, concerned that if an outsider can be enticed with gold, their loyalties may also be bought by their enemies. Any reverence of {@deity Solyma|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. respect for the traditions of Ostoya, or proven deeds against the undead are sure to soften dealings with the Order.

entries.name: No Mercy for Undeath
entries: The Order of Dawn has been pursuing a minor vampire noble, driving the noble to seek protection in a small village and charming the inhabitants. Rather than risk the vampire escaping, General Dulka has instructed the Order to burn the entire village down without warning the locals. This is despite the vampire's attempts at bargaining for her life.

entries.name: To the East
entries: An injured Raevan mage on the road comes to the party, begging for aid. A nearby Order of Dawn fortress that was once a mining outpost in the Grey Spine is under siege from a Soman army. The party must break the siege or those inside will perish. The mage suggests making use of abandoned tunnels in the mountains that lead to the fortress as a means of reaching the Order.

entries.name: Cursed Existence
entries: The Order have captured a dhampir progeny of Grand Duke Koshevek. Friends of the prisoner plea for General Dulka's mercy, insisting that the dhampir is innocent of any wrongdoing. The Order is fervent in dealing with those marked by the sanguine curse, and Dulka will likely command the execution of the dhampir. That is unless he can be convinced otherwise, or the prisoner somehow escapes.

entries.name: Dawn in Darkness
entries: The party has been hired by the Order to assassinate one of the members of the Crimson Court. Upon reaching one of the Order's safehouses in the province, the party realizes that their mission is simply a diversion for the Order to conduct their true assassination attempt.

entries.name: Push back the Darkness
entries: The party are hired by one of the strongholds to cleanse a village and nearby woods of undeath. However, the village is protected by a benevolent ghost claiming to be one of General Dulka's slaughtered family members and possesses crucial information regarding Crimson Court activity.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: While the Order of Dawn will reward hired mercenaries with {@item Gold|XDMG|Gold}, they prefer to offer equipment and Magic Items best suited to fight the undead in the hopes of enticing outsiders to join their cause. The most powerful artifacts are reserved for the most loyal members of the Order.

Cleansers stationed at Order of Dawn strongholds may offer their services to lift curses or cast greater restoration. As death is a natural part of life, Order members refuse to cast resurrection spells and will likely throw a party out if such a request is made.

Thanks to their vast spy network, the Order can provide information on the whereabouts of prominent vampires or offer safehouses in Crimson Court territory for recovery. The Order is willing to share any weaknesses of undead should the party prove themselves as agents of the Order's cause.

entries.title: {@i Only blood and sacrifice can keep the Prismatic Wyrm slumbering, sparing the world from its apocalyptic wrath}.

items: Most folk of Etharis view Valika as a barbaric land built on blood and malice. A good deal of this reputation is owed to the Prismatic Circle, who stoke a hunger for blood and violence among the clans of Thrull. The Circle embraces this fear, ensuring the {@book Valikan Clans|GrimHollowCG24|7} over which they hold sway remain dedicated to violence, reaving, and sacrifice in the name of ancient prophecy.

The Prismatic Circle is an ancient religious order that existed long before the founding of Etharis's modern nations. It is not a faith devoted to a divine ideal or godly salvation, instead, it is driven by the fear of what will happen should they fail to appease a sleeping Primordial's thirst for bloodshed.

The Circle was founded by the elemental druid Kybard, whose long-ago visions decreed that the Great Prismatic Wyrm {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} had not been slain by the Seven Heroes of legend. Instead, the Prismatic Serpent still slept beneath earth and sea, and its reawakening would herald the end of the world.

Kybard preached that the north must embrace the old ways of battle, fury, and sacrifice lest {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} be roused by the lack of blood. Since then, the Circle's Great Druids have driven the clans of Thrull to pursue their traditions of raiding and violence. This is the core tenet of the Circle—only through the constant supply of bloody sacrifices can the end of the world be forestalled

As part of this tradition, the druids make regular pilgrimages to the Sentinel Woods. Dozens of these widely scattered oak groves rise from the endless landscape of rock, grass, and lichen, where the Circle's dark rites allow them to survive. Here, the Circle's druids and squads of allied Völgr warriors drive captured livestock and hosts of people for sacrifice. The condemned consist of criminals, captives stolen from southern lands, and those who've crossed the Grand Druid. Sometimes, when the ranks are thin, unfortunate folk of lesser clans are added to this march to doom.

Over several excruciating days, the druids oversee the slaughter of animals and people. Many victims are painfully flensed, with the druids using their primal magic to enhance the suffering. Once the last victim falls, the druids and warriors quickly depart, for none may linger in any Sentinel Wood between times of sacrifice. The dead are left to rot or be scavenged by animals until nothing is left but scattered bones.

The frequency of these horrid rites has increased since the scourge of coldfire reappeared. The Circle saw its return as a sign that the Great Prismatic Wyrm was beginning to wake. Only a renewed focus on the rituals of violence and blood could stop the foretold return.

The clans of Kandar in Eastern Valika oppose the Circle. This has fostered conflict between the western and eastern clans, which has escalated from necessary and acceptable raiding between clans to outright warfare. Outright opposition to the Circle's control of the Thrull clans is rare for fear of being doomed to die in the Sentinel Woods as retribution.

Nonetheless, some groups do openly oppose the order. The Cult of the Great Wyrm, led by the druid Grenhildr, who was once the protégé of the current Great Druid. A near-death experience on the winter tundra changed her views. Instead of fearing {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}'s return, she seeks it, believing the primordial will prevent the destruction of clan culture across the North. Her heretical views have brought a death sentence, but thanks to strange magic, she escaped the Order and defeated many sent against her.

Guhjdäl the Revenant was a druid from the eastern tribes who was captured by Völgr raiders and marked for sacrifice. As a Lightbringer, she sought to preserve all life, and she believed that by doing so, they could save the world. Incensed by her beliefs, the Circle's druids were exceptionally cruel when they killed her. Days later, Guhjdäl awoke, returned to life with renewed power strong enough to cast {@spell Resurrection|XPHB} spells. In the years since, she has raised many who were sacrificed by the Circle, clandestinely supported by small clans who supply the gems needed for her powerful magic. Many of those saved join the growing ranks of her Revenant followers, seeking to ultimately build an army strong enough to break the Prismatic Circle's power in Thrull.

entries.name: Goal: Keep the Wyrm Slumbering
entries: The druids of the Prismatic Circle earnestly believe that only continued war and bloodshed can keep the world safe from the reawakening of {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}, the Prismatic Wyrm that birthed the Primordials. Only through the cycles of raiding and ritual murder can the all-powerful primordial be appeased to keep slumbering beneath the earth. Failure to do so will rouse the Great Wyrm's fury, which, if unleashed, would mean the end of the world.

entries.name: Agents
entries: The Prismatic Circle is led by the Grand Druid Hjadana. She is the spiritual leader of the three clans of Thrull in Western Valika, effectively making the region a theocracy under her control.

Though the Circle's spiritual direction strongly influences Clan decisions, it has never held true political power. Their might lies in the fealty of the clans—and on the fervor Hjadana has stirred in the warriors of Clan Völgr, who serve as the Circle's military might. {@i The Raider's Guide to Valika} includes stat blocks for {@b Raider Veteran}, {@b Raider Lieutenant}, and {@b Raider Captain}; otherwise, {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers} and {@creature Warrior Infantry|XMM|Warriors} are good representations.

Hjadana has fostered this relationship by growing close to Chief Nolgr Magnusson. The clan's seat lies at Tyburn on the isle of Holgar. It is here where Kybard first declared that {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24} still slumbered, and it is here where the Prismatic Circle's sanctum is built. A living fortress of dense trees rises defiantly from the rocky ground. It is within where the druids of the Circle engage in their secret rites.

Formally, the Prismatic Circle is a communal organization, where the Grand Druid would consult with members for guidance on doctrine and interpret signs of {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}'s return. However, Hjadana has ruled without challenge since her ascendance two decades ago. No one dares to openly criticize her rule—and those who do so privately understand that they put their lives in peril.

{@i The Raider's Guide to Valika}, offers stat blocks for the druids of the Circle, including the {@b Prismatic Circle Druid}, {@b Prismatic Circle Shaman}, and {@b Prismatic Circle Spy}. Alternatively, its members can be represented using standard {@creature Druid|XMM|Druids}, {@creature Cultist|XMM|Cultists}, or {@creature Spy|XMM|Spies}.

entries.name: Quest Hooks
entries: The Prismatic Circle has a tight control over most of what occurs in Valika's Thrull region. They have agents in most larger villages and a network of fervent druids across the north who monitor goings on. The Circle is interested in protecting Thrull from all threats and are happy to use others to complete dangerous tasks. They keep watch for anything that could challenge their control of the Clans.

entries.name: Rescue Captives
entries: Several villagers are captured by Valikan raiders and taken north. While searching for the villagers, the characters must contend with Prismatic Circle druids who are preparing to take the captives to one of the Sentinel Woods to be sacrificed.

entries.name: Primordial Artifact
entries: The characters are approached by Heimir, a druid from Valika. He tells the party the story of an ancient Primordial artifact hidden in the frozen wastes of Valika. If they return the artifact to him, he promises they'll be rewarded by the Circle. Heimir is actually allied with the Circle of the Great Wyrm and intends to use the artifact to invoke a powerful primordial to devastate a small village that is loyal to the Prismatic Circle.

entries.name: Village Defense
entries: While visiting a Kandar village, the characters help defend against a Thrull raid, with druids of the Prismatic Circle among the attacking forces. Afterwards, the party learns that the druids were looking to assassinate an important villager who was secretly a warlock of {@deity Gormadraug|Etharis|GrimHollowCG24}. The warlock is no longer in the village, having fled into the wilderness during the battle.

entries.name: Coldfire Threat
entries: A remote village is threatened by coldfire. A nearby Prismatic Circle druid has a stash of ice-oil, a rare substance that is highly effective in combating coldfire. The druid is willing to part with his supply, but only if the characters provide him bring him a villager who spoke against the Circle.

entries.name: Faction Rewards
entries: The Prismatic Circle rewards those who aid them with druidic, primal, or even sangromancy-themed Magic Items. The party might receive enchanted tablets, bone, or wood that function as {@item Spell Scrolls|XDMG}, inscribed with prayers to conjure primordial energy or to summon elementals.

The Circle may offer light healing services in the villages where they have agents. They might also offer to cast other spells, such as {@spell Augury|XPHB}, {@spell Divination|XPHB}, or other {@filter Druid Spells|spells|source=aag;ai;bmt;egw;ftd;ggr;idrotf;phb;sato;scc;tce;xge;xphb;rmr;scag;eepc;erlw;vgm;mot;frhof;efa;lox;toa;tftyp-dit;pota;oota;cos;skt;tftyp-tfof;wdh;wdmm;slw;cm;scc-tmm;dsotdq;kftgv;qftis;bgdia;crcotn;rot;tor;wbtw;us;tofw;lrdt;hotdq;dc;tftyp-toh;ditlcot;tftyp-tsc;tftyp-thsot;gos;sdw;hol;scc-ck;scc-hfmt;veor;jttrc;oow;dip;tftyp-atg;pabtso;tftyp-wpm;scc-arir;dosi;efr;fs;kkw;rmbre;lmop;vnotee;dd;drde-bd;drde-twoo;drde-tdon;fraif;drde-tfv;drde-bts;drde-sd;hotb;ffotr;drde-dotsc;drde-acfas;drde-fwtvc;drde-das;helianasguidetomonsterhunting;grimhollowpg24|class=druid}.

Characters who receive education from the Prismatic Circle may receive the {@feat Magic Initiate|XPHB} feat. Spies for the Prismatic Circle may also be able to provide information about Valika and some northern regions of Etharis.

data.name: Part 3: Divinity & Magic
entries: The gods of Etharis were present in the dim past of that world's mythology and history. Even after most of the gods perished, four deities manifested and presented gifts to the mortal emperor of the {@book Bürach Empire|GrimHollowCG24|3}. When they too perished and many of their lesser servants fell from the heavens, many wept that the end of everything was at hand. Yet the sun continued to rise on Etharis.

Now, as the trusted magic of the gods gives way to arcane magic associated more with witches, fey, and the Aether Kindred, superstition and prejudice run deep. In most towns and villages across Etharis, magic is seen as both wondrous and frightening—with terror generally winning the day. Whether understood or not, the arcane is mistrusted and often blamed for problems ranging from the failure of a season's crops to the success of a rival's business. Many innocent people have been caught on the wrong end of superstition.

The fear of magic runs deeper in some parts of Etharis than others. In the southern region of {@book Castinella|GrimHollowCG24|6}, this fear has manifested into an organized crusade against the arcane: the Arcanist Inquisition. The inquisitors are dedicated to destroying the arcane and anyone who stands to defend it.

Not all the citizens of Etharis despise spellcasters. Many cities house great colleges dedicated to the art of magic. Within these cities' walls, spellcasters can apply for a permit to practice. These permits dictate where a mage can cast spells, what type of spells they can cast, and other specifics. The process to apply for such a permit is often frighteningly bureaucratic, and many spellcasters choose to hide their abilities instead.

Magic items are seen as an extension of the mistrusted magic of arcanists and daemons. An object infused with magic is just assumed to be cursed—its beneficial properties simply a cover for the horrifying and crippling corruption that must be infesting the object's core. Even the four Divine Artifacts that were a symbol of the emperor's power have disappeared and assumed corrupted by the magic of evil provenance.

In Etharis, "{@book magic|GrimHollowCG24|15}" is a catch-all term for a power outside mortal ability, a means of altering reality altogether. Much ink has been spilled trying to understand the function of magic. Nobody has ever adequately defined it. Magic is, by its very nature, elusive and unnatural: magical.

Magic is described as having three forms, each with its own mystical sources: arcane, divine, and nature. In truth, scholars are split on whether these distinctions are meaningful. To commoners, the differences are merely cosmetic, if they're able to distinguish at all.

Arcane magic produces effects often eerie and abject, while divine magic appears like miracles being worked through mortal hands. Nature magic weaves the primal forces of the world to conjure preternatural occurrences.

Each form of magic is treated differently across cultures. In the snowy northern reaches of {@book Valika|GrimHollowCG24|7}, or the southern forests of the {@book Charneault Kingdom|GrimHollowCG24|5}, primal magic is most commonly practiced. In the {@book Bürach|GrimHollowCG24|3} and {@book Ostoyan Empires|GrimHollowCG24|4}, colleges are dedicated to learning the secrets of the arcane. The {@book Castinellan Provinces|GrimHollowCG24|6} abhor magic altogether. Only those ordained by the clergy are permitted to wield divine spells, while practitioners of both primal and arcane magic are hunted for their sins.

entries.name: Arcane Magic in Etharis
entries: The archmage pours over a tome in his school. The itinerate wizard travels the world on a quest for ancient knowledge. The resolute sorcerer, haunted with power, turns the fire that burns inside them upon a snarling monster. Each of these rely on a latent force that exists throughout Etharis. Commoners call it magic. Those who can wield it call it the arcane. Despite the innumerable books written on the subject, the source of this power is not well understood.

The greatest authority in Etharis on arcane magic was a man named Raakan el-Adakar. An archmage who lived during the early Age of Expansion, Raakan built an impressive library on the nature and source of magic. Much of the modern literature concerning the arcane is rooted in Raakan's research.

He determined that arcane magic emanates from a dimension parallel to the {@area Material Plane|341|x}, a place suffused with energy that can alter reality. He named this plane "{@area Numena|34c|x}" and the energy itself "numen." Magic, according to Raakan, involves using numen to give physical shape and form to the magician's will.

Commoners with the right gifts or knowledge can touch, or according to Raakan, "speak to" the realm Numena to elicit a response.
